About Us

BLINIQ is a cosmetic surgery centre that was established by Dr Ashwani Kumar as the result of his dream project. BLINIQ aims to help you look according to your desires with the help of the latest and cutting-edge advances made in the field of cosmetic surgery. We aim to bring the latest technologies used in cosmetic surgery worldwide under a single roof in India. 

The cosmetic surgery field is still at a nascent stage in India, and thus, only a few people are able to take benefit from this completely innovative and safe field of medical science. Thus, we have tried to provide all cosmetic services at an affordable price so that our services are accessible to people of all classes. We have made amazing body transformations possible, which you would have never thought of before. We eagerly await to see you soon and help you achieve the look you always desired.

Dr Ashwani Kumar is a renowned cosmetic surgeon and is currently practising in New Delhi. He started his medical education at the University College of Medical Sciences, New Delhi, where he completed his bachelor's (M.B.B.S) in 2007 and M.S. in general surgery. During his postgraduate days, he gained immense interest in cosmetic surgery, and thus he decided to shift his base. post_title: Arm Lift Surgery India / Brachioplasty Cost India
post_content:
Arm lift surgery in India, medically known as brachioplasty, costs between Rs. 80,000 and Rs. 2 lakh, depending on the extent of skin and fat to be removed, the surgical technique used, the surgeon's experience, and the facility. Brachioplasty addresses the excess, loose skin that hangs from the upper arm between the elbow and the armpit, a concern commonly referred to as bat wings. This skin laxity typically develops after significant weight loss, as a natural result of ageing and reduced skin elasticity, or following bariatric surgery, where the body loses volume faster than the skin can contract. In 2026, brachioplasty is one of the most searched body contouring procedures among adults aged 35 to 55 in India, particularly women who feel self-conscious about their arms in sleeveless clothing.

What Is Brachioplasty and What Does It Address?

Brachioplasty is a surgical procedure that removes excess skin and fat from the inner upper arm, reshaping and tightening the arm contour from the armpit to just above the elbow. It is distinct from liposuction of the arm, which removes fat but does not address skin laxity. In patients with significant loose skin, liposuction alone will not resolve the problem and can worsen the appearance by leaving deflated, hanging skin with less fat beneath it. Brachioplasty removes the excess skin and, where necessary, the underlying fat, and re-drapes the remaining skin to create a smoother, firmer arm profile.

What Causes Loose Upper Arm Skin?

The most common causes are significant weight loss (whether through diet, exercise, or bariatric surgery), natural ageing and loss of skin collagen, and, in some cases, genetics that predispose certain individuals to skin laxity in this area regardless of body weight. Exercise can strengthen and build the underlying triceps muscle, but cannot tighten significantly loose skin.

What Does Brachioplasty Cost in India?

The cost of arm lift surgery in India ranges from Rs. 80,000 to Rs. 2 lakh. A limited brachioplasty, where the incision is confined to the armpit area and is suited to patients with mild to moderate laxity, sits at the lower end of the range. An extended brachioplasty, which addresses laxity running from the armpit down toward the elbow and sometimes continuing onto the lateral chest wall, requires a longer incision and more surgical time and sits at the higher end. At Bliniq, the cost breakdown includes the surgeon's fee, anaesthesia, operating theatre charges, compression garments, and standard follow-up visits.

How Is Arm Lift Surgery Performed?

Brachioplasty is performed under general anaesthesia. The surgeon makes an incision along the inner upper arm, the length and placement of which depend on the degree of correction required. Excess skin and fat are removed, the remaining tissue is re-draped and sutured to create a smooth, tighter arm profile, and the incision is closed in layers with dissolvable internal sutures and skin-level closures. The resulting scar runs along the inner arm, where it is largely hidden when the arm rests at the side of the body.

What Is Recovery Like After Brachioplasty?

Recovery involves swelling, bruising, and tightness in the upper arms for the first one to two weeks. A compression garment for the arms is worn for four to six weeks. Most patients can return to desk-based work within seven to ten days. Lifting, strenuous exercise, and anything that places tension across the arm incision are restricted for six weeks. Scar management with silicone gel or sheets begins once the incision is fully healed and continues for several months to help the scar mature and fade.

Patients who are planning Laser Hair Removal in the underarm or upper arm area should plan this procedure either well before their brachioplasty or at least six months after, since laser treatment cannot be performed on skin that is healing from surgery.

Are You a Good Candidate for Arm Lift Surgery?

You are a good candidate if you have loose or sagging upper arm skin that does not respond to exercise, are at or near a stable weight, are in good general health, and have realistic expectations of improvement with the understanding that a scar along the inner arm is an expected and permanent part of the outcome. Patients who are still losing weight significantly are advised to wait until their weight has stabilised for at least six months, since further weight loss after surgery can create new skin laxity.

Patients who are considering brachioplasty alongside skin-quality improvement in the treated areas may find it useful to discuss Mesotherapy as a complementary treatment for improving skin tone and hydration during the months following surgery.

What Results Can You Expect?

The result of brachioplasty is a noticeably slimmer and smoother upper arm contour. Most patients describe feeling comfortable in sleeveless clothing for the first time in years. The scar along the inner arm fades over 12 to 18 months and, in most patients, becomes a pale, thin line that is largely unnoticeable in the natural arm position. The results are long-lasting when weight remains stable.

Takeaway: Brachioplasty trades one concern, excess hanging skin, for another that is smaller, a scar, and for most patients, that trade is well worth making.

Frequently Asked Questions

What does brachioplasty cost in India?

Arm lift surgery in India costs between Rs. 80,000 and Rs. 2 lakh. The exact cost at Bliniq is confirmed at consultation, depending on the degree of correction needed and the surgical approach planned.

Will there be a visible scar after arm lift surgery?

Yes. Brachioplasty involves an incision along the inner upper arm that results in a scar. This scar fades over 12 to 18 months and sits on the inner surface of the arm, where it is largely concealed when the arm rests naturally at the side.

How long does recovery take after brachioplasty?

Most patients return to light work within seven to ten days. Compression garments are worn for four to six weeks. Strenuous activity is restricted for six weeks. Swelling resolves progressively over two to three months.

Can liposuction replace brachioplasty?

Liposuction alone is suitable only for patients with good skin elasticity and primarily fat, rather than skin laxity. If there is significant loose skin, liposuction alone will not tighten it, and brachioplasty is the appropriate procedure.

Can arm lift surgery be combined with other procedures?

Yes. It is commonly combined with body contouring procedures such as a tummy tuck or thigh lift, particularly following major weight loss. Combined procedures are assessed at consultation for safety and feasibility.

Back Liposuction Cost in India: What You Pay, What You Get, and Why It Matters

Back fat is among the most stubborn fat deposits on the body. It does not respond proportionally to diet and exercise, it creates visible rolls under fitted clothing, and it frequently causes self-consciousness in both men and women. Back liposuction in India is a surgical body contouring procedure that permanently removes localised fat from the upper back, mid back, flanks, and bra roll area using a cannula inserted through small, discreet incisions. At Bliniq, back liposuction is performed by board-certified cosmetic surgeons using modern tumescent and VASER-assisted techniques. This page addresses the cost of back liposuction in India in full detail, along with everything you need to understand about what the procedure involves and how to make an informed decision.

Breaking Down the Back: Which Areas Are Treated?

The back is not a single zone for liposuction planning. It is divided into distinct regions, each of which may be treated separately or in combination:

Zone

Common Concern

Notes

Upper back

Bra roll, fat around bra straps

Common in women, often combined with arm liposuction

Mid back

Horizontal fat rolls above the waist

Affects the appearance of fitted tops and dresses

Lower back / Flanks

Love handles, muffin top

Very commonly requested, frequently combined with abdominal liposuction

Dorsocervical fat pad

Buffalo hump

Specialised treatment at the neck-shoulder junction

Each zone carries its own time requirement and complexity level, which directly affects the total procedure cost. Fat in the upper back and flanks tends to be more fibrous than abdominal fat and may benefit from VASER technology to improve fat release and skin retraction.

Back Liposuction Cost in India: A Transparent Breakdown

The cost of back liposuction in India typically ranges from Rs. 70,000 to Rs. 1,80,000, depending on the number of zones treated and the technique used. Below is a more granular view of how cost builds:

Single back zone (e.g., flanks only or bra roll only) Approximate range: Rs. 70,000 to Rs. 1,00,000

Two back zones combined (e.g., upper back and flanks) Approximate range: Rs. 90,000 to Rs. 1,40,000

Full back treatment (upper, mid, and lower back in one session) Approximate range: Rs. 1,20,000 to Rs. 1,80,000

When combined with abdominal liposuction (360-degree approach) Approximate range: Rs. 1,50,000 to Rs. 2,50,000

These are indicative figures. The final cost at Bliniq is provided after the surgeon assesses the volume of fat, skin elasticity, technique selection, and anaesthesia type required for your specific case.

What Drives the Cost Up or Down?

Technique Used

Standard tumescent liposuction is the most cost-effective option. Power-assisted liposuction (PAL) is moderately priced and handles denser, more fibrous fat effectively. VASER ultrasound-assisted liposuction is the premium option, costing more but offering superior skin retraction and reduced trauma, making it particularly well-suited for the fibrous fat commonly found in the back region.

Number of Areas Treated in One Session

Treating multiple back zones in a single session is more time-efficient and typically more economical than booking separate sessions. Your surgeon will advise what can safely be combined in one session, as India's medically recommended safe limit for fat removal in a single sitting is 3 to 5 litres.

Anesthesia Type

Small areas of the back can be treated under local anaesthesia with sedation. Larger or combined treatments may require general anaesthesia, which adds to the overall cost through anesthesiologist fees.

Surgeon's Qualifications

A board-certified cosmetic surgeon with specific experience in back contouring will charge more than a general practitioner performing the procedure. The back is a technically demanding area for liposuction because fibrous fat and skin retraction challenges make precision critical. This is not an area to compromise on surgeon quality.

Compression Garments and Post-Operative Care

These are often not included in the quoted procedure price. A back compression garment costs approximately Rs. 2,000 to Rs. 8,000 and must be worn for four to six weeks post-surgery. Factor this into your total budget.

The Procedure Itself: What Happens on the Day

Back liposuction is typically a daycare procedure. You arrive at the clinic, anaesthesia is administered, and the surgeon makes 2 to 4mm incisions in discreet locations. Tumescent fluid is infiltrated first to numb the area, reduce bleeding, and loosen the fat. A thin cannula is then used to break up and suction out the fat cells. The procedure takes 1 to 2 hours for a single back zone and up to 3 hours for a full back and flanks treatment.

You are discharged the same day, wearing a compression garment. Most patients return to desk-based work within 5 to 7 days. Strenuous upper-body activity is avoided for three to four weeks.

What Results Can You Expect?

Liposuction permanently removes fat cells from the treated zones. With stable body weight, the result is durable. Most patients notice:

  • A smoother, flatter upper back without bra roll bulging
  • A more defined waist when flanks and lower back are treated together
  • Better-fitting clothing, particularly shirts, dresses, and sarees
  • Improved confidence in fitted or backless outfits

Final results become visible three to six months after surgery as swelling resolves fully and the skin retracts around the new contour. Patients exploring full-body contouring alongside back liposuction can also find details about our gynecomastia treatment in Delhi for male chest contouring, which is frequently combined with back and flank treatment in one session.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is the fat removed by back liposuction permanent?

The fat cells removed do not regenerate. With stable weight, the contour improvement in the treated zone is long-lasting.

Can the skin on the back sag after liposuction?

Patients with good skin elasticity typically see the skin retract smoothly. Patients with significantly loose skin may benefit from VASER, which has a mild skin-tightening effect due to the heat generated. A physical assessment at consultation determines skin quality and expected skin response.

How painful is back liposuction recovery?

Most patients describe the recovery as tightness and mild soreness rather than sharp pain. Pain peaks in the first two to three days and is well managed with prescribed medication. The back compression garment should be worn consistently to reduce swelling.

Can back liposuction be combined with abdominal liposuction in one session?

Yes, and this is a commonly performed combination. A 360-degree approach that treats the full midsection, front and back, in one session is frequently recommended for patients wanting overall waist definition.

Is back liposuction suitable for men?

Absolutely. Men frequently seek back and flank liposuction alongside gynecomastia correction or as a standalone contouring procedure for a more athletic upper body silhouette.

BBL Surgery Cost in India: What to Expect in 2026

BBL surgery cost in India typically ranges from Rs.1.5 lakh to Rs.4 lakh, depending on how much fat needs to be transferred, the technique used, the experience of your surgeon, and the facility where the procedure takes place. BBL stands for Brazilian Butt Lift, a procedure that uses your own body fat, removed through liposuction, to add volume and shape to your buttocks. There are no foreign implants involved. It is one of the fastest-growing cosmetic procedures in the world, with over 400,000 BBL procedures performed globally in 2022 according to the International Society of Aesthetic Plastic Surgery (ISAPS). If you are considering this procedure in India, here is everything you need to know, from what it costs to what actually goes into that number.

What Is BBL Surgery? A Plain-Language Explanation

How Does the Procedure Work?

BBL surgery is a two-part procedure. First, fat is removed from areas of your body where you have excess, most commonly the abdomen, flanks, lower back, or thighs, using liposuction. That fat is then processed, purified, and carefully injected into the buttocks to add volume and improve contour.

Because the procedure uses your own living tissue rather than a synthetic implant, the results tend to look and feel natural. The body accepts the transferred fat as its own. Over time, roughly 60 to 80 per cent of the transferred fat survives long-term, which is why surgeons typically transfer slightly more than the final desired volume.

The entire procedure is performed under general anaesthesia and usually takes between two and four hours, depending on the volume being transferred.

How Is BBL Different from Butt Implants?

Butt implants use silicone prosthetics placed surgically inside or beneath the gluteal muscles. BBL, by contrast, uses only your own fat. This means no foreign material in your body, a lower risk of rejection or capsular contracture, and the added benefit of slimming the donor area through liposuction at the same time.

The trade-off is that BBL requires a sufficient amount of harvestable fat in your body. If you are very lean, your surgeon may advise implants instead, or ask you to gain some weight before surgery.

What Does BBL Surgery Cost in India? A Full Breakdown

What Is the Average Price Range?

Cost Tier

Price Range (INR)

What This Typically Covers

Entry

Rs.1.5L to Rs.2L

Smaller volume transfer, basic facility

Mid-range

Rs.2L to Rs.3L

Moderate transfer, experienced surgeon, better facility

Premium

Rs.3L to Rs.4L+

High-volume transfer, senior specialist, advanced technique

These figures reflect all-in costs at most credentialed Indian clinics, including surgeon fees, anaesthesia, OT charges, and standard post-operative care. Your final cost is confirmed only after an in-person assessment, since every patient's anatomy, and therefore the scope of work, is different.

What Factors Affect the Final Price of Your BBL?

Several things influence where your cost lands within that range:

  • Volume of fat transferred. A larger transfer requires more time in the operating theatre and more technical precision.
  • Liposuction technique. VASER (ultrasound-assisted) liposuction is more precise than traditional lipo and preserves fat cell viability better, which improves long-term results, but it costs more.
  • Number of donor areas. Fat harvested from two or three sites, such as the abdomen, flanks, and thighs, adds to the procedure time.
  • Surgeon's experience. A surgeon who has performed a high volume of fat grafting procedures brings a level of technique that directly affects safety and outcomes.
  • Facility and location. A procedure in a fully equipped surgical facility with ICU backup and trained anaesthesiology support will cost more than a basic setup, and for good reason.

How Does BBL Cost in Delhi Compare to Other Indian Cities?

Delhi sits at the higher end of the Indian pricing spectrum for cosmetic surgery, but that reflects access to more experienced surgeons and better-equipped facilities. Mumbai is comparable. Smaller cities like Jaipur or Lucknow may quote lower prices, but the availability of surgeons with specific BBL expertise is more limited. For a procedure this technically demanding, proximity to a highly experienced surgeon matters considerably.

Why Does BBL in India Cost Less Than in the US or UK?

In the United States, BBL surgery costs between $8,000 and $15,000 USD (approximately Rs. 6.6 lakh to Rs. 12.5 lakh at current exchange rates). In the UK, the range is similarly high. The difference in cost when coming to India is not a difference in the quality of the surgical technique. It reflects lower operating costs, smaller facility overheads, and the overall cost of living difference. How Is BBL Surgery Performed at Bliniq in Delhi?

What Technique Does Bliniq Use for BBL?

At Bliniq, the fat harvesting stage uses VASER liposuction, an ultrasound-assisted technique that gently emulsifies fat cells before removing them. This matters specifically for BBL because the integrity of the fat cells at the point of harvest directly affects how well they survive after being re-injected. Damaged fat cells do not survive the transfer. VASER is gentler on the tissue and therefore tends to yield higher fat survival rates compared to traditional liposuction.

The harvested fat is processed and purified before injection. The injection itself is performed in small, carefully controlled passes to ensure the fat is deposited in the correct anatomical layer, which is a critical safety factor for this procedure.

Patients who are planning additional cosmetic treatments, such as Laser Hair Removal in the donor or recipient areas, should discuss timing with their surgeon, as skin that has been operated on needs sufficient time to heal before any follow-on procedures are introduced.

What Is the Recovery Timeline After BBL?

Timeframe

What to Expect

Days 1 to 3

Swelling and bruising at both donor and recipient sites. Rest at home.

Days 4 to 14

Return to light activity. Avoid sitting directly on your buttocks.

Weeks 2 to 6

Use a BBL pillow when sitting. Wear compression garments as directed.

Swelling continues to resolve. Final shape becomes visible.

The most important recovery rule for BBL is avoiding sustained pressure on the buttocks for the first four to six weeks. Sitting compresses the newly transferred fat before it has established its own blood supply. Most patients use a BBL pillow, a cushion with a cut-out at the back, that allows them to sit on their thighs rather than their buttocks during this period.

What Safety Protocols Are in Place?

BBL has historically required careful attention to surgical technique, specifically ensuring that fat is not injected into the gluteal muscle, since intramuscular injection increases the risk of fat embolism. If you are very lean with little harvestable fat, your surgeon may recommend a different approach to buttock enhancement or discuss whether gaining some weight before surgery would be appropriate in your case.

Introduction About Liposuction Surgery in Delhi

Liposuction is an advanced cosmetic procedure that reshapes and contours your body by removing the extra stubborn fat from specific parts of your body. BLINIQ is a fully equipped cosmetic and plastic surgery center that has experienced and skilled surgeons to give you the most effective results of liposuction surgery in Delhi with no side effects or health complications.

What is Liposuction Surgery?

Liposuction is a surgical treatment procedure that breaks the fat deposition in your body and sucks it out. A hollow instrument known as a cannula is used to remove fat from your body. Liposuction surgery in Delhi is often considered a weight loss surgery, but it is not a weight loss surgery. It is done for people who have a stable weight and desire to remove stubborn fat deposits from specific parts of their bodies. The procedure is done for aesthetic purposes to enhance the contour of your body. The liposuction procedure removes the fat cells from your body to give you a contoured shape and a more contoured look. It is important to manage your lifestyle effectively after the liposuction surgery so that the treatment results are not reversed. The surgery does not remove all the fat from your body. Only a limited amount of fat can be removed from your body, depending on your body weight.

What are the types of Liposuction Surgery in Delhi?

Various liposuction techniques are currently available. These techniques include:
  • Tumescent liposuction
It is the most popular liposuction technique wherein saline solution with a vessel constrictor and local anaesthetic is injected under the skin of the targeted area, followed by the suction of fat through small tubes.
  • Ultrasound-assisted liposuction (UAL)
In this method, ultrasound waves are used to melt the fat by breaking the wall of the fat cells and liquifying it. This technique is often followed by suction-assisted liposuction to remove the liquified fat. The technique is more commonly used in fibrous areas (like back and male breasts) and in parts where liposuction has already been done.
  • Power-assisted liposuction (PAL)
This technique uses more power by using a specialized cannula that moves back and forth rapidly. This enables the surgeon to suck out more fat easily.
  • Laser-assisted lipolysis (LAL)
This technique uses a laser delivered into the fat present under your skin through a small incision to heat the fat and remove it from under your skin.

When is Liposuction Surgery Done?

Liposuction surgery is mostly done for cosmetic purposes. Liposuction gives very subtle results on the targeted areas of your body like buttocks, abdomen, back, hips, chest, inner knees, neckline, double chin, love handles, upper arms, and thighs. Apart from cosmetic purposes, liposuction can also be used to treat certain health conditions. The conditions most often treated by liposuction include:
  • Gynecomastia, wherein the fat accumulated in the breasts of a male is removed through liposuction.
  • Lipidema uses the liposuction technique to reduce the pain, discomfort, and swelling caused in your arms and legs due to oedema.
  • Liposuction with removal of extra skin is also done in cases where a person loses 40% of their weight by dieting and exercise or by undergoing Bariatric Surgery and end up with loose and saggy skin  .
  • Lipodystrophy syndrome is characterised by the accumulation of fat in one part of your body and fat loss in the other, giving you a very abnormal structure. In such cases, liposuction can help you get a more natural figure.
  • Liposuction is used for lipoma treatment to remove benign, fatty tumours from your body.
  • Axillary breast/accessory breast can be treated with liposuction without any big scars.

What Happens in Liposuction Surgery in Delhi?

Liposuction surgery in Delhi is divided into three stages: the preparatory phase, during the operation, and the post-surgery phase. The preparatory phase is important to ensure that you are physically fit before your surgery. Thus, a few health tests are recommended to you before the surgery. In short, the pre-operative or preparatory phase comprises:
  • Health tests and check-ups to ensure your overall well-being.
  • You must stop taking anti-inflammatory drugs or aspirin at least 2 weeks before the surgery.
  • Females should stop the use of oral contraceptive pills
  • If you are anaemic, you are advised to take iron supplements before the surgery.
  • Stop smoking 1 month before undergoing the procedure.
  • You will also be asked to sign a consent form as well.
During the operation Liposuction is a simple procedure that is performed under general anaesthesia. The effect of general anaesthesia may last for around 1-4 hours. The lower body treatment may require the use of an epidural which involves the injection of anaesthesia into the epidural space that surrounds the fluid-filled sac around your spine. Local anaesthesia can also be used if the procedure is being done in a small area. Post-operative phase The postoperative phase is very important in a liposuction procedure. You need to take utmost care during the postoperative phase to have better and long-lasting results. Some of the events that happen during the postoperative phase include:
  • You might feel anaesthetic after the procedure and be required to spend a night in the hospital if you are given general anaesthesia. In the case of local anaesthesia, liposuction is a daycare procedure, that is, you can leave the hospital on the same day itself.
  • The targeted area will be given an elasticated support corset or bandages after the treatment to reduce your swelling.
  • Antibiotics and painkillers are prescribed after the treatment to reduce pain and inflammation.
  • It is common for some bruising to occur after the treatment. This bruising gets cured on its own sometimes.
  • Numbness can also occur in the targeted area. This numbness gets treated in around 6-8 weeks.
  • A follow-up appointment is required after some days for the removal of the stitches.

What are the risks involved in Liposuction Surgery in Delhi?

Liposuction is generally a safe procedure. However, side effects of the treatment can appear if you get your treatment done by an inexperienced surgeon. At Bliniq, we have a team of experienced and skilled cosmetic surgeons who perform liposuction routinely with almost nil side effects. The minor risks or side effects involved in the procedure, if any are:
  • Severe bruising that can exist for some weeks after the treatment.
  • Swelling and oozing of the fluid from the incision can continue for next 6 months
  • Thrombophlebitis, that is, the formation of blood clots in the veins that can lead to inflammation and future complications.
  • You may have bumpy or wavy skin if your skin elasticity is poor, uneven fat removal has been done, or your wound heals in an unusual way.
  • You may feel temporary numbness in the affected area.
  • Liposuction surgery may cause skin infections as well.
  • Other very rare complications of liposuction surgery include puncturing of the internal organs, heart or kidney problems, pulmonary oedema or embolism, skin burns, and allergic reactions.

The Best Plastic Surgeon in Delhi: What That Title Actually Means and How to Find the Right One for You

Searching for the best plastic surgeon in Delhi is one of the most important decisions you will make before any cosmetic procedure. With hundreds of clinics and surgeons operating across Delhi NCR, the phrase "best" is used freely and often without substance. This page exists to help you understand what qualifications, experience, and clinical standards genuinely separate a safe, skilled cosmetic surgeon from an unqualified one, and what questions to ask before you commit to a procedure. At Bliniq, our surgeons meet the highest standards of formal training and surgical expertise in cosmetic and plastic surgery.

What Qualifications Should the Best Plastic Surgeon in Delhi Actually Have?

In India, the recognised and accepted qualifications for a surgeon performing cosmetic procedures on the body and face are:

MCh (Plastic Surgery) This is the gold standard. An MCh (Master of Chirurgiae) in Plastic Surgery is a three-year postgraduate surgical superspeciality degree obtained after completing an MS in General Surgery. It involves formal training in reconstructive, aesthetic, and microsurgical techniques. Surgeons holding this qualification are trained to operate on complex cases, including trauma reconstruction, burns, cancer reconstruction, and cosmetic procedures.

DNB (Plastic Surgery) A Diplomate of National Board qualification in Plastic Surgery is considered equivalent to MCh and is awarded by the National Board of Examinations. It indicates rigorous formal training through an accredited institution.

Fellowship Training in Aesthetic Surgery Some surgeons pursue additional fellowship training in specific areas such as facial aesthetic surgery, body contouring, or hair restoration after completing their MCh. This is a signal of advanced specialisation.

Professional Body Memberships Membership or fellowship in the Association of Plastic Surgeons of India (APSI) or the Indian Association of Aesthetic Plastic Surgeons (IAAPS) indicates engagement with the professional and ethical standards of the surgical community.

A red flag to watch for: surgeons advertising cosmetic procedures without an MCh or DNB in Plastic Surgery. An MBBS or MS in General Surgery alone does not qualify a surgeon to perform complex cosmetic procedures safely.

Five Questions to Ask Before Choosing a Plastic Surgeon in Delhi

Before booking any consultation or procedure, these five questions will help you assess whether a surgeon meets the standard you deserve:

  1. What is your formal surgical qualification, and where did you train? A credible surgeon will answer this directly and specifically. If the answer is vague or avoids mentioning an MCh or DNB in Plastic Surgery, that is a significant concern.
  2. How many of these specific procedures have you performed, and can you share before-and-after photographs of your own patients? Experience with the specific procedure matters. A surgeon who is skilled in rhinoplasty may not have equivalent experience in body contouring. Before-and-after photographs should represent the surgeon's own work, not stock images.
  3. Is the procedure performed in an accredited surgical facility? All procedures involving anaesthesia should be performed in a properly equipped surgical theatre with qualified anaesthesiology support. Procedures performed in basic clinic rooms rather than a surgical suite are a risk to your safety.
  4. What is the plan if a complication occurs? A transparent surgeon will explain possible complications for your specific procedure and describe the protocol for managing them. A surgeon who dismisses this question is not operating with appropriate caution.
  5. Are you available for follow-up consultations post-surgery? Post-operative follow-up is not optional. A surgeon who is unavailable or dismissive about ongoing care after your procedure is not offering the standard of support required for safe recovery.

#1. What is a breast lift?

A breast lift, also called mastopexy, is a surgical procedure performed by a plastic surgeon to tighten and raise the sagging breasts and thus improve their appearance. This helps to

    • Lift your breast higher on your chest.
    •  Make it face forward and reduce the size of your areola.
    • Make your breasts appear more rounded and symmetrical.
    • Tighten your breast tissue to support your nipples and areolas.
 #2. Why is a breast lift needed?
    • Breasts lift surgery is needed when your breasts lose their elasticity and firmness and do not snap back into place after being stretched. This can happen due to:
      • Aging: When skin becomes less elastic and firm.
      • Pregnancy: When the breast tissue bands supporting them, stretch because they get fuller and heavier during pregnancy. This causes sagging breasts, not depending on whether you breastfeed or not.
      • Weight changes:   This can cause the breast skin to stretch and may result in loss of elasticity.
      • Gravity: Over time, gravity causes the breasts' ligaments to stretch and sag.
      A breast lift can reduce sagging and raise the position of the nipple and the areolae (darker areas surrounding the nipples). 

#3. Who are ideal candidates for a breast lift?

You may consider a breast lift if:

    • You are overall healthy.
    • Are not breastfeeding and do not plan to do so.
    • You have sagging breasts, and the nipples fall below your creases.
    • Your areolae have stretched out of proportion to your breasts.
    • One of your breasts falls lower than the other.
    • You have realistic expectations from the surgery and are ready to follow instructions post-surgery.

    • Avoid certain medications: Some medicines like aspirin, anti-inflammatory drugs, or some herbal supplements can increase bleeding and, therefore, must be avoided.

INTRODUCTION
Breast reduction, also known as reduction mammaplasty, is a procedure to remove
excess breast fat, glandular tissue and skin to achieve a breast size more in proportion with
your body and to alleviate the discomfort associated with excessively
large breasts (macromastia).

INDICATIONS
Breast reduction is usually performed for enlarged breasts (macromastia), but may also be
indicated by: Cystic breast infections (polycystic mastitis) Back pain, neck pain, shoulder
pain, breast pain, or headaches. Loss of sensation in the breasts, arms, or fingers.


PROCEDURE
The specific technique used to reduce the size of your breasts can vary. The procedure might
include:

  1. Surgery through incisions
  2. Liposuction to remove the excess fat in your breasts

The surgeon usually:

  1. Makes an incision around the areola and down each breast
  2. Removes excess breast tissue, fat and skin to reduce the size of each breast
  3. Reshapes the breast and repositions the nipple and areola

The nipple and areola:

  1. Usually remain attached to the breast
  2. Might need to be removed and then reattached at a higher position as a skin graft if your
    breasts are very large

Your surgeon will try to achieve symmetry between your breasts, but some variation in breast size
and shape might occur. The size of the areola also might be reduced. Your incision scars might fade
over time but will never completely disappear.

After the procedure
Immediately after surgery:

  1. Your breasts will be covered with a gauze dressing or bandages
  2. A tube might be placed under each arm to drain any excess blood or fluid
  3. You likely will take medication for pain and antibiotics, to decrease your risk of infection

For the first days or week after surgery:

  1. Your breasts will probably feel tender and sensitive
  2. Your breasts might be swollen and bruised
  3. Your surgeon might recommend an elastic compression bra to protect the breasts

After that:

  1. You'll need to limit physical activity for two to four weeks while the breasts heal
  2. Your surgeon might suggest avoiding underwire bras for a few months after surgery

Scarring usually fades over time. You will need a follow-up visit with your surgeon to remove stitches and check your recovery.

Is Breast Reduction Right for You? Start Here.

Breast reduction surgery, medically called reduction mammoplasty, is one of the most physically transformative procedures in cosmetic surgery. Unlike many elective procedures that are purely aesthetic in motivation, breast reduction is often sought for deeply functional reasons: chronic back, neck, and shoulder pain, skin rashes under the breast fold, difficulty exercising, inability to find fitting clothing, and persistent postural problems caused by disproportionately large breasts.

At Bliniq, breast reduction surgery in Delhi is performed by qualified cosmetic surgeons who treat this procedure with the clinical weight it deserves. The goal is a breast size that is proportionate to the patient's frame, aesthetically natural, and free from the physical burden that drove the patient to seek help.

Who Is a Suitable Candidate?

You may be a candidate for breast reduction surgery if you experience any of the following:

  • Chronic pain in the back, neck, or shoulders that is directly attributed to breast weight
  • Deep grooves in your shoulders from bra straps bearing excessive load
  • Skin rashes, infections, or persistent chafing in the fold beneath the breasts
  • Difficulty participating in physical activity due to breast size
  • Breathing difficulty when lying flat
  • Emotional distress or self-consciousness related to disproportionately large breasts
  • Asymmetry between the two breasts that affects posture or comfort

Surgeons also consider whether the patient is at a stable body weight, in good general health, and a non-smoker or willing to stop smoking before and after surgery. Women who plan future pregnancies are generally advised to delay breast reduction, as pregnancy can affect breast tissue and alter the result.

Surgical Techniques Used at Bliniq

The technique selected for each patient depends on the volume of tissue to be removed, the degree of breast drooping (ptosis), and the desired final shape.

Liposuction-Only Breast Reduction

Best suited for patients requiring a slight to moderate reduction where breast skin has good elasticity. Fat is removed from the breasts using a cannula without excision of breast tissue. This approach produces virtually undetectable scarring and a faster recovery, but is not appropriate for patients with significant sagging or a large volume to be removed.

Vertical or Lollipop Breast Reduction

Two incisions are made: one around the areola and one vertically from the bottom of the areola to the breast fold. This technique is suited for moderate reduction and moderate sagging. It produces a smooth, rounded breast shape with less scarring than the anchor technique.

Inverted-T or Anchor Breast Reduction

Three incisions are used: around the areola, vertically down from the areola to the breast fold, and horizontally along the fold itself. This technique allows the greatest volume of tissue removal and is used for patients with large, significantly ptotic breasts. The resulting scar pattern is more extensive but fades over twelve to eighteen months. This remains the most reliable technique for substantial reduction.

In all techniques, the nipple and areola are repositioned to a higher, more natural position on the breast mound. Excess breast tissue, fat, and skin are removed, and the remaining tissue is reshaped to create a balanced, lifted breast contour.

Step-by-Step: What Happens on Surgery Day

Step 1: Anesthesia Breast reduction is performed under general anaesthesia. You will be fully asleep and feel nothing during the procedure.

Step 2: Incisions The surgeon makes the planned incisions based on the technique agreed upon during consultation. Incision placement is carefully mapped beforehand to optimise the final scar position.

Step 3: Tissue Removal and Reshaping Excess fat, glandular tissue, and skin are removed. The remaining breast tissue is reshaped and lifted. Liposuction may be used in combination with excision to remove residual fat around the axilla (armpit area) if required.

Step 4: Nipple Repositioning The nipple-areola complex is moved upward on the breast mound to reflect the new breast position. In most cases, the nipple remains attached to the underlying tissue and retains sensation.

Step 5: Closure Incisions are closed with layered sutures. Small drains may be placed to collect fluid in the first 24 to 48 hours. A surgical bra is applied immediately.

The procedure typically takes two to four hours, depending on volume and technique.

Recovery: What to Expect Week by Week

Week 1: Rest is essential. Drains (if placed) are removed within two to four days. Swelling, bruising, and soreness are significant but manageable with prescribed medication. You will wear a supportive surgical bra continuously.

Week 2: Most patients return to desk-based work. Driving is avoided for at least two weeks. Sleeping on the back remains necessary.

Weeks 3 to 4: Swelling reduces considerably. Light walking is encouraged. Heavy lifting and raising the arms above the head are avoided.

Weeks 5 to 6: Light exercise can be resumed with the surgeon's approval. Underwire bras are avoided for at least six weeks.

Months 3 to 12: The breasts settle into their final shape progressively. Scars begin to fade. Full maturation of the result takes up to twelve months.

  • The volume of tissue to be removed and the technique required

  • Whether any additional procedures, such as a breast lift or liposuction around the sides, are performed simultaneously

Frequently Asked Questions

Will breast reduction affect my ability to breastfeed?

It depends on the technique. The liposuction-only approach is least likely to affect breastfeeding. Incision-based techniques may affect milk duct function depending on how much glandular tissue is removed. This is discussed fully at the consultation.

Will insurance cover breast reduction surgery in India?

When breast reduction is performed for documented medical reasons, such as chronic spinal pain with supporting evidence, some insurance policies provide partial coverage. Purely cosmetic motivations are not covered. Your insurer should be consulted before surgery.

Are the results permanent?

The removed breast tissue does not regenerate. However, significant weight gain, pregnancy, or hormonal changes after surgery can affect breast size and shape over time.

How much breast tissue will be removed?

The volume removed is planned at consultation based on your frame size, the degree of reduction required, and what is safe to remove in a single procedure.

When can I wear a regular bra after surgery?

Most surgeons recommend returning to a soft, supportive bra without underwire at around six weeks, and a regular bra at three months once healing is confirmed.

What Is Buccal Fat Removal and What Does It Treat?

Buccal fat pads are naturally occurring deposits that sit beneath your cheekbones and above the jawline. In some people, these pads are larger than average, creating a rounder, fuller lower-face appearance regardless of overall body weight. Buccal fat removal reduces or removes these pads through a small incision made inside the cheek, meaning there are no external cuts and no visible scars on the face after healing.

Who Seeks Buccal Fat Removal in 2026?

Patients range from young professionals in their mid-twenties looking for a more contoured face to individuals in their thirties and forties who have noticed their facial structure changing with time. The procedure is also a component of facial feminisation, since a more defined midface and reduced lower cheek volume is a common aesthetic goal for patients undergoing gender-affirming care. Interest in this procedure has grown consistently each year as awareness of minimally invasive facial surgery increases in India.

Is It the Right Procedure for Every Patient?

Buccal fat removal is most effective for adults with a round or full lower face caused specifically by prominent buccal fat pads, rather than overall facial fat or skin laxity. It is not recommended for very lean individuals, since removing buccal fat from a naturally slim face can create a gaunt appearance as natural facial volume diminishes with age. Your surgeon will assess your facial anatomy carefully during an in-person consultation before recommending this procedure.

How Is the Procedure Performed at Bliniq?

Buccal fat removal is performed under local anaesthesia with sedation, meaning you are awake but comfortable and feel no pain during the procedure. The surgeon makes a small incision of approximately one to two centimetres inside the cheek, gently locates the buccal fat pad, and removes a measured portion of it. The incision is closed with dissolvable sutures that require no removal. The entire procedure takes between 30 and 60 minutes.

What Does Recovery Look Like?

Recovery is straightforward compared to more complex surgical procedures. Most patients experience mild swelling and discomfort for the first three to five days. Soft foods are recommended for the first week to avoid placing strain on the internal incision site. Most patients return to normal daily activity within five to seven days. The final result becomes visible at three to four months, once all swelling has resolved and the facial contour stabilises.

Patients who are planning skin rejuvenation treatments as part of their overall facial improvement should plan timing carefully. Those interested in Mesotherapy to improve skin texture and radiance in the facial area should discuss scheduling with their surgeon, as operated tissue needs adequate time to heal before additional treatments are introduced.

Are You a Good Candidate for Buccal Fat Removal?

You are likely a suitable candidate if you are an adult in good general health, have a fuller lower face caused by prominent buccal fat pads specifically, and hold realistic expectations of a subtle, refined result rather than a dramatic transformation. This procedure is not a substitute for weight management. If facial fullness is primarily related to overall body weight, your surgeon will recommend a different approach.

Buccal fat removal is also sought by transgender women and non-binary individuals as part of a broader facial feminisation plan. For patients exploring comprehensive gender-affirming surgical options, Bliniq also provides sex change surgery as part of its specialist care programme.

What Results Can You Expect from Buccal Fat Removal?

The result is a more sculpted, angular lower face with greater definition in the cheekbone region. The change is noticeable but measured. Most patients describe the outcome as their face looking like a refined version of itself rather than an entirely different appearance.

Results are permanent since the removed fat does not regenerate. However, your overall facial appearance will continue to evolve with age, and natural volume loss across the face over time is something your surgeon will factor into the recommendation of how much fat to remove. A conservative approach consistently produces more natural, age-appropriate outcomes over the long term.

Buffalo Hump Removal Surgery: Getting Rid of the Fat Pad Between Your Shoulders

A buffalo hump is a visible fat deposit at the base of the neck, just between the shoulder blades. Its medical name is a dorsocervical fat pad. At Bliniq, we treat this condition surgically using liposuction, which removes the fat cells permanently through small, discreet incisions. The procedure is performed under general or local anaesthesia with sedation and takes approximately 60 to 90 minutes. Most patients return home the same day. For people who have lived with this hump for years despite diet and exercise, surgery is the only treatment that produces a meaningful and lasting result.

What Is a Buffalo Hump and What Causes It?

A buffalo hump forms when excess fat accumulates in the dorsocervical region, the area at the upper back where the neck meets the shoulders. The result is a rounded or protruding mass that sits visibly beneath clothing and affects the natural slope of the upper back and neck.

The condition has several possible causes:

  • Excess body weight: Fat tends to accumulate in the dorsocervical area with obesity, particularly in people who carry weight in the upper body
  • Long-term corticosteroid use: Medications such as prednisone, when taken for extended periods, cause fat redistribution to the upper back and face. This is one of the most common medical causes
  • Cushing's syndrome: A condition caused by prolonged exposure to high cortisol levels, either from medication or the body producing excess cortisol. A dorsocervical fat pad is a classic clinical sign
  • HIV-associated lipodystrophy: Certain antiretroviral drugs used in HIV treatment are associated with fat redistribution to the upper back
  • Genetic fat distribution: Some individuals store fat preferentially in the upper back regardless of overall body weight or medication use
  • Ageing: Loss of lean muscle mass and changes in fat distribution with age can contribute to fat accumulation in this region

It is important to have a proper medical assessment before undergoing surgical removal, particularly to identify any underlying hormonal or medication-related cause. If the hump is caused by an active medical condition such as Cushing's syndrome, addressing that condition first is necessary.

When Is Surgery the Right Answer?

Surgery is considered appropriate when:

  • The fat deposit has been confirmed clinically as a dorsocervical fat pad (not a spinal curvature or bony prominence)
  • Underlying medical causes have been ruled out or managed
  • The hump does not reduce with weight loss or lifestyle changes
  • The patient is at a stable, healthy weight and has good skin elasticity
  • The patient has realistic expectations about the contouring outcome

Patients with very significant skin laxity over the hump area may need to discuss whether skin excision is also required, as liposuction addresses fat but does not tighten loose skin. Your surgeon at Bliniq will assess both at the consultation.

The Buffalo Hump Removal Procedure at Bliniq

Pre-procedure assessment: The surgeon examines the hump physically to confirm it is a soft fat pad rather than a bony or fibrous mass. Photographs and measurements are taken. Any relevant medical history, particularly medication use or hormonal conditions, is reviewed.

Anaesthesia: The procedure is typically performed under general anaesthesia or local anaesthesia with sedation, depending on the extent of the fat pad. Local anaesthesia is adequate for smaller, well-defined humps.

The removal technique: Tumescent fluid is infiltrated into the treatment area to numb it, reduce bleeding, and loosen the fat cells. Small incisions of 3 to 4mm are made in discreet positions. A thin cannula is inserted to break up and suction out the fat. VASER (ultrasound-assisted) liposuction may be used in cases where the fat is particularly dense or fibrous, as ultrasound energy helps emulsify the fat before extraction, reducing trauma to surrounding tissue and improving skin retraction.

The procedure typically takes 60 to 90 minutes. Patients are observed for a few hours after surgery before being discharged home on the same day.

Recovery After Buffalo Hump Liposuction

  • Days 1 to 5: Swelling, bruising, and tightness in the upper back and neck. A compression garment is worn over the treated area. Mild to moderate discomfort is managed with prescribed pain relief
  • Week 1 to 2: Desk-based work can typically be resumed. Physical activity involving the upper body, including lifting and exercise, is avoided
  • Weeks 3 to 6: The compression garment is worn particularly during the day. Progressive improvement in contour is visible as swelling resolves
  • Months 3 to 6: Full results become visible once all swelling has resolved. The dorsocervical region shows its final, improved contour at this point

Maintaining stable body weight after surgery is the most important factor in preserving the result long-term.

What Results Can You Expect?

Buffalo hump removal through liposuction produces:

  • A significantly flatter and more natural upper back profile
  • Reduced pressure and discomfort at the base of the neck
  • Improved posture in some patients where the weight of the hump was affecting how they held their neck
  • Better fitting clothing around collars, necklines, and upper back
  • Improved comfort during sleep and seated activities

Results are permanent as long as body weight remains stable. Significant weight gain after surgery can cause the remaining fat cells in adjacent areas to expand, though the specific dorsocervical region will retain its relative improvement.

Patients managing multiple body contouring concerns at the same time often ask about our broader liposuction and body sculpting services. If you are also considering scar-related skin concerns, our mesotherapy service supports post-procedure skin recovery and overall skin quality.

Cost of Buffalo Hump Removal Surgery in Delhi

Buffalo hump removal surgery in Delhi falls within the broader range of liposuction pricing. The cost is influenced by:

  • The size and density of the fat pad
  • Whether local or general anaesthesia is used
  • The liposuction technique employed (standard vs. VASER)
  • Whether the procedure is combined with any other body contouring treatment

A precise cost estimate is provided after clinical assessment at Bliniq. EMI financing options are available.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can a buffalo hump go away with exercise alone?

The dorsocervical fat pad does not respond to targeted exercise. While overall weight loss may reduce it slightly in some patients, it rarely disappears without surgical intervention in those who carry a defined hump.

Is the procedure painful?

Surgery is performed under anesthesia, so there is no pain during the procedure. Post-operative soreness in the upper back is expected for the first week and is managed with medication.

How long before I see the final result?

Swelling in the upper back can take three to six months to fully resolve. Improvement is visible progressively from week two onward, with final results at around the six-month mark.

Can the hump come back after surgery?

The fat cells removed do not regenerate. However, significant weight gain after surgery can cause remaining fat cells to expand. Maintaining a stable weight protects the result.

Are there non-surgical alternatives?

Fat-dissolving injections are sometimes used for small humps but produce limited results for established dorsocervical fat pads. Surgery remains the most effective and reliable option for meaningful correction.

INTRODUCTION

A chemical peel is a procedure in which a chemical solution is applied to the skin to remove the top layers. The skin that grows back is smoother. With a light or medium peel, you may need to undergo the procedure more than once to get the desired results.

Chemical peels are used to treat wrinkles, discolored skin and scars — usually on the face. They can be done alone or combined with other cosmetic procedures. And they can be done at different depths, from light to deep. Deeper chemical peels offer more-dramatic results but also take longer to recover from.

INDICATIONS

A chemical peel is a skin-resurfacing procedure. Depending on the issues you’re addressing with the procedure, you’ll choose a chemical peel in one of three depths:

  1. Light chemical peel. A light (superficial) chemical peel removes the outer layer of skin (epidermis). It’s used to treat fine wrinkles, acne, uneven skin tone and dryness. You might have a light peel every two to five weeks.
  2. Medium chemical peel. A medium chemical peel removes skin cells from the epidermis and from portions of the upper part of your middle layer of skin (dermis). It’s used to treat wrinkles, acne scars and uneven skin tone. You might need to repeat the procedure to achieve or maintain the desired result.
  3. Deep chemical peel. A deep chemical peel removes skin cells even deeper. Your doctor might recommend one for deeper wrinkles, scars or precancerous growths. You won’t need repeat procedures to get the full effect.

Risks

A chemical peel can cause various side effects, including:

  1. Redness, scabbing and swelling. Normal healing from a chemical peel involves redness of the treated skin. After a medium or deep chemical peel, redness might last for a few months.
  2. Scarring. Rarely, a chemical peel can cause scarring — typically on the lower part of the face. Antibiotics and steroid medications can be used to soften the appearance of these scars.
  3. Changes in skin color. A chemical peel can cause treated skin to become darker than normal (hyperpigmentation) or lighter than normal (hypopigmentation). Hyperpigmentation is more common after superficial peels, while hypopigmentation is more common after a deep peel. These problems are more common in people with skin of color and can sometimes be permanent.
  4. Infection. A chemical peel can lead to a bacterial, fungal or viral infection, such as a flare-up of the herpes virus — the virus that causes cold sores.
  5. Heart, kidney or liver damage. A deep chemical peel uses carbolic acid (phenol), which can damage heart muscle and cause the heart to beat irregularly. Phenol can also harm the kidneys and liver. To limit exposure to phenol, a deep chemical peel is done a portion at a time, in 10- to 20-minute intervals.

A chemical peel isn’t for everyone. Your doctor might caution against a chemical peel or certain types of chemical peels if you:

  1. Have taken the oral acne medication isotretinoin (Myorisan, Claravis, others) in the past six months
  2. Have a personal or family history of ridged areas caused by an overgrowth of scar tissue (keloids)
  3. Are pregnant
  4. Have frequent or severe outbreaks of cold sores

PROCEDURE

Choose a doctor with knowledge of the skin and procedure — a dermatologist or dermatologic surgeon. Results can be variable and depend on the expertise of the person doing the peel. Improperly done, a chemical peel can result in complications, including infection and permanent scars.

Before you have a chemical peel, your doctor will likely:

  1. Review your medical history. Be prepared to answer questions about current and past medical conditions and any medications you are taking or have taken recently, as well as any cosmetic procedures you’ve had.
  2. Do a physical exam. Your doctor will inspect your skin and the area to be treated to determine what type of peel you might benefit from most and how your physical features — for example, the tone and thickness of your skin — might affect your results.
  3. Discuss your expectations. Talk with your doctor about your motivations, expectations and potential risks. Make sure you understand how many treatments you might need, how long it’ll take to heal and what your results might be.

Before your peel, you might also need to:

  1. Take antiviral medication. Your doctor might prescribe an antiviral medication before and after treatment to help prevent a viral infection.
  2. Use a retinoid cream. Your doctor might recommend using a retinoid cream, such as tretinoin (Renova, Retin-A) for a few weeks before treatment to help with healing.
  3. Use a bleaching agent. Your doctor might recommend using a bleaching agent (hydroquinone), a retinoid cream, or both before or after the procedure to reduce the risk of side effects.
  4. Avoid unprotected sun exposure. Too much sun exposure before the procedure can cause permanent irregular pigmentation in treated areas. Discuss sun protection and acceptable sun exposure with your doctor.
  5. Avoid certain cosmetic treatments and certain types of hair removal. About a week before the peel, stop using hair removal techniques such as electrolysis or depilatories. Also, avoid hair dying treatments, permanent-wave or hair-straightening treatments, facial masks, or facial scrubs in the week before your peel. Don’t shave the areas that will be treated beginning 24 hours before your peel.
  6. Arrange for a ride home. If you’ll be sedated during the procedure, arrange for a ride home.

What you can expect

Before the procedure

A chemical peel is usually done at an office or in an outpatient surgical facility. Before the procedure, your doctor will clean your face, protect your hair, and cover your eyes with ointment, gauze, tape or goggles.

Pain relief isn’t typically needed for a light chemical peel. If you’re having a medium peel, you might receive a sedative and painkiller. For a deep peel, you might have a sedative, something to numb the treatment area and fluids delivered through a vein.

During the procedure

During a light chemical peel:

  1. Your doctor will use a brush, cotton ball, gauze or sponge to apply a chemical solution typically containing glycolic acid or salicylic acid. The treated skin will begin to whiten.
  2. You might feel mild stinging while the chemical solution is on your skin.
  3. Your doctor will apply a neutralizing solution or wash to remove the chemical solution from the treated skin.

During a medium chemical peel:

  1. Your doctor will use a cotton-tipped applicator or gauze to apply a chemical solution containing trichloroacetic acid, sometimes in combination with glycolic acid. The treated skin will begin to whiten.
  2. After a few minutes, your doctor will apply cool compresses to soothe treated skin. You might also be given a hand-held fan to cool your skin. No neutralizing solution is needed, however.
  3. You might feel stinging and burning for up to 20 minutes.

During a deep chemical peel:

  1. You’ll be given intravenous (IV) fluids, and your heart rate will be closely monitored.
  2. Your doctor will use a cotton-tipped applicator to apply carbolic acid (phenol) to your skin. Treated skin will begin to turn white or gray.
  3. To limit your exposure to phenol, your doctor will do the procedure in portions at about 15-minute intervals. A full-facial procedure might take about 90 minutes.

After the procedure

After a chemical peel of any depth, your skin will be red, tight, irritated or swollen. Follow your doctor’s directions for sun protection, cleansing, moisturizing and applying protective ointments to your skin. And avoid picking, rubbing or scratching your skin. It may take several months before your skin color returns to normal and you can see the full results of the peel.

After a light chemical peel, treated skin will be red, dry and mildly irritated — although these effects might be less noticeable with each repeat treatment. Your doctor might apply a protective ointment, such as petroleum jelly, to soothe the skin. You can usually wear makeup the next day if you wish.

Treated areas take about one to seven days to heal after a light chemical peel. New skin might temporarily be lighter or darker than normal.

After a medium chemical peel, treated skin will be red and swollen. You’ll feel stinging. Your doctor might apply a protective ointment, such as petroleum jelly, to soothe the area and prevent dryness. After five to seven days, you can use cosmetics to cover any redness.

Use ice packs for comfort. Over-the-counter pain-relieving medication, such as ibuprofen (Advil, Motrin IB, others) and naproxen sodium (Aleve, others), may help reduce any discomfort. You’ll likely schedule a checkup soon after your treatment so that your doctor can monitor your healing.

As swelling decreases, treated skin will begin to form a crust and might darken or develop brown blotches. Treated areas take about seven to 14 days to heal after a medium chemical peel, but redness might last for months.

After a deep chemical peel, you’ll experience severe redness and swelling. You’ll also feel burning and throbbing, and the swelling may even make your eyelids swell shut.

Your doctor will apply a surgical dressing to treated skin. He or she might also prescribe painkillers. You’ll need to soak the treated skin and apply ointment several times a day for about two weeks.

Treated areas will develop new skin within about two weeks after a deep chemical peel, although redness might last for months. Treated skin might become darker or lighter than normal or lose the ability to tan.

You might prefer to remain at home while you’re healing from a chemical peel. You’ll likely need several follow-up visits soon after your treatment so that your doctor can monitor your healing.

Once new skin completely covers the treated area in about two weeks, you can use cosmetics to conceal any redness. Use sunscreen every day.

Results

A light chemical peel improves skin texture and tone and lessens the appearance of fine wrinkles. The results are subtle but increase with repeated treatments. If you have a medium chemical peel, treated skin will be noticeably smoother. After a deep chemical peel, you’ll see a dramatic improvement in the look and feel of treated areas. Results may not be permanent. Over time, age and new sun damage can lead to new lines and skin color changes.

With all peels, the new skin is temporarily more sensitive to the sun. Talk with your doctor about how long to protect your skin from the sun.

Q1 What exactly does a chemical peel do?
Q2 A chemical peel is a technique used to improve the appearance of the skin on the face, neck or hands. A chemical solution is applied to the skin that causes it to exfoliate and eventually peel off. The new, regenerated skin is usually smoother and less wrinkled than the old skin.

A2 The results of chemical peels can last anywhere from one month to the rest of your life. The longevity of a chemical peel's results depends on several factors, but the most significant factor is the strength of the peel. A light, or superficial, peel produces results that usually last between one and two months.

Introduction

The goal of a chin augmentation is to enhance the contours of the chin, jawline, and neck, and thereby improve overall facial appearance. Chin implants are the most common type of chin augmentation. The placement of synthetic implants around the existing chin bone alters the underlying shape of the face, which enhances the balance of other facial features.

Chin augmentation can provide a permanent solution for a recessed chin during an outpatient procedure without using general anesthesia.

FAQ

 

About the Procedure

During the chin augmentation procedure, a board certified plastic surgeon will place an alloplastic chin implant underneath the skin around the patient’s existing chin bone. This outpatient surgery typically takes about one hour to perform, does not require the use of general anesthesia, and provides a permanent solution to weak, recessed chins. The goal of the procedure is to enhance the natural contours of the neck, jaw, and chin in order to improve facial appearance.

Chin implants are one of the two main types of chin augmentation. The other is called sliding genioplasty (osseous genioplasty) and does not involve the use of synthetic implants. Instead, the surgeon slides the lower jaw bone forward to produce more lower face and chin projection. Genioplasty is associated with higher risks for complications that chin implants are.

Chin augmentation implants can be placed either through small incisions made inside the mouth or under the chin. Dissolvable sutures are used to close the incisions after the implants are placed appropriately. The typical recovery time for this procedure is 1-3 weeks in most patients.

The goal of chin augmentation surgery is to restore facial harmony. Chin augmentation can be used to define an underdeveloped chin or give it more prominence. As the shape of the chin is affected by the shape of the nose, chin augmentation is often paired with rhinoplasty.

Recovery Notes

A chin strap dressing may be placed to keep pressure on the implant and to improve adherence of the soft tissue to the mandible. This is typically removed after 1-3 days. A soft diet may also be advised for 1-7 days. If an intraoral approach is used, peroxide rinses may be prescribed after each meal. Some discomfort, mild pain, swelling, redness and bruising, along with tenderness to touch is expected. Candidates can typically resume normal function within a week.

Ideal Candidate

Ideal candidates for chin augmentation are those whose facial features are fully developed and who are unhappy with the appearance of their chin and facial profile.

Not Recommended For

Chin augmentation is not recommended for patients with severe microgenia, labial incompetence, lip protrusion, shortened mandibular height, severe malocclusion, and periodontal disease.

FAQ

Q1 Are chin implants permanent?
A1 Once successfully placed, chin implants are permanent. You don't have to return every year to repeat the filler injection. Despite being permanent, chin implants also can be removed if you're not happy with it

Q2 Are chin implants safe?
A2 Risks of chin implant surgery
When performed by a qualified, experienced cosmetic surgeon, chin augmentation is associated with very few complications. Possible risks include infection, hematoma (internal bleeding), shift in implant position, poor scarring, and side effects from anesthesia.

Q3 Do chins need implants?
A3 Men often consider a chin implant surgery to obtain a stronger, more masculine jawline that is associated with success. Women often consider the procedure to balance the symmetry of their facial profile. Chin implants are highly durable and designed to last a lifetime.

Introduction

Circumcision is the surgical removal of the skin covering the tip of the penis. The procedure is fairly common for newborn boys in certain parts of the world, including the United States. Circumcision after the newborn period is possible, but it’s a more complex procedure.

For some families, circumcision is a religious ritual. The procedure can also be a matter of family tradition, personal hygiene or preventive health care. For others, however, circumcision seems unnecessary or disfiguring.

Why it’s done?

Circumcision is a religious or cultural ritual for many Jewish and Islamic families, as well as certain aboriginal tribes in Africa and Australia. Circumcision can also be a matter of family tradition, personal hygiene or preventive health care.

Sometimes there’s a medical need for circumcision, such as when the foreskin is too tight to be pulled back (retracted) over the glans. In other cases, particularly in parts of Africa, circumcision is recommended for older boys or men to reduce the risk of certain sexually transmitted infections.

The American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP) says the benefits of circumcision outweigh the risks. However, the AAP doesn’t recommend routine circumcision for all male newborns. The AAP leaves the circumcision decision up to parents — and supports use of anesthetics for infants who have the procedure.

Circumcision might have various health benefits, including:

  1. Easier hygiene. Circumcision makes it simpler to wash the penis. However, boys with uncircumcised penises can be taught to wash regularly beneath the foreskin.
  2. Decreased risk of urinary tract infections. The risk of urinary tract infections in males is low, but these infections are more common in uncircumcised males. Severe infections early in life can lead to kidney problems later.
  3. Decreased risk of sexually transmitted infections. Circumcised men might have a lower risk of certain sexually transmitted infections, including HIV. Still, safe sexual practices remain essential.
  4. Prevention of penile problems. Occasionally, the foreskin on an uncircumcised penis can be difficult or impossible to retract (phimosis). This can lead to inflammation of the foreskin or head of the penis.
  5. Decreased risk of penile cancer. Although cancer of the penis is rare, it’s less common in circumcised men. In addition, cervical cancer is less common in the female sexual partners of circumcised men.

The risks of not being circumcised, however, are not only rare, but avoidable with proper care of the penis.

Circumcision might not be an option if certain blood-clotting disorders are present. Also, circumcision might not be appropriate for premature babies who still require medical care in the hospital nursery or for babies born with abnormalities of the penis.

Circumcision doesn’t affect fertility, nor is circumcision generally thought to enhance or detract from sexual pleasure for men or their partners.

Risk

The most common complications associated with circumcision are bleeding and infection. Side effects related to anesthesia are possible as well.

Rarely, circumcision might result in foreskin problems. For example:

  1. The foreskin might be cut too short or too long
  2. The foreskin might fail to heal properly
  3. The remaining foreskin might reattach to the end of the penis, requiring minor surgical repair.

How you prepare

Before circumcision, the doctor will explain the risks and benefits of the procedure. Whether you’re planning to have your son circumcised or you’re pursuing circumcision for yourself, you’ll likely need to provide written consent for the procedure.

What you can expect

During the procedure

Newborn circumcision is often done in the hospital nursery, usually within 10 days after birth.

For newborn circumcision, your son will lie on his back with his arms and legs restrained. After the penis and surrounding area are cleansed, an anesthetic will be injected into the base of the penis or applied to the penis as a cream. A special clamp or plastic ring will be attached to the penis, and the foreskin will be removed.

Afterward, the penis will be covered with an ointment, such as a topical antibiotic or petroleum jelly, and wrapped loosely with gauze. The procedure generally takes about 10 minutes.

Circumcision is similar for older boys and adults. However, the procedure might need to be done under general anesthesia, recovery might take longer and the risk of complications might be greater when done later in life.

After the procedure

It usually takes seven to 10 days for the penis to heal. The tip of the penis is likely to be sore at first, and the penis might look red, swollen or bruised. You might notice a small amount of yellow fluid on the tip of the penis as well.

If your newborn is fussy as the anesthetic wears off, hold him gently — being careful to avoid putting pressure on the penis.

It’s OK to wash the penis as it heals. For newborns, change the bandage with each diaper change, and apply a dab of petroleum jelly to the tip of the penis to keep it from sticking to the diaper. Change your baby’s diaper often, and make sure the diaper is loosely fastened.

If there’s a plastic ring instead of a bandage, it will drop off on its own — usually within about a week. Once the penis heals, wash it with soap and water during normal bathing.

Problems after circumcision are uncommon. Contact the doctor if:

  1. Normal urination doesn’t resume within 12 hours of the circumcision
  2. There’s persistent bleeding
  3. There’s foul-smelling drainage from the tip of the penis
  4. The plastic ring remains in place two weeks after the circumcision

    FAQ
    Q1 What age is best for circumcision?
    A1 Most religious traditions recommend an early circumcision. From our experience, we have seen the Plastibell method work best when a child is circumcised between the ages of one and three months old. This is the age that has been most comfortable for mother and child both during the operation and in the recovery period.

    Q2 Can a grown man be circumcised?
    A2 Adult circumcision is often a simple procedure, though it's a larger surgery than it is in infants. People who choose to have it done may do so for many of the same reasons parents choose it for their newborns — medical, religious, or social

    Q3 Is a circumcision painful?
    A3 Circumcision can be done at any age. Traditionally, the most common time to do it is soon after your baby is born, or within the first month of life. Because the process is painful, a local anesthetic is used to numb the area and the surgery is performed while the baby is still awake.
