Mole Removal in Delhi: What It Costs, How It Works, and When to Act

Mole removal in Delhi is a quick, minimally invasive procedure performed by qualified cosmetic surgeons and dermatologists to eliminate unwanted or medically concerning skin growths. At Bliniq, we assess each mole individually before recommending a removal method. The cost of mole removal in Delhi typically ranges from Rs. 3,000 to Rs. 15,000 per mole, depending on size, depth, location, and the technique used. Most procedures are completed within 30 to 60 minutes as a daycare treatment, meaning you go home the same day. Whether your concern is cosmetic or clinical, the right first step is a professional assessment to determine the nature of your mole and the safest removal approach.

What Exactly Is a Mole and When Should It Be Removed?

A mole, medically called a nevus, forms when clusters of melanocytes (pigment-producing skin cells) group together instead of spreading evenly across the skin. Most moles are harmless and appear during childhood or early adulthood. However, certain moles require prompt medical attention.

You should consider mole removal if:

  • The mole has changed in size, shape, or colour over a short period
  • The border of the mole appears irregular or uneven
  • The mole bleeds, itches, or causes persistent discomfort
  • It is positioned where clothing or accessories rub against it repeatedly
  • You find it cosmetically bothersome and want it permanently gone

A sudden change in a mole's appearance is the most important signal to see a specialist. Dysplastic nevi, which have uneven pigmentation and irregular borders, carry a higher risk of developing into melanoma and should be evaluated without delay.

Types of Moles We Treat at Bliniq

Understanding your mole type guides the entire treatment decision.

Benign Common Moles: These are brown or pink, round, and have a defined border. They are not cancerous and are typically removed for cosmetic reasons. They respond well to laser and shave excision.

Congenital Nevi: Present from birth, these moles are more likely to change over time. About 1 in 100 people are born with them. Surgical excision is often recommended for larger congenital moles.

Dysplastic Nevi: These have uneven colouring, often darker at the centre and lighter toward the edges. They are frequently inherited and carry a measurable risk of becoming malignant. A biopsy may accompany removal.

Raised Moles: Elevated above the skin surface, these respond particularly well to laser and shave excision techniques with minimal scarring.

Flat Pigmented Moles: Dark, flat moles are best treated with targeted laser technology such as Q-switched Nd: YAG or fractional CO2 laser.

Mole Removal Procedures Available at Bliniq

Surgical Excision: The mole is cut out using a scalpel along the natural crease lines of the skin, and the wound is closed with fine sutures. This method is used for deeper moles or any mole that requires a biopsy to rule out malignancy. The incision heals into a thin, barely visible line within weeks.

Shave Excision: A flat blade removes the mole at skin level without requiring stitches. Best suited for raised, superficial moles. The procedure takes under a minute with local anaesthetic and leaves a small pink mark that typically fades within a few weeks.

Laser Removal: Class 4 medical-grade lasers, including Q-switched Nd: YAG and Er: YAG systems, are directed at the mole cells. The cells absorb the laser energy and are eliminated without affecting the surrounding skin. Laser removal is ideal for flat or lightly pigmented moles and for patients who prefer a non-cutting approach. Newer, shallower moles respond better than older, deeper ones.

Radiofrequency Removal: High-frequency energy precisely removes the mole tissue layer by layer. This is especially effective for small, raised moles and produces very minimal scarring.

Punch Excision: A circular blade removes small, deep moles cleanly. Particularly useful for moles under 5mm in diameter that sit deep within the dermis.

The right procedure is always selected after a physical examination. No two moles are identical, and no two patients have the same skin type, which is why a one-size-fits-all approach does not work here.

How Much Does Mole Removal Cost in Delhi?

Mole removal cost in Delhi varies depending on several factors:

  • Size and depth of the mole: Larger or deeper moles require more time and a more involved technique
  • Number of moles: Removing multiple moles in one session may be priced differently from a single removal
  • Method used: Laser removal tends to sit at the higher end compared to shave excision or cauterisation
  • Whether a biopsy is needed: Sending the removed tissue for histopathology adds Rs. 800 to Rs. 2,500 to the overall cost
  • Location on the body: Moles on the face, around the eyes, or near sensitive structures require greater precision and may cost more

At Bliniq, you receive a transparent cost estimate at your consultation. EMI options are also available for patients managing multiple moles or combining mole removal with other procedures. If you are exploring skin-boosting treatments alongside mole removal, our mesotherapy treatments can support overall skin quality and tone.

What to Expect Before, During and After Mole Removal

Before: Your surgeon examines the mole, photographs it, and determines whether removal is purely cosmetic or medically advisable. You may be asked to avoid blood-thinning medications in the days before the procedure.

During: Local anaesthetic is applied to the area. The mole is then removed using the chosen method. The entire process typically takes 30 to 60 minutes. Most patients feel no pain, only mild pressure.

After: The treated area will appear red or slightly raised for the first few days. A small dressing is applied post-procedure immediately. It is important to:

  • Keep the area clean and moisturised
  • Avoid sun exposure on the treated site for at least four to six weeks
  • Do not pick at any scab that forms, as this can cause scarring
  • Apply sunscreen daily once healing is underway

The mole removal site heals completely within two to four weeks for most techniques. Surgical excision sites take slightly longer, given the sutures involved.

Why the Method of Removal Matters

Choosing laser removal when surgical excision is clinically indicated, or choosing excision when laser would produce a cleaner cosmetic result, directly affects your outcome. At Bliniq, the surgeon's recommendation is based on a thorough clinical evaluation of your mole, not on what is simplest or fastest to perform.

Patients who have also considered hair-related cosmetic concerns during the same visit often enquire about our laser hair removal services, which use a similar approach of targeted energy to address unwanted skin conditions.

Frequently Asked Questions

Does mole removal hurt?

The area is numbed with local anaesthetic before any procedure begins. You may feel mild pressure or tugging, but no significant pain. Post-procedure soreness, if any, is manageable with standard medication.

Will mole removal leave a scar?

All procedures leave some trace, but skilled technique minimises this significantly. Laser and shave excision typically leave a faint pink mark that fades. Surgical excision leaves a thin line scar that becomes almost invisible over time.

Can a removed mole grow back?

If the mole was completely removed, including its root cells, recurrence is very unlikely. Incomplete removal, particularly with laser on deep moles, can sometimes result in partial regrowth requiring a follow-up session.

How many sessions will I need?

Most moles are removed in a single session. Laser treatment for older or pigmented moles may require two to three sessions spaced several weeks apart.

Is mole removal safe for darker skin tones?

Yes, but the technique must be matched to skin tone. Certain laser wavelengths carry a risk of post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation on darker skin. At Bliniq, skin type is assessed before any laser procedure is recommended.

Mommy makeover surgery in Delhi costs between Rs. 2 lakh and Rs. 6 lakh, depending on the combination of procedures chosen, the surgeon's experience, and the facility. A mommy makeover is not a single procedure. It is a customised combination of surgical treatments, most commonly a tummy tuck, breast lift or augmentation, and liposuction, designed to address the physical changes that pregnancy, breastfeeding, and postpartum weight fluctuation bring to a woman's body. At Bliniq in Delhi, this combination is tailored to each patient based on a thorough physical assessment, so the procedures performed reflect your actual concerns rather than a fixed package. For women who have spent years carrying the marks of motherhood, this is a meaningful decision that deserves honest, clear information.

What Is a Mommy Makeover and What Does It Include?

The term mommy makeover refers to a personalised group of procedures performed in a single surgical session or staged over two sessions, aimed at restoring or enhancing the body after childbirth and breastfeeding. There is no single fixed combination. The specific procedures recommended depend entirely on your concerns, anatomy, and health.

Common Procedures Included in a Mommy Makeover

The most frequently combined treatments include an abdominoplasty (tummy tuck) to tighten abdominal muscles and remove loose skin, liposuction to address stubborn fat deposits in the waist, flanks, or thighs, and a breast lift or breast augmentation to restore volume and shape lost after breastfeeding. Some patients also include treatment for diastasis recti, a condition in which the abdominal muscles separate during pregnancy and fail to fully rejoin afterwards. Each component addresses a different physical change, and combining them in one session reduces overall anaesthesia exposure and recovery time compared to staging each procedure separately over months.

Why 2026 Is a Practical Year to Consider This Surgery

Many women delay body restoration surgery for years while managing young children. By 2026, a growing number of patients in Delhi NCR are choosing to act on this decision as awareness of available options increases and waiting lists at credentialed clinics have become more manageable. If you have completed your family and have no plans for future pregnancies, this is the appropriate time to explore a mommy makeover, since subsequent pregnancies can affect surgical results significantly.

What Does Mommy Makeover Surgery Cost in Delhi?

The cost ranges from Rs. 2 lakh for a more limited combination to Rs. 6 lakh or more for a comprehensive plan involving multiple procedures. The primary cost drivers are the number of procedures combined, the duration of the operating session, the surgeon's experience, and the facility's level of care. Anaesthesia for a longer combined procedure is naturally more extensive than for a single short surgery. At Bliniq, you receive a written cost breakdown at consultation that details each component separately so you understand exactly what each element of your plan involves financially.

EMI financing options are available for patients who prefer to distribute the cost over a period of months, which is discussed in detail during your consultation.

How Are Mommy Makeover Procedures Performed?

A tummy tuck involves an incision along the lower abdomen, through which excess skin is removed, and the underlying abdominal muscles are sutured back together. Liposuction uses a small cannula to remove stubborn fat deposits through tiny incisions. A breast lift repositions and reshapes the breast tissue, with or without an implant, depending on the degree of volume loss. All of these are performed under general anaesthesia in a fully equipped surgical facility.

What Is Recovery Like After a Mommy Makeover?

Combined procedures mean a more involved recovery than any single surgery alone. Most patients require one to two weeks of rest at home before returning to light activity. Strenuous activity and lifting are restricted for six to eight weeks. Compression garments are worn as directed during the healing period. Full results become visible over three to six months as swelling resolves and the tissues settle.

Patients who are planning skin rejuvenation treatments in the weeks or months following their surgery may find that Mesotherapy supports skin quality improvement in areas treated by liposuction, helping to improve surface texture and tone as the body recovers. Timing of any additional treatments should always be confirmed with your surgeon.

For patients who are also considering hair removal in the treated areas during or after their recovery period, Laser Hair Removal is available at Bliniq and can be scheduled at an appropriate point in your recovery timeline.

Are You a Good Candidate for a Mommy Makeover?

You are a suitable candidate if you have completed your family and do not intend to have further pregnancies, are in good general health, are at or near a stable weight, and have realistic expectations of improvement rather than perfection. Patients who are still breastfeeding or who are within six months of delivery are generally advised to wait before proceeding. Weight stability is particularly important, since significant weight change after surgery affects both the abdominal and breast results.

A thorough pre-operative health assessment is conducted at Bliniq before any surgical plan is finalised.

What Results Can You Expect?

The goal of a mommy makeover is a body contour that more closely reflects how you felt before pregnancy, adjusted for your current anatomy and age. Most patients report a significant improvement in how their clothes fit, how they carry themselves, and how comfortable they feel in their own skin. Results are long-lasting when supported by a healthy lifestyle and stable weight.

Takeaway: The more specific you are about your concerns at consultation, the more precisely your surgical plan can be tailored to address what matters most to you.

What does a mommy makeover cost in Delhi?

Most patients need one to two weeks of rest before returning to light activity. Full activity restrictions last six to eight weeks. Final results are visible over three to six months as swelling resolves.

INTRODUCTION
Monsplasty is a type of cosmetic surgery where excess skin and fatty tissue are removed from the fatty pad over the pubic bone.
This fatty pad is called the mons pubis, which is how the procedure got its name. Another name for this procedure is a pubic lift.
The mons pubis may become enlarged from fat accumulation or because the overlying skin is loose. Monsplasty can help create a firmer, flatter appearance in this area.
Here’s a look at what monsplasty involves, what the recovery is like, and what it typically costs.

INDICATIONS

Monsplasty is the surgical procedure that’s used to remove excess skin and fatty tissue when a noticeable bulge forms in the upper pubic area due to weight gain or sagging skin.

A monsplasty is usually done at the same time as a tummy tuck (abdominoplasty) because a bulging mons pubis is often more noticeable after you’ve removed fatty tissue and tightened the skin on your abdomen.

Monsplasty and liposuction can be done together, but they’re different procedures. With monsplasty, excess fat and skin is cut away with a scalpel.

Liposuction uses suction to remove excess fat but it can’t change the appearance of or remove saggy skin. Laser liposuction can help tighten the skin in the area if it’s not too excessive.

What causes an enlarged mons pubis?

Your mons pubis is the triangular area that runs from the top of your pubic hair down to the place where your genitals begin. It’s more obvious in women, but men have one too.

When you gain weight, excess fat often accumulates in your mons pubis. This can cause it to enlarge and bulge out. Your skin has to stretch out to cover the extra fatty tissue as it accumulates. The more weight you gain, the more your skin stretches.

If you then lose a large amount of weight, you’re left with the same amount of skin but less fatty tissue that needs to be covered. Because the extra skin is loose, it can sag.

When you’re young and carry excess weight for only a short time, your skin has enough elasticity to bounce back to its original flat shape. However, the longer you have the extra fatty tissue and the older you are, the less elastic your skin becomes.

Without enough elasticity, the excess skin stays stretched and you’re left with loose skin.

Other reasons for a bulging mons pubis may be due to:

  1. C-section. After a C-section, the skin around the scar may remain tight against your tissues, but the skin below it doesn’t. As a result, it can form a bulge.
  2. Hormones. Estrogen can cause increased fat deposition in your mons pubis
  3. Genetics: You can inherit the tendency for increased fat deposition in your mons pubis from your parents.

Besides removing excess fat and skin for aesthetic purposes, or to reduce body image concerns, there are several other reasons why someone may decide that a monsplasty is right for them. For instance, an enlarged mons pubis may:

  1. droop over the vulva or penis, making it difficult to have sexual intercourse or urinate
  2. make sexual intercourse painful
  3. make it hard to maintain good hygiene

INDICATIONS

Before having monsplasty or other cosmetic surgery, you’ll talk with your surgeon to determine the best procedures for achieving the results you want.

It’s important have realistic expectations. Remember that the goal is improvement, not perfection, and the look you want may not be achievable.

Monsplasty works best if you’re at a healthy weight and in good overall health. You may want to consider delaying the procedure if you have weight to lose or have an active health condition.

There are several steps you’ll need to take in preparation for your surgery.

PROCEDURE

  1. Arrange for someone to drive you to and from your surgery, and for someone to be with you for at least 24 hours after your surgery.
  2. Avoid drinking alcohol, starting 2 days before surgery.
  3. Stop taking medication that increases your risk of bleeding such as aspirin, nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory (NSAID) drugs and all herbal remedies 10 days before surgery, or according to your doctor’s instructions.
  4. Avoid smoking 2 weeks before and after surgery.

Usually, monsplasty is performed at an outpatient same-day surgery center using sedation and local anesthesia.

An overnight hospital stay might be required when monsplasty is done in combination with other more extensive procedures, like a tummy tuck.

During the surgery

  1. For this procedure, you’re first sedated with intravenous medication.
  2. Next a local anesthetic is injected into your skin, and the tissues in your lower abdomen and mons pubis, to make it numb.
  3. In an area of your lower abdomen that can be covered by a bikini, a horizontal incision is then made in your skin.
  4. Excess fatty tissue and skin are removed using a scalpel.
  5. The underlying tissue and muscle may be pulled up and tightened using sutures, and your mons pubis is “sculpted”.
  6. The incision is then closed with sutures and adhesive strips.

What’s the recovery like?

Once the sedation has worn off, you’ll be monitored for a short while to make sure you don’t have any complications, and then you’ll be released. You’ll need to have someone drive you home after the procedure.

After surgery, you’ll usually have some discomfort in your lower abdomen and mons pubis. You’ll notice mild bruising and swelling soon after surgery that’ll typically last 2 to 3 weeks.

Recovery usually happens fairly quickly, and the recovery timeline will likely look something like this:

Recovery timeline

  1. A day or two after surgery, you’ll be encouraged to take slow-paced walks.
  2. One week after surgery, you can usually return to work. If your job involves heavy lifting or exertion, you’ll need more time to heal before going back to work.
  3. After about 3 weeks, your incision may be healed enough for you to soak in a bathtub.
  4. You can typically begin participating in exercise and other physical activities after 4 to 6 weeks.

You’ll notice the results of your monsplasty immediately after surgery. By 6 weeks you’ll see the final results. The changes are permanent but excess fat deposition can reoccur if you gain weight.

The benefits of monsplasty include:

  1. a firmer, smoother, and flatter mons pubis
  2. improved self-esteem
  3. easier, less-painful sexual intercourse, if these were problems before surgery

Possible complications from monsplasty

Monsplasty is a safe procedure with a low risk of complications. However, in some situations, the following complications are possible:

  1. infection of the incision
  2. excessive bleeding
  3. a scar that may be larger or more noticeable than expected

Are there other options besides monsplasty?

Monsplasty is the only surgical option for removing excess fat or saggy skin in your pubic area, but healthy eating, regular exercise, and other weight loss tips may also help reduce the amount of fat in your mons pubis.

Your mons pubis area is usually one of the last parts of your body to lose excess fat. Even so, eating a healthy, reduced-calorie diet that causes fat loss throughout your body can help reduce fat in this area.

It’s hard to specifically target your mons pubis with exercise, but some that do target the general area are:

  1. planks
  2. pelvic tilt
  3. bicycle crunch
  4. straight leg raise
  5. burpees
  6. the Hundred

Other nonsurgical therapies that may help get rid of excess fat or tighten your mons pubis include:

  1. laser skin tightening
  2. radiofrequency skin tightening
  3. CoolSculpting

Cost and insurance coverage for monsplasty

Like most cosmetic surgeries, monsplasty is almost always done for cosmetic and aesthetic reasons, rather than out of medical necessity.

Medicare and other insurances will only cover procedures that are medically necessary, so monsplasty is rarely covered by medical insurance.

  1. surgical center
  2. surgeon
  3. anesthesiologist
  4. anesthesia
  5. cost of materials such as bandages and sutures

Monsplasty may not be cheap but most cosmetic surgeons offer financing that may make it more affordable.

The bottom line

Monsplasty is a surgical procedure that removes excessive fatty tissue and sagging skin from your mons pubis. It’s usually done along with a tummy tuck.

It’s a low-risk outpatient procedure that offers noticeable results immediately. Mild discomfort, bruising and swelling are common after this procedure, but these side effects usually go away after 2 to 3 weeks.

Because monsplasty is usually considered a cosmetic procedure, and not one that’s medically necessary, it’s generally not covered by insurance. Be sure to check the cost and available financing options with your surgeon before you agree to the procedure.

Introduction

Liposuction has become one of the most commonly performed cosmetic surgery procedures all over the world. Evolution of liposuction has seen refinements in technique and improvement of patient safety related standards of care. Liposuction relies on an awareness of 3D anatomy of fat layers. Subcutaneous fat is divided into superficial, intermediate and deep.The majority of liposuction should be performed on the middle or deeper adipose layer. In fact, it is the preservation of the superficial adipose layer that is crucial in preventing skin irregularities including dimpling, waviness, hyperpigmentation, burns, and contour irregularities.

A variety of techniques have been described:
  1. Suction-assisted liposuction: It removes fat cells other tissues through mechanical avulsion
  2. Ultrasound-assisted liposuction: It is performed in 3 stages :tumescent anaesthesia  infiltration , UAL treatment to emulsify fat from deep to superficial layers and aspiration through suction cannula.It is of advantage in patients with saggy skin, fibrous areas and secondary surgeries.
  3. Power-assisted liposuction : It uses the reciprocating motion of the tip of the cannula to facilitate fat removal. It is advantageous in large volume liposuction as it reduces operator fatigue drastically.
  4. Laser assisted liposuction: It uses laser energy to liquefy the fat which is then aspirated via suction cannulas. The liquefying process is a bit more time consuming than other methods. 

Grading

 

  • Type 1: Localized lipodystrophy
    Younger patients with good skin tone and minimal skin irregularities.

Type 2: Generalized lipodystrophy
Slightly diminished skin tone with some skin irregularities and circumferential lipodystrophy throughout their trunk and extremities.

Type 3: Skin redundancy and lipodystrophy
Patients displaying significant skin redundancy that would be more amenable to excisional surgical techniques to improve shape and contour. Liposuction is used as an adjunct for optimal result

Are you the right candidate?

Liposuction is performed in healthy patients who maintain realistic goals and expectations. If you are willing to bring long-term life style changes involving a healthy diet and regular exercise, then you will experience the best results which will be long lasting.

Which body parts can be treated with liposuction?

Liposuction can be performed for your double chin, reduction of your breast size, arms, back, tummy/abdomen, love handles/flanks, buttocks, thighs and legs.

Process at Bliniq

Consultation

You are required to visit the clinic for a face to face consultation with Dr Ashwani Kumar. You can discuss about various issues or doubts about the surgery. The body areas you want to be treated will be examined and a plan of action, number of sittings required will be discussed with you.

You will be required to undergo a few blood tests and imaging investigations to assess your fitness.

Surgery

You are called fasting on the day of surgery. The procedure is done under general anaesthesia. The duration of surgery depends on areas being treated. You will be kept under observation for 6 hours after surgery and then you will be discharged wearing a compression garment.

Follow up visits
Day 2:
Your dressing will be changed.
Day 10:
Sutures will be removed
Day 30:
For assessing your progress and discussing any issues you are facing.
Day 90:
Assessment of your final results

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1. How much weight loss will occur after the surgery ?
A1. We expect a 10 percent body weight decrease after your full body liposuction surgery.
Q2. When will I see my final results?
A2. Your final results will be visible 3 months after the procedure.
Q3. When can I join back work/college?
A3. You need to take 7 days off from work before you are fit to join back work.
Q4. When can I take a flight after the surgery?
A4. You can travel on the 3rd day after surgery.
Q5. When can I start my exercises?
A5. You can Join back gym after 4 weeks.
Q6. The compression garment has to be worn for what duration of time?
A6. Compression garment has to be worn for 8weeks after the surgery.

Neck Liposuction in Delhi: Redefine Your Jawline With Precision

Neck liposuction in Delhi is a minimally invasive surgical procedure that permanently removes localised fat deposits from beneath the chin and along the neck to create a sharper, more defined jawline and profile. At Bliniq, the procedure is performed by board-certified cosmetic surgeons using precision cannula techniques under local or general anaesthesia, depending on the extent of fat to be removed. Neck fat does not always respond to diet or exercise because the body tends to deposit and retain fat in specific areas regardless of overall weight. If a double chin or thick neck contour has been bothering you despite a healthy weight, neck liposuction may be the most effective and permanent solution available.

Why Does Fat Accumulate Around the Neck?

The neck and chin area, medically referred to as the submental region, is one of the most genetically predetermined fat deposit zones on the body. Several factors contribute to fat buildup here:

  • Genetics: Many patients with neck fat are at a healthy body weight. The tendency to store fat in the submental region is often inherited
  • Weight gain: Fat cells in the neck expand with overall weight increase and do not always reduce proportionally with weight loss
  • Ageing: As facial and neck skin loses elasticity over time, even moderate fat deposits become more visible
  • Posture and anatomy: A naturally lower hyoid bone position can make the neck appear fuller, regardless of fat levels

Neck liposuction targets the fat cells directly and removes them permanently. Unlike weight loss, which shrinks fat cells without eliminating them, liposuction physically removes them from the treated area.

Who Is a Good Candidate for Neck Liposuction in Delhi?

You are likely a suitable candidate if:

  • You have visible fat beneath the chin or along the neck that does not respond to diet and exercise
  • Your skin has reasonable elasticity, meaning it is likely to retract smoothly after fat removal
  • You are at or near your stable, healthy body weight
  • You do not smoke, or are willing to stop at least two to three weeks before and after surgery
  • You have realistic expectations about what contouring can achieve

Patients with significant skin laxity alongside fat may benefit from a combination approach involving neck liposuction and a neck lift or thread procedure. Your surgeon at Bliniq will assess both factors at consultation and recommend the appropriate plan. Neck liposuction alone on a patient with very loose skin can sometimes leave the skin looking more slack after fat is removed, which is why this assessment matters.

The Neck Liposuction Procedure at Bliniq

Consultation: Your surgeon physically assesses the neck and chin area, examines your skin elasticity, and discusses your goals. Photographs are taken for planning and progress tracking.

Anaesthesia: Most neck liposuction procedures are performed under local anaesthesia with sedation or, for more extensive cases, light general anaesthesia. You will not feel pain during the procedure.

The procedure itself: Small incisions of approximately 2 to 4mm are made in discreet locations, typically beneath the chin and behind the earlobes. A thin cannula (tube) is inserted through these incisions and used to break up and suction out the fat cells. Tumescent fluid, a saline solution with anaesthetic and adrenaline, is injected into the area first to reduce bleeding and make fat removal easier.

The procedure typically takes 45 to 90 minutes. Most patients can return home the same day.

After the procedure: The incisions are so small that they rarely require sutures and heal on their own within a few days. You will wear a compression garment around the chin and neck for one to two weeks to reduce swelling and help the skin retract smoothly.

Recovery Timeline After Neck Liposuction

Most patients follow this general recovery pattern:

  • Days 1 to 3: Swelling, mild bruising, and tightness in the neck area. Pain is minimal and managed with prescribed medication. Rest is advised
  • Days 4 to 7: Swelling begins to reduce. Most patients return to desk work within five to seven days
  • Weeks 2 to 3: Compression garment continues to be worn, especially at night. Strenuous activity and exercise are avoided
  • Month 1 to 3: The treated area continues to settle. The full, final result, including complete resolution of swelling, becomes visible between three and six months

Patience during the recovery phase is important. The neck will look progressively better over several months rather than changing dramatically overnight.

What Results Can You Expect?

Neck liposuction produces a permanent reduction in fat cells in the treated area. As long as your weight remains stable, the fat does not return to the neck region in the same volume. Most patients notice:

  • A significantly sharper and more defined jawline
  • A cleaner transition from the face to the neck when viewed from the side
  • Better-fitting collars, necklines, and clothing around the neck
  • Improved confidence in photographs and public-facing settings

This procedure is particularly popular with professionals in Delhi NCR between the ages of 25 and 45 who are at a healthy weight but carry persistent neck fat that affects how they present in professional and social contexts.

How Much Does Neck Liposuction Cost in Delhi?

Neck liposuction cost in Delhi generally ranges from Rs. 40,000 to Rs. 1,20,000. The final cost depends on:

  • Whether the procedure is performed under local or general anaesthesia
  • Whether it is combined with a neck lift or other complementary treatment
  • The extent of fat to be removed
  • Surgeon's qualifications and facility standards

At Bliniq, a detailed cost estimate is provided after the surgeon assesses your case. EMI options make this procedure accessible to patients who prefer to spread the payment. Patients who are also managing concerns like gynecomastia alongside neck fat often find it efficient to explore both at a single consultation, and our gynecomastia treatment in Delhi page provides detailed information on how that procedure works separately.

Frequently Asked Questions

Does the fat come back after neck liposuction?

The fat cells removed by liposuction do not regenerate. Significant weight gain after surgery can cause remaining fat cells in other areas to expand, but the specific neck region treated will retain its improved contour with stable weight.

Is neck liposuction painful?

The procedure is performed under anaesthesia, so there is no pain during treatment. Post-operative discomfort is mild and well-managed with standard pain medication.

Can neck liposuction be combined with other procedures?

Yes. It is frequently combined with a chin augmentation, neck lift, or facial liposuction for a more comprehensive result. Your surgeon will advise what combination best addresses your concerns.

How long does the result last?

With stable body weight, results are considered permanent because the removed fat cells do not return.

Will there be visible scars?

The incisions are 2 to 4mm and are placed in natural skin folds or behind the ears. They are virtually invisible once healed.

Introduction

Nose has been traditionally considered to be the most important part of facial beauty in Indian culture. From the times of Ramayana, so much importance has been given to the beauty of the nose. Indian noses are generally broad based with a bulbous or a rounded tip and wide nasal dorsum to go alongwith.

Rhinoplasty or Nose-job is an easy way to modify your nose aesthetics to achieve that perfect nose you always wanted. Minor nose modifications nowadays can be achieved with Liquid Rhinoplasty without undergoing the knife, but any deformities which need structural changes still require a surgical rhinoplasty.

Techniques

Liquid Rhinoplasty

Due to the recent developments with safety and quality of hyaluronic acid fillers, now they are being commonly used to achieve minor changes in your nose. Changes which can be achieved with fillers are:

  1. Tip modification
  2. Making dorsum of the nose look narrower
  3. Correcting nasal bridge irregularities 
 

 Closed Rhinoplasty

  1. No external scar
  2. Less postoperative edema
  3. Faster recovery
  4. Less operative time
  5. Difficult to assess and visualize anatomic variations and therefore make an appropriate operative plan

Open Rhinoplasty

  1.  More accurate assessment of anatomic variations
  2. Requires wider dissection
  3. More postoperative edema particularly at the tip
  4. Exposure takes more time
  5. Possible longer recovery time
  6. Columellar scar

What modifications can be done with rhinoplasty? 

  1. Increase or decrease tip projection
  2. Narrowing of the tip
  3. Rotation of the hanging tip

  4. Correction of an asymmetric tip

  5. Lengthening of the nose
  6. Augmentation of a saddle nose deformity
  7. Straightening of the deviated cartilaginous dorsum
  8. Straightening of the crooked nose
  9. Management of a hump deformity

FAQ

Q1 .How is rhinoplasty different from septoplasty?
A1.Rhinoplasty is a surgery to change the shape of the nose. Because both breathing and the nose's shape are interrelated, a rhinoplasty may sometimes be performed not only to change the way the nose looks but also to improve breathing through the nose.

Q2.Is rhinoplasty a simple operation?
A2.No. Rhinoplasty is a challenging operation. This is due to several factors. First, the nose is complicated 3D shape that is in the middle of the face. Changes made during rhinoplasty are often
very small. But these changes can make a major difference in the way the nose looks and functions. Because these changes are small, so is the margin for error. Swelling and the placement of local anesthetic in the skin distort the nose during surgery, hiding
many of the subtle changes made. Rhinoplasty also doesn't have a standard plan or set order of steps. Doctors tailor each operation to the needs of the patient.

Q3.Will I need to stay in the hospital?
A3.Nearly everyone who has rhinoplasty is able to safely leave the hospital the same day after surgery. In rare cases, you may stay in the hospital for one night if you're having a hard time with nausea or have other health problems that need to be monitored

Knowing the lipoma removal cost is the first step to treatment. A lipoma is a common, harmless growth of fat tissue. They are often harmless, but many people choose to remove them. This guide explains the costs. We want to give you clear details for your choice. At Bliniq, we believe in clear pricing and expert care for every patient.

Understanding Lipoma and Its Removal

A lipoma feels like a soft, rubbery lump under your skin. They can show up almost anywhere on the body. Most lipomas are small and do not cause pain. However, some can grow larger or cause discomfort. Removal is a simple process. It gives you peace of mind and improves your comfort. Our team at Bliniq focuses on safe and good lipoma removal.

Otoplasty in Delhi costs between Rs. 50,000 and Rs. 1.2 lakh for a single surgical session, with the final figure determined by whether one or both ears require correction, the degree of structural complexity involved, the technique used, and the surgeon's experience. Otoplasty is ear reshaping surgery that addresses the position, contour, or size of the outer ear without affecting hearing in any way. It is one of the few cosmetic procedures performed routinely for both children from the age of six and adults across all age groups, and one of the few where patients describe the motivation as equal parts aesthetic and practical. In 2026, it remains a consistently sought procedure at Bliniq's Delhi facility, particularly among adult patients who have carried this concern for years and are finally ready to address it.

What Ear Reshaping Surgery Actually Does

The most common concern treated by otoplasty is prominent ears, where the ears project outward from the sides of the head by more than the standard distance of approximately 1.5 to 2 centimetres. This happens most often because the antihelical fold, the curved inner ridge of the outer ear, is underdeveloped or absent, causing the upper portion of the ear to splay outward. In other patients, the conchal bowl, the deeper central cup of the ear, is disproportionately large and pushes the entire ear forward from the head.

What Otoplasty Changes and What It Does Not

Otoplasty works entirely on the visible outer ear cartilage and skin. It does not touch the ear canal, eardrum, or any part of the hearing system, so hearing is completely unaffected by the procedure. Beyond prominence, otoplasty also corrects asymmetry between the two ears in terms of position or shape, ears where the upper third is unusually shaped, and earlobes that are enlarged or torn. Each of these concerns is assessed individually and planned for separately at consultation.

Takeaway: Otoplasty addresses the outer ear structure only. It produces a change that is visible to others while leaving your hearing entirely undisturbed.

What Determines the Cost of Otoplasty in Delhi?

The range of Rs. 50,000 to Rs. 1.2 lakh is a real reflection of variation in patient needs. Treating one ear costs less than treating both. A straightforward antihelical fold recreation is less technically involved than a case requiring both fold creation and conchal reduction simultaneously. A surgeon who has performed a high volume of otoplasty procedures over many years brings a refined understanding of cartilage behaviour and a consistency of result that an inexperienced surgeon cannot replicate, and that expertise is reflected in the fee.

What the Bliniq Cost Covers

The cost quoted at Bliniq includes the surgeon's fee, anaesthesia, operating theatre charges, the padded post-operative bandage worn for the first week, and scheduled follow-up visits during your recovery period. Pre-operative blood tests and take-home medications are listed separately. You receive a clear written breakdown at the consultation before committing to the procedure.

Takeaway: The surgeon's experience is the most important cost variable in otoplasty. Cartilage that is over-corrected or inconsistently shaped cannot easily be revised, making the initial choice of surgeon the highest-stakes decision.

The Two Main Surgical Techniques Used in Otoplasty

The otoplasty technique is not one-size-fits-all. The approach your surgeon uses depends on the stiffness and characteristics of your specific cartilage, and many surgeons use elements of both primary techniques in combination.

The Suture Technique

In the suture technique, also known as the Mustarde approach, permanent or long-lasting internal sutures are placed through the ear cartilage to fold it into the correct position and create the absent antihelical fold. The cartilage is not cut or removed. This technique is well-suited to patients with more pliable cartilage, typically children and younger adults, where the tissue responds readily to being repositioned and held.

Cartilage Scoring

In the scoring technique, the front surface of the cartilage is weakened by making controlled shallow cuts or abrasions that allow it to flex into a new shape. This technique is used for patients with stiffer cartilage, more commonly adults, where the suture technique alone may not hold reliably over time as the cartilage resists repositioning. Most experienced surgeons combine both approaches, using scoring to allow the cartilage to flex and sutures to hold it in position.

Takeaway: The technique your surgeon selects is determined by what your cartilage will actually respond to, not by preference, which is why an experienced surgeon who has seen a wide range of cartilage types produces more reliable results.

What Recovery Looks Like After Otoplasty at Bliniq

A padded bandage covers the ears for the first five to seven days following surgery. After the bandage is removed at your first follow-up appointment, a soft headband is worn, particularly at night, for a further four to six weeks to prevent the ears from being accidentally pulled or folded during sleep. Most patients return to everyday activity within seven to ten days. Strenuous activity and contact sports are restricted for six to eight weeks, since a blow to the ear during the healing period could displace the repositioned cartilage. The final ear position and contour are clear by eight weeks when residual swelling and healing stiffness have resolved.

Patients who are planning any skin rejuvenation treatments around the ear or facial area should wait until healing is complete. Those interested in Mesotherapy for skin quality improvement in the area should confirm timing with their surgeon before scheduling any additional treatments.

When Is the Right Time to Consider Otoplasty?

For children, the standard recommendation is to wait until the age of five or six, when the ear cartilage has developed adequately to hold its reshaped position while still being sufficiently pliable to work with surgically. Acting before the age of seven or eight, before significant social awareness of physical appearance develops, is a common approach among parents who are advised this is appropriate by the operating surgeon.

For adults, the right time is whenever the concern has become significant enough to justify the procedure. There is no upper age limit for a healthy adult. Many patients describe having thought about ear correction since childhood and having finally decided to act in their thirties or forties. That delay is understandable, and the surgical outcome for adults is just as effective as for children.

Otoplasty is also sought by patients undergoing facial feminisation who want their ears to frame the face differently as part of a broader change in facial appearance. For those exploring wider gender-affirming surgical care, Bliniq also provides sex change surgery as part of its specialist offering.

Takeaway: There is no single correct age for otoplasty. The right time is when the concern feels significant enough to address, and the patient is in good health to do so.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is otoplasty suitable for adults as well as children?

Yes. Otoplasty is equally appropriate for adults and children. The minimum recommended age for children is five to six years when the ear cartilage has developed sufficiently. For adults, there is no upper age limit, provided general health is good. Many adult patients describe having considered the procedure for years before proceeding.

What is the difference between the suture technique and cartilage scoring in otoplasty?

The suture technique uses internal stitches to fold the cartilage into a new position without cutting it. Cartilage scoring weakens the cartilage by making controlled surface abrasions, allowing it to flex more easily. Most surgeons combine both approaches based on the stiffness and behaviour of each patient’s individual cartilage.

How noticeable is the scar after otoplasty?

The incision is placed in the crease behind the ear, where it is hidden by the ear itself in everyday situations. The scar fades progressively over twelve to eighteen months and is not visible in photographs or normal daily life for the vast majority of patients.

Can otoplasty correct asymmetry if one ear is more prominent than the other?

Yes. Unilateral correction addresses one ear only. However, when one ear is notably more prominent, the other often benefits from minor adjustment as well to achieve balance. Your surgeon will assess both ears and give a clear recommendation at the consultation.

Can otoplasty results relapse over time?

Some minor relaxation from the immediate post-operative position is normal during the first few weeks of healing. However, the ear does not return to its original position in patients where the procedure is performed correctly. The fibrous tissue that forms during healing supports the repositioned cartilage permanently.

Introduction

An adequate chest wall is psychologically very important for males. A well-developed chest denotes fitness, strength, and power. The exposure of the male body in media, which features large and well-defined pectoral muscles, makes the latter highly desirable as an ideal standard.
Many men strive to develop their pectoral muscles for a look of bulk and projection. Some men work out extensively and still are unable to develop the pectoral muscle they hope to achieve. It is a fact that some body types do not achieve chest muscle tone and definition as easily as others. Genetically, some men are predisposed to building fullness in the lower rather than the upper part of the chest. This gives them an unbalanced, heavy look. Even those men who work out frequently may gain strength in the pectoral area without noticeable enlargement of the muscle. In other instances, underdevelopment of the muscle in the chest can be a result of a growth defect or injury.

Chest enhancement surgery via pectoral implant insertion has been growing rapidly, attracting guys who are conscious about their looks to create an impression.

Indications

  1. Men unable to develop their pectoral muscles even with a regular exercise regimen.
  2. Men with congenital absence of the pectoralis muscle on one side
  3. Men with injuries to the muscle due to accident or sports injury
  4. Women undergoing gender reassignment surgery

Procedure

The procedure is performed under General anaesthesia. A 4cms incision is given in the axilla through which a pocket is dissected for insertion of the implant. The procedure lasts for 45-60 minutes. Patient is kept under observation for 6 to 8 hours and then discharged to home with instructions.

Follow-up Visits

Day 3
You are required to visit the clinic after 3 days for getting a dressing change and to monitor your progress.

Day 15
You will be visiting the clinic for suture removal and to discuss any issues you are facing after surgery.

Day 30
The doctor will examine you and advice regarding faster recovery and discuss with you any other issues you are facing.

Frequently Asked Questions

After the Weight Loss: What Post Bariatric Surgery in Delhi Can Do for Your Body

Losing a large amount of weight through bariatric surgery is a significant achievement. But for many patients, reaching their target weight reveals a new concern: excess, loose skin that diet and exercise cannot address. Post-bariatric surgery in Delhi refers to a category of body contouring procedures performed after significant weight loss to remove and tighten sagging skin across the abdomen, arms, thighs, chest, and back. At Bliniq, our cosmetic surgeons work with patients who have completed their weight loss journey and are ready for the final step of reshaping and restoring their bodies. These procedures are not about vanity. For many patients, loose skin causes rashes, infections, hygiene difficulties, and chronic discomfort that significantly affects quality of life.

Why Loose Skin Happens After Bariatric Surgery

When the body carries excess weight for a prolonged period, the skin stretches significantly to accommodate the volume. The underlying collagen and elastin fibres that give skin its ability to retract become damaged over time. When weight is lost rapidly, as it typically is after bariatric procedures like sleeve gastrectomy or gastric bypass, the skin does not have enough time or structural capacity to retract on its own.

Several factors determine how much loose skin a patient is left with:

  • The amount of total weight lost and the speed at which it was lost
  • The patient's age, since skin elasticity declines with age
  • Genetics and the original distribution of body fat
  • How long the patient carried excess weight before the bariatric procedure
  • Smoking history, which damages skin quality significantly

Most bariatric surgeons recommend waiting at least 12 to 18 months after weight loss surgery before pursuing body contouring. This allows the weight to stabilise fully and the skin to settle to its new position before surgical planning begins.

Procedures Offered as Part of Post Bariatric Body Contouring at Bliniq

Post-bariatric body contouring is not a single procedure. It is a planned, staged surgical programme tailored to where excess skin is most significant for each patient.

Abdominoplasty (Tummy Tuck) This is the most commonly requested post-bariatric procedure. The surgeon removes excess abdominal skin and tightens the underlying muscle layer. After significant weight loss, the abdominal skin often hangs in a pronounced apron-like fold called a pannus. Abdominoplasty removes this fold and creates a flatter, firmer abdominal profile. In some patients, a lower body lift that addresses the abdomen, flanks, and lower back simultaneously is more appropriate than an isolated tummy tuck.

Brachioplasty (Arm Lift) Significant weight loss often leaves skin hanging from the inner upper arms. This loose skin, commonly called bat wings, cannot be resolved with exercise because the skin itself has lost its ability to retract. Brachioplasty removes the excess skin from the inner arm between the elbow and armpit, restoring a tighter contour.

Thigh Lift Loose inner thigh skin causes chafing, rashes, and difficulty with movement. A thigh lift removes excess skin from the inner or outer thigh and reshapes the thigh for a smoother, more proportionate lower body.

Breast Lift (Mastopexy) or Male Chest Contouring Weight loss frequently deflates and droops the breast tissue. A breast lift repositions and reshapes the breast to a more youthful position without necessarily adding volume. For men who have lost significant weight, residual glandular tissue or loose chest skin may require surgical contouring as a separate procedure.

Lower Body Lift This is a circumferential procedure that addresses the abdomen, flanks, outer thighs, and buttocks in a single surgery. It is suited for patients with excess skin across the entire lower trunk and is one of the most comprehensive post-bariatric contouring options.

How Staging Is Planned at Bliniq

Post-bariatric body contouring is rarely completed in a single surgical session. Combining too many procedures at once increases surgical risk, complication rates, and recovery burden. At Bliniq, the surgeon works with you to create a staged surgical plan based on:

  • Which areas of excess skin are causing the most physical or functional discomfort
  • Your overall health and fitness for surgery
  • A realistic timeline that accounts for healing between procedures
  • Budget and recovery considerations between stages

For most patients, the abdomen is addressed first, followed by the arms and thighs in subsequent stages spaced at least three to six months apart.

Are You Ready for Post Bariatric Surgery?

Before any body contouring procedure is planned, the following conditions must be met:

  • Weight must be stable for a minimum of six months, and ideally 12 months
  • BMI should be at a healthy or near-healthy level, typically below 30 to 32 for most procedures
  • Nutritional levels, particularly protein, iron, and vitamins, must be adequate
  • Any comorbidities, such as diabetes or hypertension, should be well controlled
  • The patient must be a non-smoker or willing to stop at least six weeks before and after surgery

Patients who are still losing weight or whose weight is fluctuating are not suitable candidates for body contouring yet. Operating on skin that continues to change after surgery undermines the result.

What Does Post Bariatric Surgery Cost in Delhi?

The cost of post-bariatric body contouring in Delhi depends on the procedure or combination of procedures planned, the extent of skin removal required, whether general anaesthesia is used, and the number of surgical stages involved.

As a general reference:

  • Abdominoplasty: Rs. 1,00,000 to Rs. 2,50,000
  • Lower body lift: Rs. 2,00,000 to Rs. 3,50,000
  • Brachioplasty: Rs. 70,000 to Rs. 1,50,000
  • Thigh lift: Rs. 90,000 to Rs. 2,00,000

These are indicative ranges. Your actual cost is discussed in full at the consultation. Bliniq offers EMI options to help patients manage staged procedures across a planned timeline. If you are also considering skin quality treatments between surgical stages, our mesotherapy service supports skin hydration and texture during the recovery and waiting periods.

Frequently Asked Questions

How long after bariatric surgery should I wait for body contouring?

Most surgeons recommend at least 12 to 18 months after your bariatric procedure and a period of weight stability lasting at least six months before any contouring surgery is planned.

Will post bariatric surgery leave scars?

Yes. All skin excision procedures leave scars. However, incisions are placed strategically along natural body creases and underwear lines to keep them as discreet as possible. Scars fade significantly over 12 to 18 months.

Can all areas be addressed in one surgery?

Only in selected cases. Combining too many procedures in a single session carries significant risk. A staged approach over 12 to 24 months is safer and typically produces better individual results.

Is post bariatric surgery covered by health insurance in India?

Most insurers classify body contouring as cosmetic and do not cover it. In cases where excess skin causes documented medical complications such as chronic rashes or infections, coverage may be partially applicable. Your insurer should be consulted directly.

Does losing more weight after the procedure affect the result?

Significant additional weight loss after body contouring can affect the result. Maintaining stable weight after surgery is important for preserving the outcome. Your surgeon will discuss target weight and timing considerations at consultation.

INTRODUCTION
Platelet rich plasma therapy is one of the most common hair fall solutions being used all over the world. It provides the following growth factors to your hair and stop your hair loss:

  1. platelet-derived growth factor (PDGF)
  2. transforming growth factor ? (TGF-?)
  3. vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF)
  4. epidermal growth factor (EGF)
  5. insulin-like growth factor (IGF)
  6. fibroblast growth factor
INDICATIONS
  • Joint pains (chronic arthritis)
  • Chronic tendinopathies.
  • Ligament sprains.
  • Muscles strains.
  • Intervertebral discs.
  • Plantar fasciitis.
  • Subacute and chronic sports injuries.
  • Nerve injuries.


Method

  1. Your blood sample is withdrawn and centrifuged in a pre-programmed machine for a specific amount of time and revolutions.
  2. PRP thus recieved is injected into your scalp in the central area of your scalp prone to male pattern baldness.
  3. The procedure is performed under Local anaesthesia and lasts for 15mins.
  4. You are given 4 sessions at monthly intervals in one go.

PROCEDURE

PRP therapy is a relatively simple process that can be performed in under two hours. It does not require hospitalization, and may be done as an outpatient procedure.

Unlike blood tests, where the patient is generally instructed not to eat or drink for up to 12 hours before, no preparations are required for PRP therapy. The patient is only recommended to hydrate well in advance, as higher levels of water in the body will make drawing blood easier for the doctor.

If you use corticosteroids or NSAID anti-inflammatory medicines, such as ibuprofen (Motrin) or naproxen (Aleve), you will be required to have stopped taking them up to a week before PRP therapy, and not to resume use of these types of medications immediately afterwards. They will interfere with PRP therapy and possibly nullify any results.

The steps involved in PRP therapy are:

Step 1: Collect the patient’s own blood.
Less than two ounces (between 15 to 50 milliliters) are required for the procedure. The collection is virtually identical toprp therapy process giving blood for a blood test, with a collection needle inserted into a vein in the arm and the blood captured in a small vial.

Step 2: Centrifuge the blood.
A centrifuge is a device that spins at high speeds. This action physically separates the solid and liquid parts of the blood: red blood cells (erythrocytes), white blood cells (leukocytes), platelets (thrombocytes), and plasma (liquid).

  • Step 3: Process and collect the platelets.
    Regular blood contains about 200,000 platelets per milliliter, while platelet-rich plasma contains as much as five times that amount. The resulting three to seven milliliters of platelet-rich plasma will be collected in a syringe, to be administered immediately.
  • Step 4: Inject the PRP into the desired site.
    The final syringe of platelet-rich plasma will contain approximately 1-2 teaspoons of fluid. With the guidance of an ultrasound probe, the PRP will be guided into the proper location, based on the nature of the injury being treated.

FAQ

Q1 What is PRP treatment good for?
A1 PRP treatment has been used to support wound healing in trauma and joint injury. The technique can address male pattern baldness, stimulate the growth of hair transplants and enhance other cosmetic procedures.

Q2 Why is PRP so painful?
A2 You may feel more pain at first, since we are causing swelling in the area that was already sore. The swelling is needed for the blood cells to start helping you heal for the long term.

Q3 Does PRP really work?
A3 PRP consists of a large number of proteins and growth factor mediums in it. It is believed that due to its rich content of tissue repairing components, PRP can be successfully administered to select cases of hair loss. It is known that a certain type of hair loss results from damage to the hair follicles

Introduction

Puffy nipples is a common condition seen in men. It presents as change in size of the nipples with seasonal change or on excitement . Patients commonly complaints of not being able to wear body hugging clothing. It is commonly associated with gynecomastia grade 1. Some patients also complain of pain being associated with the puffy nipples

Treatment 

Surgery
Surgery is the only modality of treatment approved for its treatment. Surgery is performed through a small incision in the nipple-areola complex through which the glandular tissue present under your nipple areola complex is excised.

Procedure at Bliniq

You are required to visit the clinic for consultation or a video consultation can be arranged for you with our Plastic Surgeon, Dr Ashwani Kumar, at the time of your convenience. Few blood tests will be prescribed at the time of consultation.

Surgery

Follow up

Day 10
You will be visiting the clinic for suture removal.

Day 30

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1. When can I join back work ?
A1. You can join back office 1 day after the surgery.

Q2. Will there be scars after the surgery ?
A2. The surgery is performed through a 2.5 cm incision within your nipple-areola complex and the scar is hardly discernible after 3 months have passed by.

The Nose You Have Always Wanted: Choosing the Right Rhinoplasty Surgeon in Delhi

The nose sits at the centre of the face. It affects every angle, every photograph, and often, every moment of self-consciousness a person carries. Rhinoplasty in Delhi is a surgical procedure to reshape the nose for cosmetic refinement, functional correction, or both. At Bliniq, rhinoplasty is performed by board-certified cosmetic surgeons who understand that the goal is never to create a generic nose, but to reshape what exists into something better proportioned, better functioning, and unmistakably yours. This page covers what rhinoplasty involves, what makes a great rhinoplasty surgeon, and how to make the right decision before you commit to one of the most visible surgical changes a person can make.

What Makes Rhinoplasty Different From Every Other Cosmetic Procedure?

Most cosmetic procedures can be revised, corrected, or adjusted with relative ease. Rhinoplasty cannot. The nose is a three-dimensional structure made of bone, cartilage, soft tissue, and skin, and each of these layers behaves differently after surgery. A modification in one zone changes the appearance of another. The nasal tip, bridge, nostrils, and profile are interconnected in ways that require the surgeon to think sculpturally, not just technically.

This is why the choice of rhinoplasty surgeon in Delhi is the single most important decision in the entire process. Surgical technique alone is not enough. A rhinoplasty surgeon must have a calibrated aesthetic sensibility, strong anatomical knowledge, and significant case volume specifically in nasal surgery before they can reliably deliver natural, proportionate results.

At Bliniq, patients who present for rhinoplasty receive a thorough facial assessment before any surgical plan is discussed. The assessment examines facial width, jawline definition, forehead projection, and skin thickness before determining what changes to the nose will improve overall harmony rather than simply altering a single feature.

What Concerns Does Rhinoplasty Address?

Rhinoplasty is performed for a wide range of concerns, both cosmetic and functional.

Cosmetic concerns surgeons address:

  • A dorsal hump (bump on the bridge of the nose when viewed in profile)
  • A drooping or bulbous nasal tip that lacks definition
  • A wide nose at the bridge or at the nostrils (alar base)
  • A crooked or asymmetric nose
  • Overprojection or underprojection of the nasal tip
  • A flat nasal bridge requiring augmentation using cartilage graft
  • Asymmetry between the two sides of the nose

Functional concerns surgeons address:

  • A deviated nasal septum that restricts breathing through one or both nostrils
  • Collapsed internal nasal valves that cause breathing difficulty
  • Enlarged turbinates that reduce airflow

When both cosmetic and functional concerns exist in the same patient, a combined procedure called rhinoseptoplasty addresses both simultaneously. This is one of the more complex rhinoplasty procedures and requires a surgeon experienced in both aesthetic and functional nasal surgery.

Open Versus Closed Rhinoplasty: What Is the Difference?

The two primary surgical approaches to rhinoplasty are open and closed, and the right choice depends on the degree and complexity of reshaping required.

Open Rhinoplasty

A small incision is made across the columella, the narrow strip of tissue between the two nostrils. This allows the surgeon to lift the skin and access the full nasal structure. Open rhinoplasty provides superior visibility of the underlying anatomy and is used for complex cases involving significant tip reshaping, cartilage grafting, or revision of a previous procedure. The columella incision heals into a very fine, nearly invisible scar within a few months.

Closed Rhinoplasty

All incisions are made inside the nostrils, leaving no visible external scar. This approach is suited for cases requiring minor adjustments, such as a modest bridge reduction or subtle tip refinement. The operating field is more limited than in open rhinoplasty, which means its suitability depends entirely on the complexity of the changes required.

Revision Rhinoplasty

Patients who have had a previous rhinoplasty that produced unsatisfactory results or caused structural complications may require revision surgery. Revision rhinoplasty is significantly more complex than primary rhinoplasty because the tissue has already been operated on and scar tissue may have formed. It requires both advanced technical skill and realistic expectation setting.

What the Surgical Process Looks Like at Bliniq

Consultation: The surgeon conducts a detailed examination of the nose and face. Your medical history, any previous nasal procedures, and your specific concerns are discussed. Photographs are taken from multiple angles to assist with surgical planning.

Pre-operative preparation: Blood-thinning medications and supplements are stopped two weeks before surgery. Smoking and alcohol are avoided to support healing. Pre-operative tests, including blood work and an ECG, are completed.

Surgery: Rhinoplasty at Bliniq is performed under general anaesthesia. The procedure typically takes two to four hours, depending on complexity. Bone and cartilage are reshaped, grafts are placed where required, and incisions are closed with fine sutures.

Recovery: A nasal splint is applied and worn for approximately seven to ten days. Swelling is significant in the first two weeks and then reduces gradually. The majority of visible swelling resolves within six to eight weeks, though subtle refinement continues for up to twelve months. Most patients return to office work within ten to fourteen days and resume physical activity after four to six weeks.

Rhinoplasty cost in Delhi ranges from Rs. 45,000 for minor adjustments to Rs. 4,00,000 for complex primary or revision procedures. The cost at Bliniq is provided in a detailed, transparent estimate after consultation. EMI options are available. Five Questions to Ask Your Rhinoplasty Surgeon Before Agreeing to Surgery

  1. How many rhinoplasty procedures do you perform per year, and can I see before-and-after photographs of patients with a similar concern to mine?
  2. Will you be performing open or closed rhinoplasty for my case, and why?
  3. If I need cartilage grafting, where will the graft be taken from?
  4. What are the most likely complications specific to the changes I am requesting?
  5. What is your protocol if I am unhappy with the result or if there is an issue during recovery?

A surgeon who answers each of these questions clearly and without deflection is demonstrating the standard of transparency you should expect.

Frequently Asked Questions

How long does rhinoplasty take to show its final result?

Swelling from rhinoplasty resolves gradually. While the overall shape improvement is visible within six to eight weeks, the full, final result, including subtle tip refinement, takes up to twelve months.

Can rhinoplasty also fix my breathing?

Yes. A combined rhinoseptoplasty corrects both the cosmetic appearance and internal structural issues affecting breathing in a single procedure.

Is the surgery painful?

The procedure is performed under general anaesthesia. Post-operative discomfort is mild to moderate and managed with prescribed pain relief. Most patients describe the recovery as pressure and congestion rather than sharp pain.

At what age can rhinoplasty be performed?

Surgeons typically recommend waiting until nasal growth is complete, which is around 17 to 18 years in females and 18 to 19 years in males, before performing rhinoplasty.

Will my nose look natural after surgery?

A well-planned rhinoplasty should produce a result that looks natural and proportionate to your face. At Bliniq, the surgical goal is always harmony, never a dramatic or obvious change.

INTRODUCTION

Underarm surgery or brachioplasty is more commonly known as an arm lift. This procedure tightens loose, sagging skin and removes excess fat deposits in the upper arm that develop with age or significant weight loss.

INDICATIONS

  1. excessive bleeding.
  2. permanent scarring.
  3. fluid buildup.
  4. nerve, muscle, or blood vessel damage.
  5. fatty tissue death.

PROCEDURE

Before the procedure

  1. Avoid certain medications. You'll likely need to avoid taking aspirin, anti-inflammatory
    drugs and herbal supplements, which can increase bleeding.
  2. Arrange for help during recovery. Make plans for someone to drive you home after surgery
    and stay with you as you begin to recover.

During the procedure

Your plastic surgeon will make incisions on the undersides of your arms. The length and
pattern of the incisions depends on how much skin will be removed.

After making the incisions, the plastic surgeon will tighten your underlying tissues and
secure them with stitches. He or she might also use a suction technique to remove fat
(liposuction).

Your skin will then be draped over the new contours and excess skin will be removed.
Stitches or surgical tape will be used to close the incisions.

After the procedure

After an arm lift, your incisions will be covered in bandages. Your arms will be loosely
wrapped in elastic bandages to minimize swelling. Small tubes might be placed in your arms
to drain any excess blood or fluid.

You'll likely see someone from your plastic surgery team within a day or two after your arm
lift. He or she may remove your bandages and drainage tube if used. Some plastic surgeons
may have you wear a compression sleeve for a few weeks to keep swelling down

In the first few days after an arm lift:

  1. Avoid lifting your arms above shoulder level for three to four weeks.
  2. Avoid physical and athletic activities with your arms that might stretch the incisions for
    four to eight weeks after surgery.
  3. Take pain medication as needed and use topical or oral antibiotics as directed to
    prevent wound infections.

Talk to your doctor about when — or if — your stitches will be removed. Some stitches
dissolve on their own. Others must be removed in the doctor's office in the weeks after
the procedure.

After an arm lift, contact your doctor immediately if you have:

  1. Shortness of breath
  2. Chest pains
  3. An irregular heartbeat
  4. Redness of the skin and a fever

Results

By removing loose skin, an arm lift can give your upper arms a more toned
appearance.
Arm lift results are typically long lasting. Remember, however, your skin will naturally
lose some firmness as you age and some sagging might occur. Maintaining a stable,
healthy weight can help you retain your results.

FAQ

Q1 Is there surgery for flabby arms?
A1 Similarly, flabby arms can be corrected through a procedure known as
brachioplasty, or arm lift surgery. It is common to develop sagging, “bat-wing” arms as
we age or fluctuate in weight.


Q2 What is the best treatment for sagging arms?
A2 With no anaesthesia and no downtime, CoolSculpting is the ideal treatment to tone
your arms, targeting fat cells beneath the skin while leaving the skin itself unaffected.
The treated fat cells are crystallised (frozen), then die.


Q3 What is the recovery time for an arm lift?
A3 There is no universal timeline for recovery from an arm lift. Everybody heals at a
different rate based on factors such as skin health and overall health. However, in
general, the entire recovery process will take around two to three weeks.
