INTRODUCTION
Scars form as part of the healing process after your skin has been cut or damaged. The skin repairs itself by growing new tissue to pull together the wound and fill in any gaps caused by the injury. Scar tissue is made primarily of a protein called collagen.

Scars develop in all shapes and sizes. Some scars are large and painful, while some are barely visible. People with dark skin (especially people with African, Asian or Hispanic heritage), as well as red-haired individuals, are more likely to develop keloid scars. Keloids are raised scars that grow and extend beyond the injured area. Depending on their size, type and location, your scars may look unsightly and may even make it difficult to move.

Not all scars require treatment, and many fade away over time. If a scar is bothering you or causing pain, treatments can help.

Scars don’t get removed they are made less noticeable so a better term used for scar reduction is scar remodeling. Scar management depends on several factors:

  1. Depth of the scar
  2. Width of the scar
  3. Weather it’s crossing a joint or not
  4. Is the scar raised from surrounding area or not?
  5. Cause of scarring
  6. Is it causing any functional deformity or not?
  7. Is it associated with hypertrophic scarring/keloid formation?

Treatment modalities available for scar remodeling are:

  1. Surgical scar revision: Used for wide linear scars
  2. Laser: Used for shallow scars over a wide area like acne scarring over cheeks etc.
  3. Triamcinolone injections: Used for hypertrophic scarring or keloid formation
  4. Skin grafting/Flaps: Used for burn scar contracture across joints.
  5. Z-Plasty/W-Plasty: For linear scar revisions or burn contractures.
  6. Subcision with fat injection: Used for acne scarring or burn scars over wide areas.

Serial excision: For very wide scars

INDICATIONS

Scars are part of the body’s healing process. As part of your immune system, your skin is the barrier to protect you from germs and other harmful substances. When skin is injured, the body creates new tissue made of collagen to help reseal itself.

Collagen plays many important roles throughout your body, including plumping up your skin and helping your cartilage protect your joints. When a scar develops, collagen fibers repair damaged skin and close any open areas. The new tissue protects you from infection.

PROCEDURE

Can scars be treated?

Several treatments can make scars smaller or less noticeable. Your healthcare provider may recommend one treatment or a combination. Scar treatment depends on several factors, including:

  • Type, size and location of the scar.
  • Whether the scar is causing you pain or affecting your ability to move.
  • Your age and the age of the scar.
What are the treatments for scars?

Treatments can reduce a scar’s size or appearance, but the scar will never completely go away. Some treatments prevent a scar from forming as a wound heals. Scar treatments include:

  1. Dermabrasion: A common acne scar treatment, dermabrasion removes the top layer of skin by gently “sanding” the skin. The procedure softens and smooths the skin and can improve the appearance of scars.
  2. Injections: Your healthcare provider injects medication directly into the scar, making it smaller and flatter. Corticosteroid injections can reduce the size of keloid scars. Your healthcare provider may inject drugs that treat cancer, such as bleomycin (Bleo 15k™) and fluorouracil (Adrucil® or 5-FU), to flatten scars and reduce itching and pain.
  3. Laser treatments: Several types of laser and light treatments can make scars (including acne scars) less noticeable. Laser treatments use a particular wavelength of light to cause a particular action in the skin. The V beam is a pulsed dye laser at 595 nm (nanometers) that targets small blood vessels in the skin. Sometimes the scars remain pink or red because the new blood vessels that developed to heal the wound never receded once their job was done. This laser can cauterize the small vessels from the inside out to remove them from the scar and allow the pink or red color to fade. This action may also help the scar flatten if it’s too thick or thicken if it is too thin. Other lasers (such as the Fraxel laser) can vaporize small columns of tissue within the scar to break up the collagen fibers and allow the scar to remodel and become more flexible. The treatments can also help with pain, itching and sensitivity. Laser treatments may cause hyperpigmentation (skin darkening) or hypopigmentation (skin lightening) in people who have dark skin. Talk to your healthcare provider about side effects before starting treatments.
  4. Pressure therapy: An elastic bandage, dressing or stocking puts pressure on a wound during the healing process. The pressure prevents a scar from forming or decreases its size. Massage therapy can also help break up scar tissue and allow it to remodel.
  5. Scar-revision surgery: A range of surgical procedures can remove a scar, improve its appearance or transplant skin from another area (skin graft). This is an exchange of one type of scar for a different, more preferable scar.
  6. Topical creams and ointments: Applying silicone ointment to a scar may make it smaller or prevent it from forming. Or your healthcare provider may recommend applying corticosteroid cream or a silicone gel sheet to the area. If you have dark skin, ask your provider about using a skin-lightening cream with hydroquinone to lighten scars.

FAQ
Q1 Does scar revision really work?
A1 Scar revision helps in making the scar less noticeable and more cosmetically appealing; however, it does not completely erase it. Scar revision is also useful in restoring a poorly healed scar. Scar revision may include both surgical and non-surgical techniques.

Q2 Can you get surgery to fix a scar?
A2 Scar revision is plastic surgery performed to improve the condition or appearance of a scar anywhere on your body. The different types of scars include: Discoloration or surface irregularities and other more subtle scars can be cosmetically improved by surgery or other treatments recommended by your plastic surgeon.

Q3 Is scar revision surgery painful?
A3 You may instead be given local anesthesia to numb the surgery area. With local anesthesia, you may still feel pressure or pushing during surgery, but you should not feel any sharp pain. Your surgeon may make an incision around the scar to remove it.

Scar Removal Surgery in Delhi: Types, Results and Cost at Bliniq

Scar removal surgery in Delhi is a clinical procedure designed to reduce the visible appearance of scars caused by acne, burns, injuries, or previous operations. At Bliniq, our board-certified cosmetic surgeons evaluate each scar individually and recommend a treatment plan based on scar type, skin tone, depth, and location. Scars cannot be completely erased, but surgery can make them significantly less noticeable, smoother in texture, and closer in colour to the surrounding skin. Both surgical and non-surgical options are available depending on what your scar requires. If a scar is affecting your confidence or daily comfort, a consultation with a qualified cosmetic surgeon at Bliniq is the right first step.

What Is Scar Removal Surgery?

Scar removal surgery, also called scar revision, is a procedure that improves how a scar looks or functions. Scars form when the deeper layer of skin, called the dermis, is damaged. The body repairs this damage with collagen, but the new tissue rarely matches the original skin in texture or colour. Surgery either removes the old scar tissue and closes the wound more precisely, or resurfacing techniques are used so the scar blends better with the surrounding skin. In some cases, particularly after burns, scar tissue tightens and restricts movement. Surgery here restores function alongside appearance.

The type of procedure selected depends entirely on the nature and severity of the scar, which is why a clinical assessment comes before any treatment decision.

What Types of Scars Do We Treat?

At Bliniq, we treat all major categories of scars:

Acne Scars: Depressed or pitted scars most commonly appear on the face, chest, or back. These are among the most frequently treated concerns in patients aged 20 to 35.

Keloid Scars: Thick, raised scars that grow beyond the original wound boundary. They are more common in deeper skin tones and carry a risk of recurring without careful management.

Hypertrophic Scars: Raised scars that remain within the wound boundary. They often respond well to a combination of surgical excision and laser treatment.

Burn and Contracture Scars: Burns can cause extensive scarring that tightens over time and restricts movement. Skin grafting is frequently required to address both appearance and function.

Post-Surgical and Traumatic Scars: Scars from previous operations or accidents that are wide, uneven, or poorly positioned. Scar revision can reposition and refine these so they heal in a far less visible way.

Identifying your scar type accurately is the foundation of every successful scar revision outcome.

What Scar Removal Procedures Are Available at Bliniq?

Surgical Scar Excision: The scar tissue is removed, and wound edges are sutured with precision. The resulting scar is thinner and less visible than the original. Best suited for wide, discoloured, or poorly healed scars.

Laser Scar Resurfacing: Fractional CO2 and erbium laser treatments remove damaged outer skin layers while stimulating collagen production deeper in the skin. Results improve across multiple sessions. Downtime is typically five to seven days of redness and mild peeling.

Dermabrasion: A controlled mechanical technique that smooths surface irregularities. Effective for raised scars with significant textural variation.

Skin Grafting: Healthy skin is taken from another area of the body and placed over a large scarred region. This is the preferred approach for extensive burn scars or contractures where movement is restricted.

Dermal Fillers: Injectable fillers such as hyaluronic acid are used to fill depressed or pitted scars and bring them level with the surrounding skin. Results are visible immediately with no surgical downtime. Patients interested in broader skin quality improvement can also explore mesotherapy at Bliniq as a complementary treatment.

Combination Approach: Many patients achieve better results through a combination of procedures. Surgical excision followed by a laser course, for example, often outperforms either treatment alone. Bliniq's surgeons design multi-step plans where clinically appropriate.

Your procedure is selected based on your scar's specific characteristics, not a default treatment list.

How Do Our Surgeons Decide the Right Treatment for You?

Every scar revision at Bliniq begins with a detailed consultation. The surgeon physically examines the scar, reviews its history, and considers several clinical factors before recommending any treatment.

The age of the scar matters because a maturing scar may respond to simpler options, while an older scar may need surgery. Location is important because scars on joints or areas under constant tension behave differently from those on flat surfaces. Skin type and tone are carefully assessed since certain laser wavelengths carry different risk profiles for patients with deeper melanin levels. A previous history of keloid formation is also factored in, as this affects both procedure selection and post-treatment management.

The consultation at Bliniq is not a sales appointment. It is a clinical discussion where you receive an honest assessment of what is achievable for your specific scar, and a clear explanation of every option available to you.

Your treatment plan is built around your scar, your skin, and your goals.

What to Expect Before, During and After Scar Removal Surgery

Before: A confidential consultation with your surgeon. Pre-procedure photographs may be taken to document the baseline and track progress over time.

During: Procedures are performed under local anesthesia, sedation, or general anesthesia depending on the complexity. Laser treatments and filler injections require only topical numbing. Surgical excision and grafting are performed in a dedicated surgical suite.

After: For surgical excision, most patients return to normal daily activity within seven to ten days. Strenuous activity is avoided for at least two to three weeks. Swelling and redness are expected in the first few days and are managed with prescribed medication. For laser treatments, mild redness and peeling typically resolve within five to seven days. Sun exposure must be avoided during healing, and daily sunscreen use is essential.

Scar revision results continue to improve for up to twelve months after treatment as the skin heals and remodels. Following your surgeon's aftercare instructions is the most important factor in achieving the best possible outcome.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can scar removal surgery completely remove a scar?

No. Surgery significantly reduces the visibility, texture difference, and pigmentation of a scar but does not eliminate it entirely. A finer, better-positioned scar replaces the original.

How many sessions will I need?

Surgical excision may require a single session. Laser-based treatments typically involve three to six sessions. Keloid management may require a combination approach with follow-up treatments.

Is the procedure painful?

All procedures are performed under appropriate anesthesia. Post-procedure discomfort is managed with prescribed medication and typically resolves within a few days.

How long before I see final results?

Initial improvement is visible within weeks. Full results develop over six to twelve months as the treated skin continues to remodel.

Who is a suitable candidate?

Most adults in good general health with a fully matured scar are suitable candidates. The only way to confirm candidacy accurately is through a consultation with one of Bliniq’s cosmetic surgeons.

Introduction

Lipoma is a benign tumor of adipose tissue or fat cells. It can be solitary or occur all over the body in large numbers. It may be associated with pain in a few people and may be familial in a few cases. People mostly get it removed due to other people disturbing them repeatedly about its unsightly presence. 

Causes of Lipoma 

1.Idiopathic: In most of the cases no cause is known
2.Madelung’s disease
3.Familial multiple subcutaneous lipomatosis (FML)

Diagnosis

A lipoma is mobile, free from skin, painless, bosselated surface, firm to feel, well defined on examination. Its diagnosis can be confirmed with a simple test known as fine needle aspiration cytology {FNAC}. 

Treatment 

There is only one treatment for lipoma that is to get it excised. The surgery can be performed in 2 ways : 

1. Open surgery : An incision appropriate for the size of lipoma is given and lipoma is excised. Incision is closed with sutures which are removed after 7-10 days. 

2. Liposuction : With advances in cosmetic surgery techniques, Lipoma now can be removed with a 5mm incision. There are no stitches needed. You can resume your daily work from the next day. 

INDICATIONS

Lipoma removal surgery involves a small incision made to the skin above the fatty tissue. Dr. Volshteyn has developed a special technique to utilize the smallest incision possible, liquify the tumor, and gently suction out removing it completely.  In some cases, sutures are not necessary.  In others, minimal small sutures are placed to close the incision.  Surgery to remove a lipoma is typically very quick and can be done on an outpatient basis.

Lipoma surgery recovery time is minimal, and patients can go home the same day as their surgery. Usually, they can return to work and other daily activities the following day. For many individuals, the benefits of lipoma removal treatment far outweigh the small concern about scarring. Removal can mean relief from any pain associated with the fatty tumor and a return to the natural appearance of your skin with no lump to feel self-conscious about. The best way to reduce scarring is by having the tumor removed while it is still small.

PROCEDURE

After lipoma removal, follow Dr. Volshteyn’s post care instructions carefully and be reserved in your movements until the stitches have completely healed. Additionally, you can apply a scar cream to the area after it has healed. Over time the scar should fade or even disappear completely. Be sure to monitor the area of the lipoma for bleeding or other abnormalities.

As each case is unique to the individual, you may need more rest before exercising that area of the body.  Low activity is suggested for a period of time following the removal procedure, depending on the extent of the surgery.  It is acceptable for range of motion exercises during this time as per the surgeon’s guidelines.  If the lipoma was deep and muscles had to be cut, you may require physical therapy to achieve proper healing.

If exercise or any other type of movement causes pain, be sure to stop what you are doing and call our office right away. If you suffer from one lipoma or multiple lipomas, there are options available for treatment. At Atlantic Surgical Associates in West Long Branch, we are experts at removing these fatty tumors with minimal scarring. Call our office today and schedule a comprehensive evaluation to learn more about healing after lipoma removal.

A1 Lipoma removal may cause a permanent scar.

Q2 Can you get rid of lipomas without surgery?
A2 No treatment is usually necessary for a lipoma. However, if the lipoma bothers you, is painful or is growing, your doctor might recommend that it be removed. Lipoma treatments include: Surgical removal.

Sex change surgery

Gender reassignment, sex reassignment, or sex change surgery is performed for people with gender dysphoria to transition to their desired gender. Gender dysphoria is a condition wherein the person feels that they are born with the wrong gender and that they identify themselves more as the other gender. A gender affirmation surgery performed on such people is of three different types. The past reports of sex change surgery have observed that these surgeries improve the mental health and quality of life of the concerned person.

The sex change surgery is of different types depending upon the procedures performed during the surgery. The main types of sex change surgery are:

Facial reconstructive surgery

As the name suggests, this procedure changes your facial features so that your face looks more feminine or masculine depending on the gender to which you are transforming. 

Top surgery

This surgery refers to the procedures done on the upper part of your body. In a sex change surgery from male to female, the breast size and shape are enhanced to give it a more enhanced appearance whereas, in the case of a female to male sex change surgery, the breast tissues are removed from the chest to give it a masculine appearance.

Bottom surgery

This involves reconstruction of the genitals depending upon the sex in which the patient is getting transformed.

Why is sex change surgery done? A sex change surgery is generally done for people who want to change their gender. However, the surgery is not performed on all people. The individuals who can go for a sex change surgery include:
  • People who are identified as transgenders
  • People who are mentally healthy. A mental health evaluation test determines the mental health of an individual.
  • People who are diagnosed with gender dysphoria or gender identity disorder

What are the procedures involved in sex change surgery?

The procedures involved in a sex change surgery vary depending upon the gender you are transforming into. 

Male to female sex change surgery

There are several steps involved in a male-to-female sex change surgery. These steps include:

  • A psychiatrist first consults the patient to confirm whether or not they are suffering from gender dysphoria.
  • Consultation is also done to know whether or not they are mentally and physically fit to undergo surgery.
  • The patient is  prescribed hormonal therapy for 6 months before they go for surgery.
  • After complete consultation and hormone therapy, the patient is taken for surgery. The procedures involved in the surgery include:
    • Insertion of breast implant to enhance the size and shape of your breasts
    • Bottom surgery involves the removal of the penis (penectomy) and the construction of the vagina (vaginoplasty).
    • The hairline is lowered down to shorten the forehead
    • The nose is reconstructed to make it look more feminine (rhinoplasty)
    • Cheek implants are used to make your cheek look fuller.
    • The upper lip is lifted while the lower lip is reduced in size.
    • Reduction of the Adam's apple
    • Full body laser treatment is done to reduce hair.
    • Liposuction can be done at the waist to make it look slim.
    • Fat is injected into your buttocks (Brazilian buttock lift).

Female to male sex change surgery

The psychiatrist consultation and hormone therapy remain the same as in male-to-female sex change surgery. However, the procedures performed during the surgery are modified to give a masculine figure. The various procedures involved in this surgery are:

  • Top surgery involves mastectomy, that is, the breast tissues are removed.
  • Hysterectomy with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy is done for the removal of the uterus along with both the ovaries and the fallopian tube.
  • Vaginectomy is the removal of the vagina.
  • Reconstruction of the penis, known as penile reconstruction or metoidioplasty
  • Testicular implants are used, and the scrotum is constructed (Scrotoplasty)
  • Laser hair reduction treatment is done to widen your forehead

What does the recovery period look like post-surgery?

The recovery period after a sex change surgery depends on the procedures that you have gone through during the surgery and the corrections made in different parts of your body. Some of the common effects that you might see after a sex change surgery include:

  • Swelling in the cheek and the nose area may last for 2-4 weeks after the surgery.
  • You may also observe swelling in the chin and jaw area, which goes away in around 2 weeks.
  • There might be swelling and soreness in the chest area for 1-2 weeks after the surgery.
  • You must avoid vigorous exercise for at least one month or six weeks after the surgery.
  • Weekly follow-up with your doctor is essentially important.
  • Regular sessions with a counsellor are equally important as it can support your mental health and social transition.

What are the risks involved in sex change surgery?

Sex change surgery is a combination of procedures and is thus a massive surgery. The hormonal therapy given before the sex change surgery has several risks. The few complications that may arise due to the hormonal therapy include:

  • Sleep apnea,
  • High blood pressure,
  • A tumour affecting your pituitary gland may develop,
  • Increased chances of heart disease,
  • Uncontrolled weight gain,
  • Infertility,
  • Occurrence of blood clots,
  • Increased levels of liver enzyme,
  • Feeling of confusion, uncertainty, and anxiety.

The chances of these complications can be reduced by regular counselling sessions and continuous consultation from the endocrinologist.

What does the sex change surgery cost?

The sex change surgery cost in Delhi varies depending upon the surgical procedures included in the treatment. The cost also depends on the skill and experience of your performing doctor and the medical centre.

Bliniq is a leading cosmetic surgery clinic and has a team of experienced and skilled cosmetic surgeons. At BLINIQ, we aim to provide you with the best doctor for sex change surgery. We also ensure that we take complete care of our patients by providing them with facilities like free pickup and drop facilities, unlimited follow-ups, easy EMI options to support their treatment, and luxurious amenities.

Introduction

Six-packs abs is a dream of every guy who has always imagined to be fit. But getting them is not an easy task. It involves sticking to a regular regimen of exercise in the gym for unimaginable amount of years combined with a tight control on dietary habits. Even after following all this, some still fail to achieve and flaunt that chiselled look of six pack abs.
We have a way out now and that is abdominal etching/six pack abs creation through highly advanced and skilled usage of liposuction cannulas to suck out that resistant fat around your abdominal muscles.

PROCEDURE

The procedure involves three steps:

1. Markings: Your muscles are felt over your abdomen and the sites of indentations are marked where your abs are to be created. The markings are confimed with you

2. Details of the procedure: The procedure is performed under general anaesthesia and lasts for 2 to 3 hours.
a. Infiltration: Tumescent anaesthesia is infiltrated under your skin, which reduces the blood supply of the area and makes it numb.
b. VASER: The fat cells are emulsified with help of ultrasonic waves.
c. Liposuction: The marked areas and area below your umbilicus is suctioned to create those six-pack abs.

3. Recovery:

a. You are discharged on the same day after the procedure and called back the next day for a change in dressing.
b. There will be mild discomfort for initial 4 days and you will be given pain medications and antibiotics to cover that period.
c. You will experience some bruising and areas of altered sensations in your skin which will return to normal in 3 weeks and 3 months of time respectively.
d. You can do your household chores from the 2nd day and join back work on the 5th day.

FAQ

Q1 Can you get plastic surgery for a six pack?
A1 Abdominal etching is done with a special tumescent liposuction technique. The procedure removes and sculpts fatty tissue residing under the skin. Before the procedure, detailed markings are made of the patient's flexed abs. During the surgery, small incisions are made within the natural contours of the abdomen.

Q2 How long does it take to get a 6 pack abs?
A2 The American Council on Exercise says a 1 percent body fat loss per month is safe and achievable. Given that math, it could take a woman with average body fat about 20 to 26 months to achieve the appropriate amount of fat loss for six-pack abs. The average man would need about 15 to 21 months.

Q3 How do I flatten my stomach after abdominal surgery?
A3 Do crunches while reclining on an exercise ball or perform leg lifts. Or simply hold a resistance band wrapped around the arches of your feet to isolate and tone the muscles below your waistline. Exercise and build your abdominal muscles with strength training, but only when your doctor says it is safe to do so.

Understanding the basics

Skin tags are painless, noncancerous growths on the skin. They’re connected to the skin by a small, thin stalk called a peduncle. Skin tags are common in both men and women, especially after age 50. They can appear anywhere on your body, though they’re commonly found in places where your skin folds such as the:

  1. armpits
  2. groin
  3. thighs
  4. eyelids
  5. neck
  6. area under your breasts

PROCEDURE

Tiny skin tags may rub off on their own. Most skin tags stay attached to your skin. In general, skin tags don’t require treatment. If skin tags hurt or bother you, you may opt to have them removed.

Your doctor may remove your skin tags by:

  1. Cryotherapy: Freezing the skin tag with liquid nitrogen.
  2. Surgical removal: Removing the skin tag with scissors or a scalpel.
  3. Electrosurgery: Burning off the skin tag with high-frequency electrical energy.
  4. Ligation: Removing the skin tag by tying it off with surgical thread in order to cut off its blood flow.

Having small skin tags removed doesn’t usually require anesthesia. Your doctor may use local anesthesia when removing large or multiple skin tags.

You can also try natural remedies to remove skin tags. These include tea tree oil, apple cider vinegar, and lemon juice. Keep in mind that there’s no scientific evidence to support these remedies.

It’s not a good idea to try to remove skin tags on your own. Many websites offer DIY instructions for removing skin tags by tying them off with string or applying a chemical peel. Even in a sterile environment, removing skin tags may cause bleeding, burns, and infection. It’s best to let your doctor handle the job.

How to identify a skin tag

The main way to identify a skin tag is by the peduncle. Unlike moles and some other skin growths, skin tags hang off the skin by this small stalk.

Most skin tags are tiny, typically smaller than 2 millimeters in size. Some can grow as large as several centimeters. Skin tags are soft to the touch. They may be smooth and round, or they may be wrinkly and asymmetrical. Some skin tags are threadlike and resemble grains of rice.

Skin tags may be flesh-colored. They can also be darker than the surrounding skin due to hyperpigmentation. If a skin tag becomes twisted, it may turn black due to a lack of blood flow.

What causes skin tags?

It’s unclear exactly what causes skin tags. Since they usually show up in skin folds, friction may play a role. Skin tags are made up of blood vessels and collagen surrounded by an outer layer of skin.

According to a 2008 study, the human papillomavirus (HPV) may be a factor in the development of skin tags. The study analyzed 37 skin tags from various sites of the body. Results showed HPV DNA in almost 50 percent of the skin tags examined.

Insulin resistance, which may lead to type 2 diabetes and prediabetes, may also play a role in the development of skin tags. People with insulin resistance don’t absorb glucose effectively from the bloodstream. According to a 2010 study, the presence of multiple skin tags was associated with insulin resistance, a high body mass index, and high triglycerides.

Skin tags are also a common side effect of pregnancy. This may be due to pregnancy hormones and weight gain. In rare cases, multiple skin tags can be a sign of a hormone imbalance or an endocrine problem.

Skin tags aren’t contagious. There may be a genetic connection. It isn’t unusual for multiple family members to have them.

Risk factors to consider

You may be at greater risk of getting skin tags if you:

  1. are overweight
  2. are pregnant
  3. have family members who have skin tags
  4. have insulin resistance or type 2 diabetes
  5. have HPV

Skin tags don’t become skin cancer. Irritation may occur if they rub with clothing, jewelry, or other skin.

Shave with caution around skin tags. Shaving off a skin tag won’t cause permanent damage, though it may cause pain and prolonged bleeding.

When to see a doctor

Other skin conditions such as warts and moles can resemble skin tags. Since some moles may be cancerous, it’s best to have your skin tags examined by a doctor. Your dermatologist or family doctor will be able to diagnose skin tags. They’ll likely do this through a visual exam. If they have any doubt about the diagnosis, they may also perform a biopsy.

If you develop a skin tag, it may not be cause for concern. For most people, skin tags are just a nuisance. If they don’t bother you, and you’re sure of the diagnosis, you can leave them alone. Keep in mind that where you have one skin tag, more may appear.

Some skin tags are stubborn. You may need more than one treatment to get rid of them. If a skin tag is frozen or ligated, it can take a few weeks for it to fall off. In some cases, skin tags will regrow and need to be removed again.

If you’re overweight, losing weight won’t make your existing skin tags go away. It may help reduce your risk of developing more.

If you have a skin growth that bleeds, itches, or changes color, contact your doctor immediately. They’ll need to rule out a serious condition such as skin cancer.

FAQ

Q1 Can you safely remove skin tags yourself?
A1 If your skin tag is small with a narrow base, your GP may suggest that you try to remove it yourself. For example, they may suggest tying off the base of the skin tag with dental floss or cotton to cut off its blood supply and make it drop off (ligation

Q2 What happens if you accidentally shave off a skin tag?
A2 There is typically no harm done when small skin tags are removed by shaving. Sometimes, even a small skin tag base may bleed for a while and require constant applied pressure for 10-15 minutes to stop bleeding. Skin infection is a rare possible complication of accidentally shaving off skin tags.

Q3 Does toothpaste remove skin tags?
A3 People use toothpaste for all sorts of health-related purposes, from shrinking pimples to treating bug bites. There's no scientific evidence, however, that toothpaste effectively or safely removes skin tags. The American Academy of Dermatology recommends that you consult a physician to have a skin tag removed

What Are Skin Tags (Acrochordons)?

Skin tags are soft, harmless growths of skin. They are not cancer. These growths are made of collagen fibers and blood vessels. They are not a health risk. But they can be a concern for your looks. They can also get sore if they rub on clothes or jewelry.

Common Causes and Areas of Concern

What are the types of Liposuction Surgery in Delhi?

Various liposuction techniques are currently available. These techniques include:

  • Tumescent liposuction

It is the most popular liposuction technique wherein saline solution with a vessel constrictor and local anaesthetic is injected under the skin of the targeted area, followed by the suction of fat through small tubes.

  • Ultrasound-assisted liposuction (UAL)

In this method, ultrasound waves are used to melt the fat by breaking the wall of the fat cells and liquifying it. This technique is often followed by suction-assisted liposuction to remove the liquified fat. The technique is more commonly used in fibrous areas (like back and male breasts) and in parts where liposuction has already been done.

  • Power-assisted liposuction (PAL)

This technique uses more power by using a specialized cannula that moves back and forth rapidly. This enables the surgeon to suck out more fat easily.

  • Laser-assisted lipolysis (LAL)

This technique uses a laser delivered into the fat present under your skin through a small incision to heat the fat and remove it from under your skin.

Thigh lift surgery in Delhi costs between Rs. 1 lakh and Rs. 2.5 lakh, with the final figure shaped by the extent and location of skin laxity, whether the inner thigh, outer thigh, or both are addressed, and whether liposuction is incorporated in the same session. Thighplasty removes excess skin from the upper thigh and reshapes the surrounding tissue to create a smoother, firmer leg contour. The concern it addresses is one that many patients describe as both visible and physical: loose inner thigh skin causes friction during walking, limits clothing choices, and cannot be improved through exercise alone, regardless of fitness level. For patients who have worked through significant weight loss or postpartum body changes and find the thigh skin has simply not kept pace, a thigh lift addresses what lifestyle alone cannot.

Why Exercise Does Not Fix Loose Inner Thigh Skin

Understanding this is the starting point for anyone researching thighplasty. Exercise develops and strengthens the muscle layers beneath the skin. Squats, lunges, adductor exercises, and resistance training all build the underlying musculature and improve thigh firmness when the muscles are engaged. What exercise cannot do is restore skin elasticity or retract skin that has already lost its collagen structure. Once skin has been stretched significantly, whether through major weight change or pregnancy, the structural proteins that give skin its snap and resilience are depleted and do not regenerate on their own. No amount of targeted exercise reverses that process.

When Liposuction Alone Is Not the Answer

Liposuction of the inner thigh removes fat but does not address skin. For patients who have good skin elasticity and primarily excess fat, liposuction alone may produce a satisfactory result. For patients with loose or hanging skin, liposuction without skin removal can worsen the appearance by removing the volume that was at least partially supporting the skin from beneath. In those cases, removing the excess skin surgically is the approach that produces genuine improvement.

Takeaway: If your inner thigh skin does not spring back when you pinch and release it, liposuction alone is unlikely to give you the result you are looking for.

What Does Thigh Lift Surgery Cost in Delhi?

The Rs. 1 lakh to Rs. 2.5 lakh range reflects genuine variation in what different patients require. A limited medial thigh lift confined to the upper inner thigh, with a short incision in the groin crease, sits at the lower end of the range. An extended correction addressing the full inner thigh from groin toward the knee, or a lateral lift addressing the outer thigh and hip, involves a longer incision, more surgical time, and a correspondingly higher cost. When liposuction is added in the same session to address residual fat alongside the skin excess, this also affects the total. At Bliniq, the cost confirmed at consultation covers the surgeon's fee, anaesthesia, operating theatre charges, the compression garment, and post-operative follow-up visits, all listed in a written breakdown before any decision is made.

Takeaway: Ask your surgeon clearly at the consultation whether the quote covers only the skin excision or includes liposuction, so there are no surprises in the final figure.

How Your Thigh Lift Is Planned at Bliniq

Thighplasty planning begins with a detailed physical assessment in which the surgeon evaluates the location and severity of skin laxity, the distribution of fat in the inner and outer thigh, skin quality and elasticity in the affected area, and the natural position of the groin crease where the primary scar will be placed. This assessment is what determines which approach is recommended.

The Importance of Scar Placement Planning

Incision placement is one of the most consequential decisions in thighplasty because the resulting scar is permanent. For a medial lift, the incision is placed in the groin crease, extending along the inner thigh for more extensive corrections. For a lateral lift, the incision runs along the outer hip. The goal in both cases is to position the scar so that it remains within the coverage area of standard underwear and swimwear after healing. Your surgeon will show you the planned incision markings on your skin during your pre-operative appointment before the day of surgery.

Takeaway: Bring fitted underwear or swimwear to your pre-operative appointment so your surgeon can mark incision lines with their coverage in mind.

A Week-by-Week Guide to Thigh Lift Recovery

Days one to three: Expect swelling, bruising, and tightness in the thigh region. Gentle, short walks are encouraged from the first day to support circulation. Avoid positions that place tension across the inner thigh incision. You will go home wearing your compression garment.

Days four to fourteen: Most patients feel comfortable resuming light activity and desk-based work by the end of the second week. The compression garment is worn continuously. Swelling peaks in the first ten days and then begins to reduce gradually.

Weeks three to six: Swelling reduces progressively. The compression garment continues and may transition to intermittent use under your surgeon's guidance. Tightness in the thigh softens as the healing tissue relaxes.

Months two to six: The thigh contour becomes increasingly clear as residual swelling resolves. Scars are still maturing and may be raised or pink. Silicone scar management begins once the incision is fully closed.

Patients who would like to support skin quality in the treated area during recovery may find that Mesotherapy is a useful complement at an appropriate point in the healing timeline, supporting skin hydration and surface texture in the thigh region. Timing must always be discussed with your surgeon before scheduling.

Who Seeks Thigh Lift Surgery at Bliniq in Delhi?

The most common patient group is women who have experienced significant weight loss, whether through sustained lifestyle change or bariatric surgery, and find that the inner thigh skin has not retracted in the way they hoped. Post-pregnancy changes in skin structure also bring some patients to this procedure, particularly where the thighs were significantly affected by the weight fluctuation of pregnancy.

Thigh lift surgery is also sought by transgender women as part of a body feminisation plan, particularly to achieve a smoother inner thigh profile as part of a broader gender-affirming physical transformation. For patients who are exploring a comprehensive approach to gender-affirming surgical care, Bliniq also provides sex change surgery alongside its range of body contouring services.

Patients who are also considering Laser Hair Removal in the thigh area should plan this either before their thigh lift or at least six months after surgery, since laser treatment is not safe on skin that is still actively healing from a surgical procedure.

What Long-Term Results Can You Expect?

The skin removed during thighplasty does not return. The improvement in contour is long-lasting when weight remains stable. Scars progress from their initial red or pink state to a softer, paler line over eighteen to twenty-four months, and in their final healed form are hidden by underwear and swimwear. The practical changes described by most patients centre on the freedom to wear different clothes, the elimination of chafing during daily movement, and a noticeably improved sense of comfort in their body.

Takeaway: Thigh lift surgery gives you the outcome that years of exercise were working toward but could not deliver on their own, and the recovery commitment reflects a genuinely lasting change.

Frequently Asked Questions

What does thigh lift surgery cost in Delhi in 2026?

Thigh lift surgery in Delhi costs between Rs. 1 lakh and Rs. 2.5 lakh, depending on the areas treated, the extent of skin correction, and whether liposuction is included. At Bliniq, the final cost is confirmed after your in-person surgical assessment.

Where exactly are the scars placed after a thigh lift?

For a medial thigh lift, the scar sits in the groin crease and extends along the inner thigh for more extensive corrections. For a lateral lift, the scar runs along the outer hip. All incisions are planned to fall within the coverage of standard underwear and swimwear in their healed position.

How is a thigh lift different from liposuction of the thighs?

Liposuction removes fat but does not address loose skin. A thigh lift removes the excess skin itself and re-drapes the remaining tissue to create a firmer contour. In patients with significant skin laxity, liposuction alone may worsen the appearance rather than improve it.

When can I resume exercise after thigh lift surgery?

Gentle walking is encouraged from the first day after surgery. Moderate activity resumes at four to six weeks. Full exercise involving the thigh muscles is typically cleared at six to eight weeks, confirmed at your post-operative follow-up.

Can a thigh lift be combined with a tummy tuck or other procedures?

Tummy tuck surgery

Do sit-ups not give you the flat stomach you crave? You might be thinking about getting a "tummy tuck," also known as an "abdominoplasty," if you have too much flab or extra skin in your abdomen that just doesn't react to diet or physical workout.

It is a surgical procedure that improves body shape by flattening the stomach. It assists you in getting rid of extra loose skin accumulated on your stomach due to aging, childbirth, weight loss, or simply having many loose muscles and skin. A flat belly, tightened muscles, the elimination of stretch marks, and an improved body shape are the ultimate effects of tummy tuck surgery.

Different Types of Tummy Tucks Surgery

Different people choose to have tummy tuck surgery for various reasons. Depending on the patient's needs, the surgeon will choose the type of tummy tuck. With minimal scarring, the treatment results in the desired natural-looking results. There are several different kinds of tummy tucks:

  • Mini Tummy Tuck: In this procedure, only the lower belly is improved through less extensive surgery. This technique is the ideal choice to cure loose skin and stretch marks on the lower tummy. During this treatment, a single incision is made right above the pubic region, which causes the tightening of lax muscles and the removal of extra skin, resulting in a smooth and flat abdominal wall. The scar from this surgery resembles a thin horizontal line. It leads to flatter, slimmer lower abdomen. It is a quick and short procedure with Less recovery time.
  • Classic or Full Tummy Tuck: The abdomen's upper and lower regions are also strengthened by this technique. For those with extra skin folds or plumpness, this approach works well. The optimum outcome is obtained with an extended tummy tuck, which addresses the entire abdominal wall. The upper portion of the pubic mound is sliced  horizontally during this surgery to remove extra skin and tighten the abdominal muscles. For results that look natural, a second cut can be made to relocate belly button. The scar from this treatment resembles one from a mini-tummy tuck. However, it might extend further from the hip to the hip.
  • Extended Tummy Tuck: It works to improve the upper and lower abdominal flanks. The ideal candidates for this surgery are those with extra sagging skin from excessive weight reduction and women with less elastic abdominal muscles from previous pregnancies. Long incisions are made during this treatment to tighten the abdominal wall and remove extra, drooping skin from the upper and lower abdomen, flanks, and back.

INTRODUCTION

A lip lift is an in-office surgical procedure that shortens the space between the nose and the top of the lip, known as the “philtrum.” The procedure increases the amount of pink tissue that's visible
which makes the lips look fuller and more pronounced.

LIP LIFTING: MARKING MATTERS

The actual lip lift procedure takes about 30 minutes and can be performed with local anesthesia,
although I usually operate with IV or general anesthesia. The crux of this procedure is to remove
excessive upper lip skin just beneath the nose. As the incision is larger in the center and tapers out
for blending on the lateral edges, a specific geometric pattern is used. This pattern resembles a
“bullhorn,” “Angel Wing,” or “Mustache,” and the procedure is sometimes named after one of
these.
It is very important to draw this outline with a marking pen that will not fade with surgical prep, etc.,
as it will serve as the precise template for excision. It is also important to mark the pattern before
any anesthesia and with the patient in the upright position.
I have seen some surgeons use a simple elliptical incision for this procedure, which does not heal as
well as the precise geometric incision. The preferred incision corresponds to the lower nasal
anatomy and gently tapers out to the lateral nostril. It is also important to make the incision just at
the nasal sill and not extend into or too far above or below the nostrils. Again, marking is everything
in this procedure.
The maximum amount of skin resection is commensurate with the length of the lip. In general, I
usually remove 5-8mm of skin, which can represent up to one-third of the total lip length as
measured from philtrim to vermilion (Figure 1). Novice surgeons should remain conservative in
determining how much skin to remove.

PROCEDURE

A small amount of local anesthesia is injected to provide hemostasis, but not to excessively distort
the tissues. Using a #11 blade, the intricate pattern is incised then removed. Although some
surgeons remove skin and orbicularis muscle, I generally do not remove muscle, as I have seen
abnormal animation occur in excessively operated patients from other surgeons.
A full thickness skin excision is performed using the fine tip of the #11 blade (Figure 2). This is an
adjustable procedure as at this point, the surgeon can estimate the amount of incision show and
remove more skin if desired.
After this skin is excised, hemostasis is performed with microbipolar cautery, as the upper lip is a
vascular area. Next, subcutaneous sutures are placed using 5-0 gut suture (Figure 3). This is
important to bolster the strength of the repair, as this area is subject to a lot of animation. These
subcutaneous sutures also help align the precise pattern between the proximal and distal skin
margins. Final closure is performed with alternating 5-0 and 6-0 gut sutures or the surgeon's choice  Sutures are left in place about one week. Patients are asked to refrain from significant animation for the first week and to avoid foods that would aggravate the area or require excessive lip movement.

INTRODUCTION

When a woman gives birth or ages, her body undergoes a lot of changes. A vaginal muscle loosening is one such change. Many women opt to undergo surgery to reverse this process either for cosmetic or reconstructive purposes. The two types of genital surgeries performed on a woman for vaginal tightening purposes are vaginoplasty and labiaplasty. In addition, there are few other types of reconstructive and cosmetic vaginal surgeries that are available.

Some cosmetic surgeries includes,

  1. Genital Hymenoplasty
  2. Clitoris unhooding
  3. G-spot amplification.

For further information on vaginoplasty or labiaplasty as vaginal tightening procedures, consult the best doctors in India at Columbia Asia Hospital. We have a number of professional doctors who will guide you through the process of understanding and undergoing these procedures.

What’s the difference between Vaginoplasty & Labiaplasty?

  1. Vaginoplasty is a procedure that involves surgically tightening the entire vagina from loosening post pregnancy or due to age.
  2. Labiaplasty on the other hand is performed separately or in combination with vaginoplasty. It involves surgery on the labia of the vagina alone. The labia, consists of the labia major or labia minor (the outer and inner lips of the vagina). This surgery helps make the lips symmetrical by changing the size or shape of the labia.

INDICATIONS

There are a number of reasons why women opt to undergo vaginal tightening surgeries. It is important to recognize whether these purposes are reconstructive or cosmetic in nature.

Reconstructive surgery is done with the purpose of improving a bodily function for a certain area. For example, birth defects in a baby girl can require reconstructive vaginoplasty or labiaplasty. This is so that she is able to have normal function of the urinary system, menstruation cycles, sexual pleasure and birthing as she grows.

Other functional issues that require reconstructive surgery include urinary incontinence and bladder control problems. This issue may cause an overactive bladder and releases urine when one laughs or coughs. It can also cause muscle spasms in the urinary system. Vaginal tightening through vaginoplasty or labiaplasty helps reconstruct the area to function correctly.

PROCEDURE

A cosmetic vaginal surgery on the other hand, is performed to alter a normal functional vagina. A lot of women in India might want to opt for a genital-hymenoplasty for example, due to societal pressures of being a virgin prior to being married. It is important you have open discussions and consultations with your doctors to ensure that you are making an informed decision.

Types of Cosmetic Vaginal Surgeries

  1. Genital-Hymenoplasty: This procedure is done to restore the hymen to its original state. The hymen is a tissue that can tear either due to strenuous physical activity, use of tampons or sexual intercourse.
  2. G-spot Amplification: Collagen in injected into the vaginal walls to increase sexual pleasure and stimulation in this procedure.

Vaginal Tightening, Vaginoplasty & Labiaplasty Risks

When undergoing a surgery on such a sensitive area, one must get all information on risks and benefits. Known risks of vaginal cosmetic or reconstructive procedures are infections, scarring, pain and discomfort, and changes in sensitivity.

A3 Why does my girlfriend feel loose sometimes?
A3 Women's vaginas are less elastic when they are not sexually aroused. They become more elastic — “looser” — the more sexually excited they become. A woman may feel “tighter” to a man when she is less aroused, less comfortable, and having less pleasure than her partner.

If you have been researching VASER liposuction in India, you have probably found a wide range of prices that seem impossible to directly compare. One clinic quotes Rs. 50,000. Another quotes Rs. 3,00,000. Both claim to offer VASER. This guide exists to explain exactly what drives that price difference, what a fair and complete cost estimate for VASER liposuction in India actually looks like, and what you should be asking before you book a consultation.

First: What Is VASER and Why Does It Cost More Than Standard Liposuction?

VASER stands for Vibration Amplification of Sound Energy at Resonance. It is an ultrasound-assisted liposuction technology that uses sound energy to emulsify fat cells before they are suctioned out. This is fundamentally different from traditional liposuction, which physically scrapes and breaks fat using mechanical force through a cannula.

The difference matters clinically for three reasons:

  1. Less trauma to surrounding tissue. VASER is selective in its action. It targets fat cells while largely preserving blood vessels, nerves, and connective tissue. Traditional liposuction is less selective, which is why it is associated with more bruising, bleeding, and post-operative swelling.
  2. Better skin retraction. The heat generated by ultrasound during VASER stimulates the skin's deep connective tissue, encouraging it to tighten around the new contour rather than hanging loosely. This is especially valuable in areas like the abdomen, inner arms, and back, where skin laxity is common alongside fat deposits.
  3. High-definition sculpting capability. VASER allows surgeons to work in the superficial fat layer, close to the skin, to create muscle definition and contour detail that traditional liposuction cannot safely achieve. This is the technology behind six-pack ab creation, athletic chest sculpting, and other high-definition body contouring procedures.

VASER costs more because the equipment itself is expensive, requires trained operators, and demands a higher level of surgical skill to use correctly.

VASER Liposuction Cost in India: Area-by-Area Breakdown

Cost is primarily determined by how many body zones are treated and the volume of fat involved. Below is a transparent reference guide.

Body Zone

VASER Cost Range (India)

Single small zone (chin, gynecomastia, arms)

Rs. 50,000 to Rs. 1,00,000

Single medium zone (abdomen, flanks, thighs)

Rs. 90,000 to Rs. 1,50,000

Two zones combined

Rs. 1,20,000 to Rs. 2,00,000

Three zones combined

Rs. 1,50,000 to Rs. 2,50,000

Full body or 360-degree sculpting

Rs. 2,00,000 to Rs. 3,50,000

VASER 4D (high-definition with muscle definition)

Rs. 1,50,000 to Rs. 4,00,000+

Traditional liposuction in India, for comparison, typically costs Rs. 30,000 to Rs. 1,00,000 for a single zone. The premium for VASER reflects both technology and expected outcome quality.

What Your Quote Should Include: A Checklist

Many patients receive a low headline quote that excludes several high costs. Before accepting any quote for VASER liposuction, confirm whether the following are included:

  • Surgeon's fee
  • Anesthesiologist's fee (a separate specialist, not a technician)
  • Operating theatre charges for the duration of your procedure
  • Tumescent fluid and consumables used during surgery
  • Compression garments (you will need at least one, sometimes two)
  • Pre-operative blood tests and ECG
  • Post-operative medication
  • Follow-up consultations (how many, and over what period)

A quote that excludes anaesthesia, garments, or follow-up visits will appear lower but cost more in total. Always request an itemised breakdown before comparison.

What Drives the Cost Up in Specific Cases?

Several factors increase the final cost beyond the base zone price:

Volume of fat. India's recommended safe limit for fat removal in a single session is 3 to 5 litres. Patients requiring high-volume removal across multiple zones need a longer surgical session, which increases operating theatre time and anesthesiologist charges.

Fibrous fat. Fat in the back, male chest (gynecomastia), and flanks is significantly denser and more fibrous than abdominal fat. VASER is particularly well-suited for fibrous fat, but it takes longer to emulsify, adding to surgical time.

VASER 4D. High-definition sculpting to create visible muscle definition requires greater precision, a longer operative session, and a surgeon with specialised experience in body sculpting. This commands a premium over standard VASER.

Combination procedures. Patients who combine VASER with a tummy tuck, gynecomastia correction, or body lift increase both the complexity and the total cost of the session.

City-by-City Cost Variation in India

VASER liposuction is available across major Indian cities, but costs vary.

  • Delhi and NCR: Rs. 90,000 to Rs. 3,50,000 depending on zone and technique
  • Mumbai: Slightly higher than Delhi, given real estate and operating costs
  • Bangalore and Chennai: Broadly comparable to Delhi
  • Tier 2 cities: Lower base cost but limited access to VASER-specific equipment and surgeons with high case volumes

Delhi is among the most competitive markets for VASER in India, offering access to experienced surgeons with significant body contouring caseloads at costs significantly below Western equivalents, where similar procedures range from USD 4,000 to USD 12,000.

Is VASER Worth the Extra Cost Over Traditional Liposuction?

This is the question every patient asks. The honest answer depends on what you are trying to achieve.

VASER is the better choice if:

  • You are targeting areas with fibrous fat (back, male chest, flanks)
  • You want high-definition definition or muscle sculpting
  • You have moderate skin laxity and want the best chance of skin retraction without a surgical lift
  • You are having large-volume fat removal and want reduced bruising and faster recovery

Standard liposuction may be adequate if:

  • You have a single small area of soft, non-fibrous fat
  • Your primary goal is volume reduction rather than precise definition
  • Budget is a significant constraint

At Bliniq, the choice between VASER and other techniques is made by the surgeon after assessing your specific anatomy, not by default. For patients also considering non-surgical skin improvement alongside their liposuction plan, our laser hair removal and skin treatment services are available at the same facility.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is VASER liposuction permanent?

The fat cells removed by VASER do not regenerate. With stable body weight, the result is long-lasting. Significant weight gain after surgery causes remaining fat cells in untreated areas to expand.

How long is the recovery after VASER?

Most patients return to desk work within three to five days. Strenuous exercise is resumed at four to six weeks. Final results, including full resolution of swelling, are visible at three to six months.

Is VASER safe for all body types?

VASER is safe for most adult patients in good health who are near their stable body weight and have realistic expectations. Patients with very loose skin may need a combined procedure to achieve their goals.

Can VASER be done under local anaesthesia?

Yes. Smaller zones or targeted procedures can be performed under local anaesthesia with sedation. Larger or multi-zone sessions typically require general anaesthesia.

Does VASER hurt more than traditional liposuction?

No. Because VASER causes less tissue trauma, most patients report less post-operative soreness than they expect. Recovery is generally smoother than traditional liposuction.

VASER liposuction in Delhi costs between Rs. 80,000 and Rs. 2.5 lakh per treated area, depending on the size and number of areas addressed, the surgeon's experience, and the facility. VASER stands for Vibration Amplification of Sound Energy at Resonance, which is a way of saying it uses ultrasound energy to break up fat cells before removing them, making the process more precise and less traumatic to surrounding tissue than traditional liposuction. In 2026, VASER liposuction has become a preferred technique at credentialed Delhi clinics for patients who want not just fat removal but also improved body definition and skin contraction in the treated area.

What Makes VASER Liposuction Different from Traditional Liposuction?

Traditional liposuction uses mechanical force to dislodge and suction fat cells. VASER liposuction adds an ultrasound step first. A small probe emitting ultrasound energy is inserted through a tiny incision, gently liquefying the fat cells while largely leaving the surrounding nerves, blood vessels, and connective tissue intact. This selective disruption is what makes VASER a more precise option, particularly for areas where detail and definition matter, such as the abdomen, flanks, arms, and male chest.

Why Does This Precision Matter?

Because VASER disrupts fat cells more gently, there is typically less bruising and swelling after surgery compared to traditional lipo. The fat cells removed by VASER are also more intact and viable, which is why VASER-harvested fat is specifically preferred when the goal is fat transfer to another area, such as in a Brazilian Butt Lift. The skin in the treated area also tends to contract and retract more smoothly because the ultrasound energy stimulates the overlying skin to tighten rather than leaving it loose after fat removal.

What Does VASER Liposuction Cost in Delhi in 2026?

The cost of VASER liposuction in Delhi ranges from Rs. 80,000 to Rs. 2.5 lakh per area treated. Treating multiple areas in a single session increases the total cost but is generally more efficient than staging separate procedures. Common treatment areas include the abdomen, flanks, love handles, inner and outer thighs, arms, back, and male chest. The final cost at Bliniq is confirmed at consultation after assessing the volume of fat to be removed, the number of areas, and the complexity of the contouring required.

What Is Included in the Cost?

The quoted cost at Bliniq covers the surgeon's fee, VASER probe usage and equipment, anaesthesia, operating theatre charges, and post-operative follow-up visits. Compression garments, which are essential after liposuction and worn for four to six weeks, may be listed separately. A written cost breakdown is provided before any booking is confirmed.

How Is VASER Liposuction Performed at Bliniq?

The procedure begins with tumescent fluid, a solution of saline, anaesthetic, and adrenaline, being infused into the treatment area. This fluid numbs the area, shrinks blood vessels to reduce bleeding, and makes the fat easier to remove. The VASER probe is then inserted to deliver ultrasound energy and emulsify the fat. Finally, a thin cannula removes the liquefied fat by suction. The procedure is performed under general or regional anaesthesia depending on the areas being treated and the extent of the planned work.

What Areas Can VASER Liposuction Treat?

VASER is particularly well-suited for areas requiring detail and definition: the abdomen and flanks for both men and women, the arms and inner thighs where skin quality matters, the back and bra rolls, the chin and neck for facial contouring, and the male chest. For male patients dealing specifically with excess glandular breast tissue, VASER liposuction is often used as part of Gynecomastia Treatment in Delhi at Bliniq, where removing the fat component precisely around the glandular tissue is key to achieving a flat, masculine chest contour.

What Is Recovery Like After VASER Liposuction?

Recovery from VASER liposuction is generally more comfortable than traditional liposuction, though it still requires proper rest and post-operative care. Most patients experience swelling, bruising, and mild soreness for one to two weeks. Compression garments are worn continuously for the first three to four weeks and then intermittently for a further two weeks. Patients can typically return to desk work within five to seven days and resume moderate exercise after three to four weeks.

Patients who are considering Laser Hair Removal in any of the treated areas should plan this either well before or several months after their VASER procedure, since the skin in treated areas needs adequate time to heal before it can safely undergo laser treatment.

Are You a Good Candidate for VASER Liposuction?

VASER liposuction is best suited to adults who are near their target weight with localised pockets of stubborn fat that do not respond to diet and exercise, have good skin elasticity that will allow proper retraction after fat removal, and are in good general health with no conditions affecting healing or anaesthesia tolerance. It is not a weight loss procedure and is not a substitute for lifestyle changes. Patients with poor skin laxity may be advised to consider combining liposuction with a skin-tightening procedure.

Takeaway: VASER liposuction delivers its best results when used for contouring and definition in patients who are already close to a healthy weight, not for removing large volumes of fat.

Frequently Asked Questions

What does VASER liposuction cost in Delhi?

VASER liposuction in Delhi costs between Rs. 80,000 and Rs. 2.5 lakh per area treated. Treating multiple areas in a single session will adjust the total cost, which is confirmed at your in-person consultation at Bliniq.

How is VASER liposuction different from regular liposuction?

VASER uses ultrasound energy to liquefy fat cells before removal, making the process more precise, less traumatic to surrounding tissue, and typically resulting in less bruising and smoother skin contraction compared to traditional mechanical liposuction.

How long does VASER liposuction recovery take?

Most patients return to light work within five to seven days. Compression garments are worn for four to six weeks. Final results are visible at three to six months as swelling fully resolves.

Is VASER liposuction suitable for the male chest?

Yes. VASER is commonly used for male chest contouring, both for fat reduction around the chest and as a precision tool in gynecomastia surgery, where the fat component around glandular tissue needs to be removed with care.

Can the fat removed by VASER be used for fat transfer?

Yes. VASER-harvested fat cells are more intact and viable than those removed by traditional liposuction, making them well-suited for fat transfer procedures such as a Brazilian Butt Lift or facial fat grafting.

INTRODUCTION

Electrolysis is the best and the only reliable method to permanently remove white hairs.  The electric current passes through the shaft of the hair and destroys the growing cells of the hair follicle to that the hair does not grow again. Electrolysis does not depend on the pigment of the hair (unlike lasers) and permanently removes of the hair irrespective of the colour of the hair (grey, blond or white) or the color of the skin (dark, tan, white or brown). It is the only FDA (Food and Drug Administration, USA) approved method to permanently remove the hairs (be it white or black) and has been used for the past 100 years with perfect efficacy and results.


The modern methods of hair removal using lasers all rely on pigment.  If a hair is white, the laser cannot interact with the hair follicle and will have no effect. Laser hair removal requires pigmentation in the hair follicle, thus laser hair removal is completely useless for white hairs (also blond and grey hairs) and also lasers are harmful on certain types of skins as well like dark skin, brown skin or tanned skin. Some laser experts try to fool you by saying that they will dye the hairs and use laser hair removal, but that is totally useless, as the hair is dyed only on the shaft and not in the root. And the part that is dyed is shaved off as in any laser hair removal procedure, the roots of the hairs still remain white, on which the laser does not act. The principle of working of all the laser machines is heat produced by absorption of the laser energy the dark melanin pigment. If the dark melanin pigment is absent as in white hairs or if the skin is dark as well then the laser light will not act on the hairs but will burn the skin. White hairs have no pigment, therefore does not get affected by a laser energy. Even if the hair is dyed, there is no effect.  

INDICATIONS

Laser hair removal is used to reduce unwanted hair. Common treatment locations include legs, armpits, upper lip, chin and the bikini line. However, it's possible to treat unwanted hair in nearly any area, except the eyelid or surrounding area. Skin with tattoos should not be treated either.

Hair color and skin type influence the success of laser hair removal. The basic principle is that the pigment of the hair, but not the pigment of the skin, should absorb the light. The laser should damage only the hair follicle while avoiding damage to the skin. Therefore, a contrast between hair and skin color — dark hair and light skin — results in the best outcomes.

The risk of damage to skin is greater when there is little contrast between hair and skin color, but advances in laser technology have made laser hair removal an option for people who have darker skin. Laser hair removal is less effective for hair colors that don't absorb light well: gray, red, blond and white. However, laser treatment options for light-colored hair continue to be developed

PROCEDURE

If you're interested in laser hair removal, choose a doctor who's board certified in a specialty such as dermatology or cosmetic surgery and has experience with laser hair removal on your skin type. If a physician assistant or licensed nurse will do the procedure, make sure a doctor supervises and is available on-site during the treatments. Be cautious about spas, salons or other facilities that allow nonmedical personnel to do laser hair removal.

Before laser hair removal, schedule a consultation with the doctor to determine if this is an appropriate treatment option for you. Your doctor will likely do the following:

  • Review your medical history, including medication use, history of skin disorders or scarring, and past hair removal procedures
  • Discuss risks, benefits and expectations, including what laser hair removal can and can't do for you
  • Take photos to be used for before-and-after assessments and long-term reviews

At the consultation, discuss a treatment plan and related costs. Laser hair removal is usually an out-of-pocket expense.

The doctor will also offer specific instructions to prepare for laser hair removal. These might include:

  • Staying out of the sun. Follow your doctor's advice for avoiding sun exposure before and after treatment. Whenever you go out, apply a broad-spectrum, SPF30 sunscreen.
  • Lightening your skin. Avoid any sunless skin creams that darken your skin. Your doctor might also prescribe a skin bleaching cream if you have a recent tan or darker skin.
  • Avoiding other hair removal methods. Plucking, waxing and electrolysis can disturb the hair follicle and should be avoided at least four weeks before treatment.
  • Avoiding blood-thinning medications. Ask your doctor about what medications, such as aspirin or anti-inflammatory drugs, to avoid before the procedure.
  • Shaving treatment area. Trimming and shaving is recommended the day before laser treatment. It removes hair above the skin that can result in surface skin damage from burnt hairs, but it leaves the hair shaft intact below the surface.
What you can expect

Laser hair removal usually requires two to six treatments. The interval between treatments will vary depending on the location. On areas where hair grows quickly, such as the upper lip, the treatment might be repeated in four to eight weeks. On areas of slow hair growth, such as the back, the treatment might be every 12 to 16 weeks.

For each treatment you'll wear special goggles to protect your eyes from the laser beam. An assistant might shave the site again if necessary. The doctor might apply a topical anesthetic to your skin to reduce any discomfort during treatment.

During the procedure

The doctor will press a hand-held laser instrument to your skin. Depending on the type of laser, a cooling device on the tip of the instrument or a cool gel might be used to protect your skin and lessen the risk of side effects.

When the doctor activates the laser, the laser beam will pass through your skin to the hair follicles. The intense heat from the laser beam damages the hair follicles, which inhibits hair growth. You might feel discomfort, such as a warm pinprick, and you'll likely feel a sensation of cold from the cooling device or gel.

Treating a small area, such as the upper lip, might take only a few minutes. Treating a larger area, such as the back, might take more than an hour.

After the procedure

You might notice redness and swelling for the first few hours after laser hair removal.

To reduce any discomfort, apply ice to the treated area. If you have a skin reaction immediately after laser hair removal, the doctor might apply a steroid cream to the affected area.

After laser hair removal and between scheduled treatments, avoid sunlight and don't use a tanning bed for six weeks or as directed by your doctor. Use a broad-spectrum SPF30 sunscreen daily.

Results

Hairs do not fall out immediately, but you will shed them over a period of days to weeks. This may look like continued hair growth. The repeated treatments are usually necessary because hair growth and loss naturally occur in a cycle, and laser treatment works best with hair follicles in the new-growth stage.

Results vary significantly and are difficult to predict. Most people experience hair removal that lasts several months, and it might last for years. But laser hair removal doesn't guarantee permanent hair removal. When hair regrows, it's usually finer and lighter in color.

You might need maintenance laser treatments for long-term hair reduction.

FAQ
Q1 Can white hair be laser removed?
A1 Many light- and heat-based therapies have been tried for white hair removal such as use of radiofrequency,[1] laser after colouring[2] or use of melanin-encapsulated liposomes[3] before laser therapy. However, none of them have proven to be an effective therapy with results varying 17%–54%.

Q2 Is there permanent hair removal for white hair?
A2 Electrolysis is your one-way solution for a more permanent and efficient method to remove grey / white hair. It is the only FDA approved hair removal method, medically recognized to be PERMANENT. Different from laser, it uses a tiny probe that kills the cells from inside the hair follicle by emitting heat.

Q3 What happens when you remove white hair?
A3 “Plucking a gray hair will only get you a new gray hair in its place because there is only one hair that is able to grow per follicle. ... Plucking can traumatize the hair follicle, and repeated trauma to any follicle can cause infection, scar formation or possibly lead to bald patches.

Knowing how is sex change surgery done is a key step in the gender affirmation path. This guide shows the process with care. At Bliniq Cosmetic Surgery Centre, we support you at every stage. We give expert medical care to help you match your body with who you are. Our goal is to make this path smooth and easy for you.

Understanding Gender Affirmation Surgery

Gender affirmation surgery helps people match their body with their gender identity. It is a set of surgeries that can bring great relief and joy. This surgery is a big part of many people's transitions.

Defining Gender Dysphoria

Gender dysphoria is the pain a person feels. It happens when their gender identity does not match their sex at birth. This feeling can be very strong. Surgery can be a key medical step to ease this pain. It is a known treatment for this medical state.

Your Journey with Bliniq Gender Surgery

Your path to feeling like yourself is a personal one. We are here to guide you with skill and kindness.

Our Commitment to Compassionate Care

Dr. Ashwani Kumar started Bliniq Cosmetic Surgery Centre. Our mission is simple. We give top-quality surgery at prices you can pay for. We put our patients first. That is why we offer follow-ups as often as you need. We also give free pick-up and drop-off services for your ease.

How is Sex Change Surgery Done for Male-to-Female (MtF)

The male-to-female surgery path has several possible surgeries. These surgeries help create female looks. Each plan is made for the person's goals.

MtF Bottom Surgery: Vaginoplasty Explained

A key surgery is vaginoplasty. This surgery makes a vagina, clitoris, and labia. One usual way is the penile inversion method. The goal is to make a vagina that works and looks natural. This helps patients feel whole and in line with who they are.

Additional MtF Feminizing Procedures

Beyond bottom surgery, other surgeries and procedures like Laser Hair Removal can help create a more female look. These surgeries focus on facial looks and body shape.

Facial Feminization and Breast Implants

Facial feminization surgery can soften facial looks. This may include nose reshaping, jaw shaping, or making the Adam's apple smaller. Breast implants are used to create a female chest. Bliniq offers these services for a full change.

How is Sex Change Surgery Done for Female-to-Male (FtM)

The female-to-male surgery process also has certain surgeries. These surgeries aim to create male looks. The path is special for each person.

FtM Top Surgery: Chest Masculinization

FtM top surgery is a very usual surgery, similar in technique to Gynecomastia Treatment in Delhi. It means removing breast tissue to create a flat, male chest. Usual ways include the double incision or periareolar methods. The goal is a chest that looks and feels like a man's.

Exploring FtM Bottom Surgery Options

For FtM people, bottom surgery offers ways to build male sex organs. There are two main choices to think about.

Understanding Phalloplasty and Metoidioplasty

Phalloplasty is making a penis using skin grafts from other body parts. Metoidioplasty is a surgery that makes the clitoris bigger to act as a small penis. Each has different results for size and feeling. We help you choose the best path.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the first step in the sex change surgery process?

How long is the recovery period for gender affirmation surgery?

Are the results of gender confirmation surgery permanent?

Can a transwoman get pregnant after surgery?
