Is Laser Hair Removal a Permanent Solution?

Many people want to know if the results of laser hair removal are permanent. While it offers a significant and long-lasting reduction in hair, it's best described as a semi-permanent solution. Understanding the hair growth process helps explain why.

Understanding Hair Growth Cycles

Hair grows in different stages. The laser is only effective during the active growth phase, called the anagen phase. Since not all hairs are in the same phase at the same time, multiple treatments are needed.

  1. Anagen (Active Phase): The hair is connected to the follicle, making the laser effective.
  2. Catagen (Transitional Phase): The hair detaches from the follicle.
  3. Telogen (Resting Phase): The old hair falls out, and a new one begins to grow.

Number of Sessions for Optimal Results

For the best outcome, most people need between 6 and 8 sessions. These appointments are usually spaced 4 to 6 weeks apart. This timing helps target new hairs as they enter the growth phase. After the initial set of treatments, some people may need a maintenance session once or twice a year to keep their skin smooth.

post_title: How to Remove Eye Bags Permanently
post_content:
Under-eye bags can make you look tired, even when you feel rested. Many people wonder how to remove eye bags permanently for a fresher look. Some fixes are short-term. Others give long-term results. At Bliniq Cosmetic Surgery Centre, we guide you through your options. We help you find the best way to look younger. This guide explains the causes and treatments.

Understanding the Root Causes of Under-Eye Bags

Knowing why eye bags form is the first step. The cause is often a mix of genetics and lifestyle. Understanding this helps you choose the right treatment.

The Role of Aging and Genetics

The skin under your eyes is very thin. As you age, your body makes less collagen. This protein keeps skin firm. Without it, skin and muscles weaken. The fat pads that support the eyes can then shift forward. This creates a bulge, or what we see as an eye bag. Genetics also play a big part. If your parents have eye bags, you might get them too.

Lifestyle Factors That Contribute to Puffiness

Your daily habits can make eye bags worse. A diet high in salt causes your body to hold water. This adds to puffiness. Lack of sleep can also cause fluid to gather under the eyes. Allergies trigger swelling and redness in this area. Smoking is another cause, as it breaks down collagen faster, which makes the skin thinner.

At-Home Remedies and Temporary Solutions

Many people first look for fixes they can try at home. These methods can help reduce short-term puffiness. But they do not offer a permanent fix for deep-set eye bags.

How to Remove Eye Bags Permanently at Home: Tips

For mild puffiness that happens now and then, some at-home steps can help. Here are a few simple tips for short-term relief:

  1. Use a Cold Compress: Put something cold on your eyes for a few minutes. This can reduce swelling. You can use chilled spoons, a cool washcloth, or cucumber slices.
  2. Elevate Your Head: Sleep with an extra pillow. This helps stop fluid from gathering under your eyes overnight.
  3. Adjust Your Diet: Eating less salty food can reduce fluid buildup and morning puffiness.

These tips help manage puffiness. But they are not a solution for how to remove eye bags permanently at home. This is true if fat or sagging skin is the cause.

The Truth About Topical Creams and Serums

Many eye creams claim to fix under-eye bags. Things like caffeine can tighten skin for a short time by shrinking blood vessels. Retinol may help boost collagen over time. These products can make skin texture better. But they cannot remove the fat pads that cause real eye bags. They are best for small changes.

Advanced Non-Surgical Treatments at Bliniq

For those who want more visible results without surgery, Bliniq offers advanced Medspa treatments. These options can smooth the under-eye area and improve its look with little downtime.

Dermal Fillers for Tear Trough Hollowness

Dermal fillers are a great non-surgical option. They are often made with hyaluronic acid. A skilled expert injects the filler into the hollow area under the eye. This area is called the tear trough. This smooths the line from the eye to the cheek. It makes bags look much smaller. Bliniq's Medspa offers this quick treatment. You get fast results that can last 6-12 months.

Laser Resurfacing and Chemical Peels

Laser resurfacing and chemical peels improve skin quality. Lasers remove the top layer of skin and heat the layers below. This prompts your body to create new collagen. The result is tighter, smoother skin. Chemical peels work in a similar way by removing dead skin. At Bliniq, these treatments, along with others like Mesotherapy, help reduce fine lines and improve texture around the eyes.

Comparing Under-Eye Bag Treatment Approaches

Choosing the right treatment depends on your goals and budget. It also depends on the cause of your eye bags. This table gives a clear look at the options.

Surgical Solutions for Lasting Results

For those asking how to remove eye bags permanently, surgery is the best answer. It fixes the main cause of eye bags for a long-term fix.

What Is Lower Blepharoplasty?

Lower blepharoplasty is the surgery to remove eye bags. A surgeon makes a small cut inside the eyelid or just below the lashes. Through this opening, the surgeon removes or moves the extra fat. They may also trim any loose skin or muscle. This is the only way to get a permanent fix for deep-set eye bags.

Choosing the Right Surgeon at Bliniq

The success of your surgery depends on the surgeon's skill. You need a board-certified surgeon with experience in procedures ranging from blepharoplasty to Gynecomastia Treatment in Delhi. Dr. Ashwani Kumar started Bliniq Cosmetic Surgery Centre. He wanted to give great care at good prices. We believe in high standards and happy patients. A Comprehensive Approach to Eye Rejuvenation

Often, eye bags are not the only concern. Dark circles can also make you look tired. A full approach fixes both issues for the best results.

How to Remove Eye Bags and Dark Circles Permanently

It's key to know the difference between eye bags and dark circles. Eye bags are about puffiness and swelling. Dark circles are about color. They can be caused by shadows from hollows, thin skin showing blood vessels, or dark spots. Surgery for eye bags can sometimes reduce shadows that look like dark circles. For other causes of dark circles, Bliniq offers special treatments. Fillers can fix hollowness, and lasers can improve skin tone. Understanding the Root Causes of Under-Eye Bags

Knowing why eye bags form is the first step. The cause is often a mix of genetics and lifestyle. Understanding this helps you choose the right treatment.

The Role of Aging and Genetics

The skin under your eyes is very thin. As you age, your body makes less collagen. This protein keeps skin firm. Without it, skin and muscles weaken. The fat pads that support the eyes can then shift forward. This creates a bulge, or what we see as an eye bag. Genetics also play a big part. If your parents have eye bags, you might get them too.

Lifestyle Factors That Contribute to Puffiness

Your daily habits can make eye bags worse. A diet high in salt causes your body to hold water. This adds to puffiness. Lack of sleep can also cause fluid to gather under the eyes. Allergies trigger swelling and redness in this area. Smoking is another cause, as it breaks down collagen faster, which makes the skin thinner.

At-Home Remedies and Temporary Solutions

Many people first look for fixes they can try at home. These methods can help reduce short-term puffiness. But they do not offer a permanent fix for deep-set eye bags.

How to Remove Eye Bags Permanently at Home: Tips

For mild puffiness that happens now and then, some at-home steps can help. Here are a few simple tips for short-term relief:

  1. Use a Cold Compress: Put something cold on your eyes for a few minutes. This can reduce swelling. You can use chilled spoons, a cool washcloth, or cucumber slices.
  2. Elevate Your Head: Sleep with an extra pillow. This helps stop fluid from gathering under your eyes overnight.
  3. Adjust Your Diet: Eating less salty food can reduce fluid buildup and morning puffiness.

These tips help manage puffiness. But they are not a solution for how to remove eye bags permanently at home. This is true if fat or sagging skin is the cause.

The Truth About Topical Creams and Serums

Many eye creams claim to fix under-eye bags. Things like caffeine can tighten skin for a short time by shrinking blood vessels. Retinol may help boost collagen over time. These products can make skin texture better. But they cannot remove the fat pads that cause real eye bags. They are best for small changes.

Laser Resurfacing and Chemical Peels

Laser resurfacing and chemical peels improve skin quality. Lasers remove the top layer of skin and heat the layers below. This prompts your body to create new collagen. Many people want to know how to remove skin tags in one night. These small skin growths can be annoying. While quick home remedies seem tempting, they are often unsafe and do not work. Understanding Skin Tags: What Are They?

Before trying to remove a skin tag, it is important to know what it is. This helps you make safe choices for your skin.

Defining Acrochordons (Skin Tags)

Skin tags are soft, noncancerous growths. They hang from the skin by a small stalk. They are often the same color as your skin or a bit darker. These growths are made of loose collagen fibers. They also have blood vessels inside. Skin tags are very common and almost always harmless.

Common Causes and Risk Factors

The main cause of skin tags is friction. This happens when skin rubs against skin or clothing. They often appear in certain areas.

  • Neck
  • Armpits
  • Groin area
  • Under the breasts
  • Eyelids

Some factors increase your risk. These include age, family history, and obesity. Health issues like diabetes can also play a role. Knowing the causes helps you manage them better.

Popular At-Home Removal Myths vs. Reality

The internet is full of DIY ideas for skin tag removal. Most of these methods are not safe or effective. Let's look at the facts.

The Truth About Toothpaste and Apple Cider Vinegar

You may see claims about using toothpaste. There is no science to support this. Toothpaste can irritate your skin or cause chemical burns. Apple cider vinegar is another popular myth. Its acid can also burn the skin and may even create ulcers. Dermatologists do not recommend these methods. They are not a real solution for how to remove skin tags in one night.

Over-the-Counter Freezing Kits and Creams

You can buy freezing kits at the store. They use a type of cryotherapy to freeze the tag. These kits are not as strong as professional treatments. You could easily damage the healthy skin around the tag. Removal creams and patches can also cause problems. They may lead to skin irritation and rashes. Results can take weeks and are not guaranteed. OTC products are risky and do not offer a real how to remove skin tag in one night solution.

post_title: How to Remove White Hair Permanently
post_content:
Many people ask how to remove white hair permanently. White hair is a normal part of aging. But it can be a worry for some. Knowing the cause is the first step to a fix. This guide looks at proven medical treatments. We also explain the limits of home remedies. We will show you the real options here today.

Understanding Why Hair Turns White

It is key to know why hair loses its color. This helps you choose the right way to handle or remove it.

The Role of Melanin in Hair Color

Your hair gets its color from a pigment called melanin. Special cells called melanocytes make this pigment in your hair follicles. There are two main types of melanin. Eumelanin makes black and brown shades. Pheomelanin causes red and yellow tones. As we age, melanocytes work less. They make less melanin. This causes hair to grow without color, so it looks white.

Common Causes of Premature Graying

Many things can cause hair to turn white early. Knowing these can help you handle the process.

  1. Genetics: Your family history is the biggest sign of when you will start to see gray hair.
  2. Stress: Long-term stress can harm your stem cells, which leads to graying sooner.
  3. Nutritional Deficiencies: A lack of key vitamins like B12 and iron can affect how you make melanin.
  4. Medical Conditions: Certain health issues, like thyroid problems, can lead to early white hair.

Exploring Natural Hair Care Methods

Many people look for natural fixes first. You need to know what these methods can really do.

Can You Remove White Hair Naturally?

People often ask if they can learn how to remove white hair naturally permanently. The short answer is no. Once a hair follicle stops making melanin, natural fixes cannot turn it back. At-home methods focus more on stopping it or on short-term coloring. They do not offer a lasting fix. You should know that learning how to remove white hair permanently naturally at home is not possible with the fixes we have today.

Popular At-Home Remedies and Their Limits

People often search online for how to remove white hair permanently naturally at home. You might find ideas like using amla, black tea, or onion juice. These items can act as mild natural dyes or conditioners. They may darken white hair for a short time. This makes it less easy to see. But these effects are not lasting. They do not stop the follicle from growing a white hair. These fixes are often talked about in guides on how to remove white hair naturally permanently. But they do not have lasting effects.

Professional Medical Treatments for Removal

For a lasting fix, medical treatments are the only choice. These treatments target the hair follicle right at the source to stop growth.

Electrolysis: A Permanent Hair Removal Solution

Electrolysis is the only method the FDA approves for lasting hair removal. It works well on any hair color, like white, gray, and blonde. The treatment means putting a fine probe into the hair follicle. The probe sends a tiny bit of electric current. This current kills the hair growth cells. Electrolysis is the best choice for those who want to know how to remove white hair permanently.

Understanding Laser Hair Removal for White Hair

Laser hair removal is a popular treatment. It uses strong light beams to target the melanin in hair. The pigment soaks up the light. This heats up and kills the hair follicle. White hair has no melanin, so the laser has nothing to target. This makes laser hair removal not work on white hair. This is a key difference to know.

You cannot stop your genes. But a healthy life can help your hair's health. These steps may help delay new white hairs from showing up.

Using healthy habits can make a difference. This is not a way for how to remove white hair permanently. But a healthy life helps your hair's health.

post_title: Is Chemical Peel Good for Skin​
post_content:
Are you wondering, is chemical peel good for skin? The answer is yes, this treatment can be a fantastic way to refresh your look. When done by a professional, a chemical peel safely removes damaged skin layers. This reveals the smoother, clearer skin underneath. At Bliniq, our experts use chemical peels to help you achieve a radiant complexion. This guide will explain how they work and what you can expect in 2026.

Understanding Chemical Peels: What Are They?

A chemical peel is a powerful skin resurfacing treatment. It uses a special solution to improve the skin's appearance. The goal is to create a controlled removal of the outer skin layers. This process helps your body generate new, healthy skin in its place. The result is often a smoother and more youthful look. Many find that a chemical peel is good for skin that shows signs of aging or damage.

How Chemical Peels Work on Skin

The process is quite simple. An expert applies a chemical solution to your skin. This solution causes the top layers to exfoliate and eventually peel off. Once the old, damaged layer is gone, new skin is revealed. This new skin is often less wrinkled, more even in color, and smoother. This renewal process is why many consider a chemical peel a great option for skin improvement.

Common Ingredients Found in Peels

Different peels use different active ingredients. Some of the most common are:

  • Alpha-Hydroxy Acids (AHAs): These are gentle acids like glycolic acid. They are great for treating fine lines and dryness.
  • Beta-Hydroxy Acids (BHAs): Salicylic acid is a popular BHA. It works well for acne-prone and oily skin because it can clean out pores.
  • Trichloroacetic Acid (TCA): This is a stronger agent used in medium-depth peels. It can address more significant issues like scars and deeper wrinkles.
  • Phenol: This is the strongest type of peel. It is used for deep peels to treat major skin concerns.

Superficial vs. Medium vs. Deep Peels

Chemical peels are categorized by how deeply they affect the skin. The right depth depends on your skin goals. A professional at Bliniq can help you choose the best one for you.

Peel Type

Skin Layer Affected

Purpose

Downtime

Superficial (Light)

Epidermis (Outer layer)

Mild discoloration, roughness, acne

Minimal (1-3 days)

Medium

Epidermis and upper dermis

Fine lines, acne scars, uneven tone

About one week

Deep

Epidermis and deeper dermis

Deep wrinkles, significant sun damage, scars

2-3 weeks or more

The Main Types of Chemical Peels Explained

Understanding the different types of peels can help you set clear expectations. Each level targets specific skin concerns and comes with a different recovery timeline.

Light Peels for Minor Imperfections

Light peels, or superficial peels, are the gentlest option. They work on the outermost layer of skin to fix small issues. These include fine wrinkles, uneven tone, acne, and dryness. Because they are mild, you can get them regularly with very little downtime. This makes them a popular choice within Bliniq's Medspa Services for a quick skin refresh.

Medium Peels for Moderate Skin Issues

Medium peels go a bit deeper to address more noticeable problems. They are effective for more prominent wrinkles, mild acne scars, and uneven skin color. The recovery time is longer than a light peel, usually taking about a week. This treatment offers more significant results for those who need it.

Deep Peels for Significant Concerns

Deep peels are for major skin concerns like deep wrinkles, scars, or even precancerous growths. This is a one-time procedure that delivers dramatic results but requires a longer healing period. It is very important to have this type of peel done by a certified professional, like the experts at Bliniq, to ensure safety and the best outcome.

Key Benefits of a Professional Chemical Peel

So, why do people ask, is chemical peel good for skin? The answer lies in its many benefits. A professional peel at a center like Bliniq can truly transform your skin's health and appearance.

Improving Skin Texture and Tone

By removing the dull, dead outer layer of skin, peels reveal a smoother and more radiant complexion. This exfoliation process helps create a more even skin tone, reducing patchiness and giving you a healthy glow.

Reducing Fine Lines and Wrinkles

Peels can also help with signs of aging. Medium to deep peels stimulate the body's production of collagen. Collagen is a protein that keeps skin firm and elastic, helping to reduce the appearance of fine lines.

Minimizing Acne and Scarring

For those with acne, chemical peels can be very helpful. They unclog pores, which reduces breakouts. They can also improve the look of mild acne scars, leading to clearer skin over time.

Fading Hyperpigmentation and Sun Damage

Peels are excellent at correcting pigment issues. They can lighten dark spots, sun spots, and other forms of hyperpigmentation. This results in a more uniform and even skin color.

Are Chemical Peels Right for Your Skin Type?

A common question is whether a chemical peel is good for skin of all types and tones. While peels are versatile, some skin types need special care. A consultation at Bliniq is the best way to determine if a peel is right for you.

Is chemical peel good for dry skin?

Yes, a peel can be helpful for dry skin. Certain types of peels, especially those with lactic acid, can actually hydrate the skin. However, choosing the right formula is key. A professional evaluation at Bliniq will ensure you receive a peel that nourishes your skin instead of causing more dryness.

Is chemical peel good for dark skin?

This is an important question. Darker skin tones have a higher risk of developing dark spots after certain peels. This is called post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation. However, superficial peels with gentler acids like mandelic or salicylic acid are generally safe and effective options for darker skin. Consulting an expert is crucial.

Who Should Avoid Chemical Peels?

Peels are not for everyone. You should avoid them if you have active skin infections or certain conditions like eczema. People who have recently used acne medications like isotretinoin should also wait. It is also not recommended for those who are pregnant or breastfeeding.

The Chemical Peel Process at Bliniq

Knowing what to expect can make the process feel more comfortable. At Bliniq, we guide you through every step, from your first meeting to your final result.

Your Initial Consultation at Bliniq

  1. An expert will assess your skin type and discuss your concerns.
  2. They will review your medical history to ensure a peel is safe for you.
  3. Based on this, we create a custom treatment plan from our range of Medspa Services.

What to Expect During Your Treatment

The treatment itself is straightforward. First, your skin will be thoroughly cleansed. Next, the chemical solution is carefully applied. You might feel a stinging or warming feeling, which is normal. Finally, the solution is neutralized and removed.

Post-Peel Care with Bliniq's Experts

Aftercare is vital for great results. Our team provides clear instructions to help your skin heal perfectly. Following these steps ensures your skin recovers well and you see the best possible outcome. Proper care also helps maintain the results for longer.

Potential Risks and Important Aftercare Tips

The Hair Transplant Procedure Explained

A hair transplant is a very detailed procedure. It needs skill and an artistic eye. At Bliniq, our experienced team, led by Dr. Ashwani Kumar, handles every step with great care to give you the best, most natural-looking results.

The Donor and Recipient Areas

The process starts with two key areas. The 'donor area' is usually the back and sides of your head. Hair here is strong and genetically resistant to balding. The 'recipient area' is where your scalp is thinning or bald. This is where the new hair will be placed. The health of your donor area is vital for a successful transplant.

How Grafts Are Prepared and Placed

Our surgeons extract tiny groups of hairs, called follicular units or grafts. Each graft can have one to four hairs. The team at Bliniq carefully prepares these grafts under powerful microscopes. Then, they are placed into the recipient area. We match the angle and direction of your natural hair growth. This careful work ensures the final result looks completely natural.

Types of Hair Transplant Techniques

There are two main methods for hair transplantation. Both are effective, and the best one for you depends on your specific needs. Dr. Ashwani Kumar will help you decide during your consultation.

What is FUE Hair Transplant?

So, what is FUE hair transplant? FUE stands for Follicular Unit Extraction. In this method, the surgeon removes individual hair follicles one by one from the donor area. The benefits are clear, there is no long scar, just tiny dots that heal quickly. Recovery is faster too. Bliniq offers advanced FUE techniques for excellent, natural-looking outcomes.

Follicular Unit Transplantation (FUT)

FUT is also known as the 'strip method'. A small strip of skin is taken from the donor area. From this strip, our team harvests many grafts. FUT is great for people who need a large number of grafts in one session. This method does leave a thin, linear scar, but it is easily hidden by the surrounding hair.

The Science Behind Mesotherapy

Hair loss can be stressful for anyone. Many people look for a safe and good answer. This guide explains what is prp therapy for hair. It is a new treatment that uses your body's own healing power. At Bliniq Cosmetic Surgery Centre, we offer new hair fall treatments. We want to help you know your choices for healthier, fuller hair. This post will tell you all you need to know.

Understanding Hair Loss and Modern Solutions

Hair loss affects both men and women. Common causes are genes, stress, and hormone changes. In the past, choices were few. Today, healing medicine offers new hope. These treatments work to fix and wake up your body's cells. Bliniq Cosmetic Surgery Centre offers new answers like PRP therapy. We focus on the latest treatments to help you reach your goals.

What is PRP Therapy for Hair Loss?

You may be asking, what is prp therapy for hair loss? It is a treatment that uses your own blood to help hair grow. The goal is to wake up weak or sleeping hair follicles. This leads to thicker, healthier hair.

The Science of Platelet-Rich Plasma

PRP stands for Platelet-Rich Plasma. Your blood has two main parts: plasma and platelets. Plasma is the liquid part. Platelets are small cells that help your body heal. They have strong growth factors. To get PRP, we draw a small amount of your blood. We spin this blood in a special machine called a centrifuge. This process separates the platelets. It makes a strong plasma that is full of growth factors.

How PRP Stimulates Hair Follicles

We inject the prepared PRP into your scalp. These shots target areas where hair is thinning. The growth factors in the PRP start to work right away. They can help wake up sleeping hair follicles. They also help keep your hair in its growth phase longer. The treatment helps blood flow to the follicles. This leads to stronger and thicker hair over time.

The PRP Treatment Process at Bliniq Centre

At Bliniq, your comfort and safety are our main goals. The PRP therapy process is simple and easy. Our team of skilled experts, led by Dr. Ashwani Kumar, performs the treatment.

Step 1: Consultation and Scalp Analysis

  1. Your journey starts with a private meeting.
  2. Our experts will check your scalp.
  3. We check your type of hair loss to see if PRP is right for you.

Step 2: Blood Draw and Preparation

  1. We draw a small amount of blood from your arm.
  2. We then put the blood into a centrifuge.
  3. This machine spins the blood for about 10-15 minutes to gather the platelets.

Step 3: PRP Injection into the Scalp

  1. We clean your scalp. We may use a numbing cream for your comfort.
  2. We then inject the prepared PRP into the areas where hair is thin.
  3. The whole process usually takes about 60 to 90 minutes.

Who is an Ideal Candidate for PRP?

Knowing if you are a good fit for what is prp therapy for hair is a key step. The treatment works best for some types of hair loss.

Identifying Early-Stage Hair Loss

PRP works best for people with early to mild hair thinning. This includes common pattern baldness, also known as androgenetic alopecia. It works by waking up hair follicles that are still alive but weak. It usually does not work for areas with full baldness. This is where follicles are no longer alive.

Contraindications for PRP Therapy

Some health issues may mean you cannot have PRP therapy. These can include blood problems, active scalp infections, or some skin issues. During your meeting at Bliniq, we do a full check of your health history. This makes sure the treatment is safe for you.

Key Benefits of PRP Hair Treatment

PRP therapy offers many benefits for those who want a natural way to restore hair.

  • Natural and Safe: The treatment uses your body's own cells. This greatly lowers the risk of bad reactions.
  • Non-Surgical: It is a simple process with no cuts or stitches needed.
  • Minimal Downtime: Most people can go back to their normal daily tasks right after the session.
  • Improves Hair Health: It can lead to thicker, fuller, and stronger hair.

Potential Side Effects and Disadvantages

Like any medical treatment, it is key to know about possible side effects. The good news is that side effects from PRP are usually mild.

Common and Mild Side Effects

Some people may feel short-term side effects after treatment. These can include mild pain, swelling, redness, or itching where you got the shots. These signs are usually small. They go away in a few days.

Understanding the Limitations

It's important to have real hopes. Results from PRP therapy are not instant. The treatment needs many sessions. How well it works can change from person to person. Things like your genes play a role. To keep the results, you will need more treatments over time.

What to Expect: Results and Timeline

You need to be patient to see results from what is prp therapy for hair. Here is a common timeline.

Timeline

What to Expect

After 1-2 Sessions

You may notice less hair falling out.

After 3-6 Months

You can often see thicker and fuller hair.

Ongoing

Upkeep sessions help keep and improve results.

Typical Treatment Schedule

A normal treatment plan starts with a first set of sessions. Most people need 3-4 treatments. These are spaced 4-6 weeks apart. After the first phase, we create an upkeep plan. This plan includes more sessions every 4-6 months to keep your hair healthy.

When Will You See Results?

You can see the first changes in a few months, like less hair fall. More major growth and thickness are easy to see after about three to six months. Results get better over time with regular treatment.

In India, the average cost per session can be from ₹3,500 to ₹8,000. Clinics often offer deals for the first set of treatments. It's key to know that PRP is a cosmetic treatment. This means insurance usually does not cover it. For a personal price, we invite you to book a meeting at Bliniq.

For even better results, you can mix PRP with other treatments. At Bliniq, we offer a full set of 'Hair Fall Therapies' to meet your own needs.

Primary Benefit

Improves healing and growth of new follicles.

Gives two-way help by healing and feeding the hair.

PRP with Hair Transplants

PRP is often used after a hair transplant surgery. It helps speed up the healing process. It also helps the newly moved hair follicles to grow strong and healthy. This full approach can lead to better results.

PRP and Mesotherapy Synergy

Mesotherapy is another treatment we offer at Bliniq. It means putting a mix of vitamins and nutrients into the scalp. When mixed with PRP, they work very well together. PRP works to fix follicles. Mesotherapy gives key food for hair growth.

Choosing the right clinic is key for your treatment. Bliniq Cosmetic Surgery Centre was started by Dr. Ashwani Kumar. We are focused on great work. We offer new and modern cosmetic treatments. Our skilled team uses the newest tools to make sure you get the best care. We offer 'Hair Fall Therapies' and other cosmetic services. We are your full answer for beauty needs. It is not a forever cure. You need upkeep treatments to keep the results long-term.

People with blood problems, active scalp infections, or full baldness may not be good fits.

What the Procedure Actually Involves

A tummy tuck is a surgery to take out extra skin and fat from the stomach. A key part of the surgery is fixing and tightening weak or split stomach muscles. This issue, known as diastasis recti, is common after pregnancy. The main goal is to make the stomach shape smoother and firmer. There are different kinds of tummy tucks.

  • A full tummy tuck uses a cut from hip to hip. It also uses another cut around the navel to work on the whole stomach.
  • A mini tummy tuck uses a smaller cut. It only works on the area below the belly button.

Tummy Tuck vs. Liposuction: Key Differences

It's good to know the difference between these two common surgeries. Both can give you a slimmer look. But they do different things. Liposuction is great for taking out tough spots of fat if your skin stretches well. A tummy tuck is better for people with loose skin and weak muscles. Dr. Ashwani Kumar is the founder of Bliniq. Steps Involved in the Surgical Process

Our skilled health experts do the surgery with great care. Here are the main steps:

  1. Anesthesia: You will get general anesthesia. This makes sure you are comfortable and feel no pain during the surgery.
  2. The Incision: The surgeon makes a side-to-side cut between your pubic hairline and belly button. The length of the cut depends on how much extra skin you have.
  3. Muscle Repair: The surgeon tightens the deep, weak stomach muscles with stitches.
  4. Skin Removal: The surgeon pulls down the extra skin. They trim it and stitch the rest together.
  5. Navel Repositioning: The surgeon makes a new opening for the belly button. This gives it a natural look.

The surgeon may place drains for a short time. This helps remove any extra fluid after the surgery.

Most people can go back to a desk job in one to two weeks. You will need to wear a special support wrap. This wrap helps the healing areas and cuts down on swelling.

Long-Term Results and Maintaining Your New Contour

It can take a few months for all the swelling to go down. Then you will see the final results. To keep your new, flat stomach, a healthy diet and regular exercise are key. The scar from the surgery is lasting. But it will fade a lot over time. This usually takes about a year.

Exploring Costs and What is Tummy Tuck Surgery Alternatives

We believe in making cosmetic surgery easy to get. At Bliniq, we give clear details on costs and options. This helps you make the best choice for your body and budget.

What is the cost of tummy tuck surgery?

Many things affect what is the cost of tummy tuck surgery. These include how hard the surgery is and the surgeon's fees. At Bliniq, we are focused on low cost. We offer easy EMI options to help you pay for it. We will clearly explain your total fee in your meeting.

Part of the Cost

What it is

Facility Costs

Includes the surgery room and care after surgery.

What is tummy tuck without surgery?

Some people ask, 'what is tummy tuck without surgery?' This term means non-surgical treatments that try to cut fat and tighten skin. Common choices include CoolSculpting (fat freezing), EmSculpt (muscle toning), and radiofrequency treatments. Bliniq's Medspa offers these and other non-surgical treatments, such as Laser Hair Removal. We can talk about if one is right for you in a meeting.

Why Choose Bliniq for Your Transformation?

Choosing where to have your surgery is a big choice. Dr. Ashwani Kumar started Bliniq Cosmetic Surgery Centre with a clear goal. He wanted to offer top-quality cosmetic surgery at prices people can afford. Our patient-first way makes us a top choice.

Our Promise to You

  • Expert Care: Our team of health experts is led by the very skilled Dr. Ashwani Kumar. They are skilled and focused on your safety.
  • Full Services: We are a multi-speciality centre. We offer many types of surgeries, including Sex change surgery, under one roof.
  • Patient-First Way: We offer perks like unlimited follow-ups. We also have free pick-up and drop-off and easy EMI options. This makes your time with us stress-free.

We invite you to set up a meeting at Bliniq Cosmetic Surgery Centre. Let's talk about your goals. We can help you get the confidence you want.

Frequently Asked Questions

What exactly does a tummy tuck do?

It takes out extra fat and skin from the stomach. It also tightens the deep muscles. This gives you a flatter, firmer look.

How painful is a tummy tuck?

You will feel some pain and soreness after the surgery. This is managed well with pain medicine we give you.

How long will a tummy tuck last?

The results are seen as lasting. But keeping a steady weight and a healthy lifestyle is key for long-term results.

What are the side effects of a tummy tuck?

You may have swelling, bruising, soreness, and numbness. You will also have a scar that will fade over time.

Can I get 'six-pack abs' with a tummy tuck at Bliniq?

A tummy tuck makes a flat and toned base. Our special ‘Tummy Tuck & Six-Pack Abs’ service can improve muscle shape even more.

Are there financing options available for the surgery at Bliniq?

Yes, we offer easy EMI options. This makes sure our great surgeries are easy to get and pay for.

Pathology Clinic

Pathology is the study of disease. It is the bridge between science and medicine. It underpins every aspect of patient care, from diagnostic testing and treatment advice to using cutting-edge genetic technologies and preventing disease.

Doctors and scientists working in pathology are experts in illness and disease. They use their expertise to support every aspect of healthcare, from guiding doctors on the right way to treat common diseases, to using cutting-edge genetic technologies to treat patients with life.

These teams are made up of pathologists – who are either doctors with specialist laboratory training or scientists with specialist clinical training – as well as biomedical scientists and support staff. Doctors, nurses, surgeons and other medical staff look to pathologists and consultant clinical scientists for advice on the nature and seriousness of a patient’s illness, making sure they get the most appropriate treatment. We will work with you to develop individualised care plans, including management of chronic diseases.

If we cannot assist, we can provide referrals or advice about the type of practitioner you require. We are committed to being the region’s premier healthcare network by providing patient-centered care that inspires clinical and service excellence, making us the first and best choice for our patients, employees, physicians, employers, volunteers and communities.

  • If your blood doesn’t clot properly – it’s a haematologist who will conduct the blood tests, confirm if you have haemophilia, and offer treatment.
  • When there’s an outbreak of infection in a hospital, it’s a medical microbiologist or infection doctor who will advise the infection control teams and work hard to contain it.
  • For those having trouble getting pregnant – it’s a reproductive scientist who will investigate, diagnose and, where possible, treat any infertility issues.
